Rotherham.label: The Emphasised Bible
Rotherham.overlayPolicy: mismatch chapters stored under translationOverlays.Rotherham without altering target verse sequence
Rotherham.emptySourceRowsPolicy: empty Rotherham source rows are governed omissions and are not imported as blank text

text.NRSV: The beginning of the good news of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
text.Rotherham: Beginning of the glad-message of Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 1:1 The beginning of the Gospel of Iesus Christ, the Sonne of God,
text.DRV: 1:1 The beginning of the Gospel of Jesus Christ the Son of God.

text.NRSV: As it is written in the prophet Isaiah, “See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way;
text.Rotherham: According as it is written in Isaiah the prophet—Lo! I send my messenger before thy face, Who shall prepare thy way,—
text.KJV: 1:2 As it is written in the Prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
text.DRV: 1:2 As it is written in Esay the Prophet : ( Behold I send mine Angel before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee.)

text.NRSV: the voice of one crying out in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight,’”
text.Rotherham: A voice of one crying aloud—In the wilderness, prepare ye the way of the Lord, straight, be making his paths.
text.KJV: 1:3 The voice of one crying in the wildernesse, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
text.DRV: 1:3 A voice of one crying in the desert. Prepare ye the way of our Lord, make straight his paths.

text.NRSV: John the baptizer appeared in the wilderness, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
text.Rotherham: John the Immerser came, in the wilderness, proclaiming an immersion of repentance for remission of sins;
text.KJV: 1:4 Iohn did baptize in the wildernesse, and preach the baptisme of repentance, for the remission of sinnes.
text.DRV: 1:4 John was in the desert baptizing, and preaching the Baptism of penance unto remission of sins.

text.NRSV: And people from the whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem were going out to him, and were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
text.Rotherham: and there were going out unto him all the Judaea country and all they of Jerusalem, and were being immersed by him in the Jordan river, openly confessing their sins;
text.KJV: 1:5 And there went out vnto him all the land of Iudea, and they of Ierusalem, and were all baptized of him in the riuer of Iordane, cōfessing their sinnes.
text.DRV: 1:5 And there went forth to him all the country of Jewry, and all they of Jerusalem, and were baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.

text.NRSV: Now John was clothed with camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
text.Rotherham: and John was clothed with camel’s-hair and a leathern girdle about his loins, and was eating locusts and wild honey;
text.KJV: 1:6 And Iohn was clothed with camels haire, and with a girdle of a skin about his loines: and he did eat locusts and wilde honie,
text.DRV: 1:6 And John was clothed with camel's hair, and a girdle of a skin about his loins: and he did eat locusts and wild honey.

text.NRSV: He proclaimed, “The one who is more powerful than I is coming after me; I am not worthy to stoop down and untie the thong of his sandals.
text.Rotherham: and he proclaimed, saying—He that is mightier than I cometh after [me], of whom I am not worthy to stoop and unloose the strap of his sandals:
text.KJV: 1:7 And preached, saying, There commeth one mightier then I after me, the latchet of whose shooes I am not worthy to stoupe downe, and vnloose.
text.DRV: 1:7 And he preached, saying: There cometh a stronger than I after me, whose latchet of his shoes I am not worthy stooping down to loose.

text.NRSV: I have baptized you with water; but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: I, have immersed you, with water,—He, will immerse you, with the Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 1:8 I indeed haue baptized you with water: but hee shall baptize you with the holy Ghost.
text.DRV: 1:8 I have baptized you with water; but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was immersed into the Jordan by John;
text.KJV: 1:9 And it came to passe in those daies, that Iesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of Iohn in Iordane.
text.DRV: 1:9 And it came to pass, in those days came Jesus from Nazareth of Galilee; and was baptized of John in Jordan.

text.NRSV: And just as he was coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens torn apart and the Spirit descending like a dove on him.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, as he was coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens rending asunder, and, the Spirit, as a dove, descending unto him;
text.KJV: 1:10 And straightway comming vp out of the water, hee saw the heauens opened, and the Spirit like a doue descending vpon him.
text.DRV: 1:10 And forthwith coming up out of the water, he saw the Heavens opened, and the Spirit as a dove descending, and remaining on him.

text.NRSV: And a voice came from heaven, “You are my Son, the Beloved; with you I am well pleased.”
text.Rotherham: and a voice [came] out of the heavens—Thou, art my Son, the Beloved,—In thee, I delight.
text.KJV: 1:11 And there came a voice from heauen, saying, Thou art my beloued Sonne, in whom I am well pleased.
text.DRV: 1:11 And a voice was made from Heaven: Thou art my beloved Son, in thee I am well pleased.

text.NRSV: And the Spirit immediately drove him out into the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, the Spirit urgeth him forth into the wilderness;
text.KJV: 1:12 And immediately the Spirit driueth him into the wildernesse.
text.DRV: 1:12 And forthwith the Spirit drove him out into the desert.

text.NRSV: He was in the wilderness forty days, tempted by Satan; and he was with the wild beasts; and the angels waited on him.
text.Rotherham: and he was in the wilderness, forty days, tempted by Satan, and was with the wild beasts,—and, the messengers, were ministering unto him.
text.KJV: 1:13 And he was there in the wildernesse fourtie daies tempted of Satan, and was with the wildbeasts, and the Angels ministred vnto him.
text.DRV: 1:13 And he was in the desert forty days, and forty nights: and was tempted of Satan, and he was with beasts, and the Angels ministered to him.

text.NRSV: Now after John was arrested, Jesus came to Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God,
text.Rotherham: And after John was delivered up, Jesus came into Galilee, proclaiming the glad-message of God,
text.KJV: 1:14 Now after that Iohn was put in prison, Iesus came into Galilee, preaching the Gospell of the kingdome of God,
text.DRV: 1:14 And after that John was delivered up, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the Gospel of the Kingdom of God,

text.NRSV: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God has come near; repent, and believe in the good news.”
text.Rotherham: [and saying]—The season is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God hath drawn near,—Repent ye, and have faith in the glad-message.
text.KJV: 1:15 And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdome of God is at hand: repent ye, and beleeue the Gospell.
text.DRV: 1:15 and saying: That the time is fulfilled, and the Kingdom of God is at hand: be penitent, and believe the Gospel.

text.NRSV: As Jesus passed along the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the sea—for they were fishermen.
text.Rotherham: And, passing by near the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon, and Andrew the brother of Simon, casting a net in the sea,—for they were fishers;
text.KJV: 1:16 Now as he walked by the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the Sea (for they were fishers.)
text.DRV: 1:16 And passing by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother, casting nets into the sea (for they were fishers)

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me and I will make you fish for people.”
text.Rotherham: and Jesus said unto them—Come after me, and I will make you become fishers of men;
text.KJV: 1:17 And Iesus said vnto them, Come ye after me; and I will make you to become fishers of men.
text.DRV: 1:17 and Jesus said to them: Come after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.

text.NRSV: And immediately they left their nets and followed him.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, leaving the nets, they followed him.
text.KJV: 1:18 And straightway they forsooke their nets, and followed him.
text.DRV: 1:18 And immediately leaving their nets, they followed him.

text.NRSV: As he went a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John, who were in their boat mending the nets.
text.Rotherham: And, going forward a little, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, and them who were in the boat putting in order the nets;
text.KJV: 1:19 And when hee had gone a little further thence, hee saw Iames the sonne of Zebedee, and Iohn his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
text.DRV: 1:19 And being gone thence a little further, he saw James of Zebedee, and John his brother, and them repairing their nets in the ship:

text.NRSV: Immediately he called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men, and followed him.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, he called them,—and, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat, with the hired men, they came away after him.
text.KJV: 1:20 And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired seruants, and went after him.
text.DRV: 1:20 and forthwith he called them. And leaving their father Zebedee in the ship with his hired men, they followed him.

text.NRSV: They went to Capernaum; and when the sabbath came, he entered the synagogue and taught.
text.Rotherham: And they journey into Capernaum. And, straightway, on the Sabbath, entering into the synagogue, he began teaching;
text.KJV: 1:21 And they went into Capernaum, and straightway on the Sabbath day he entred into the Synagogue, and taught.
text.DRV: 1:21 And they enter into Capharnaum, and he forthwith upon the Sabboths going into the Synagogue, taught them.

text.NRSV: They were astounded at his teaching, for he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
text.Rotherham: and they were being struck with astonishment at his teaching,—for he was teaching them as one having authority, and, not as the Scribes.
text.KJV: 1:22 And they were astonished at his doctrine: for hee taught them as one that had authority, and not as the Scribes.
text.DRV: 1:22 And they were astonished at his doctrine. For he was teaching them as having power, and not as the Scribes.

text.NRSV: Just then there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit,
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, there was in their synagogue, a man in an impure spirit,—and he cried out aloud,
text.KJV: 1:23 And there was in their Synagogue a man with an vncleane spirit, and he cried out,
text.DRV: 1:23 And there was in their Synagogue a man in an unclean Spirit; and he cried out,

text.NRSV: and he cried out, “What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, the Holy One of God.”
text.Rotherham: saying—What have we in common with thee, Jesus of Nazareth? Hast thou come to destroy us? I know thee, who thou art, The Holy One of God.
text.KJV: 1:24 Saying, Let vs alone, what haue we to doe with thee, thou Iesus of Nazareth? Art thou come to destroy vs? I know thee who thou art, the holy One of God.
text.DRV: 1:24 saying: What to us and to thee Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know who thou art, the Saint of God.

text.NRSV: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus rebuked him, [saying]—Be silenced, and come forth out of him!
text.KJV: 1:25 And Iesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
text.DRV: 1:25 And Jesus threatened him, saying: Hold thy peace, and go out of the man.

text.NRSV: And the unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.
text.Rotherham: And the impure spirit, tearing him, and calling out with a loud voice, came forth out of him;
text.KJV: 1:26 And when the vncleane spirit had torne him, and cried with a lowd voice, he came out of him.
text.DRV: 1:26 And the unclean Spirit tearing him, and crying out with a great voice, went out of him.

text.NRSV: They were all amazed, and they kept on asking one another, “What is this? A new teaching—with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.”
text.Rotherham: and they were amazed, one and all, so that they began to discuss among themselves, saying—What is this? New teaching! With authority, to the impure spirits also, he giveth orders, and they obey him!
text.KJV: 1:27 And they were all amased, insomuch that they questioned among themselues, saying, What thing is this? What new doctrine is this? For with authoritie commandeth he euen the vncleane spirits, and they doe obey him.
text.DRV: 1:27 And they marveled all, in so much that they questioned among themselves, saying: What thing is this? What is this new doctrine? For with power he commandeth the unclean spirits also, and they obey him.

text.NRSV: At once his fame began to spread throughout the surrounding region of Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And forth went the report of him, straightway, on every hand, into the surrounding country of Galilee.
text.KJV: 1:28 And immediatly his fame spread abroad throughout al the region round about Galilee.
text.DRV: 1:28 And the bruit of him went forth incontinent into all the country of Galilee.

text.NRSV: As soon as they left the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, out of the synagogue, going forth, he went unto the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
text.KJV: 1:29 And forth with, when they were come out of the Synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon, and Andrew, with Iames and Iohn.
text.DRV: 1:29 And immediately going forth out of the Synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

text.NRSV: Now Simon’s mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they told him about her at once.
text.Rotherham: Now, the mother-in-law of Simon, was lying in a fever, and, straightway, they speak to him concerning her;
text.KJV: 1:30 But Simons wiues mother lay sicke of a feuer: and anone they tell him of her.
text.DRV: 1:30 And Simon's wife's mother lay in a fit of a fever: and forthwith they tell him of her.

text.NRSV: He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up. Then the fever left her, and she began to serve them.
text.Rotherham: and, coming near, he raised her up, grasping her hand,—and the fever left her, and she began ministering unto them.
text.KJV: 1:31 And he came and tooke her by the hand, and lift her vp, and immediately the feuer left her, and she ministred vnto them.
text.DRV: 1:31 And coming near he lifted her up taking her by the hand: and incontinent the ague left her, and she ministered unto them.

text.NRSV: That evening, at sunset, they brought to him all who were sick or possessed with demons.
text.Rotherham: And, evening arriving, when the sun went in, they were bearing unto him all who were sick, and them who were demonized;
text.KJV: 1:32 And at euen, when the Sunne did set, they brought vnto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with diuels:
text.DRV: 1:32 And when it was evening after sun set, they brought to him all that were ill at ease and that had devils.

text.NRSV: And the whole city was gathered around the door.
text.Rotherham: and all the city was gathered unto the door;
text.KJV: 1:33 And all the citie was gathered together at the doore.
text.DRV: 1:33 And all the city was gathered together at the door.

text.NRSV: And he cured many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and he would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew him.
text.Rotherham: and he cured many that were sick with divers diseases, and, many demons, he cast out, and suffered not the demons to be talking,—because they knew him [to be Christ].
text.KJV: 1:34 And he healed many that were sicke of diuers diseases, and cast out many deuils, and suffered not the deuils to speake, because they knew him.
text.DRV: 1:34 And he cured many that were vexed with diverse diseases; and he cast out many devils, and he suffered not them to speak that they knew him.

text.NRSV: In the morning, while it was still very dark, he got up and went out to a deserted place, and there he prayed.
text.Rotherham: And, very early by night arising, he went out [and departed] into a desert place, and, there, was praying;
text.KJV: 1:35 And in the morning, rising vp a great while before day, hee went out, and departed into a solitarie place, and there prayed.
text.DRV: 1:35 And rising very early, and going forth he went into a desert place: and there he prayed.

text.NRSV: And Simon and his companions hunted for him.
text.Rotherham: and Simon, and they who were with him, went in quest of him,
text.KJV: 1:36 And Simon, and they that were with him, followed after him:
text.DRV: 1:36 And Simon sought after him, and they that were with him.

text.NRSV: When they found him, they said to him, “Everyone is searching for you.”
text.Rotherham: and found him, and say unto him—All, are seeking thee;
text.KJV: 1:37 And when they had found him, they said vnto him, All men seek for thee.
text.DRV: 1:37 And when they had found him, they said to him, That all seek for thee.

text.NRSV: He answered, “Let us go on to the neighboring towns, so that I may proclaim the message there also; for that is what I came out to do.”
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them—Let us be going elsewhere, into the neighbouring country-towns, in order that, there also, I may be making proclamation,—for, to this end, came I forth;
text.KJV: 1:38 And he said vnto them, Let vs goe into þ next townes, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I foorth.
text.DRV: 1:38 And he saith to them: Let us go into the next towns and cities, that I may preach there also; for to this purpose am I come.

text.NRSV: And he went throughout Galilee, proclaiming the message in their synagogues and casting out demons.
text.Rotherham: and he came, making proclamation, into their synagogues, throughout the whole of Galilee,—and, was casting the demons out.
text.KJV: 1:39 And he preached in their Synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out deuils.
text.DRV: 1:39 And he was preaching in their Synagogues, and in all Galilee, and casting out devils.

text.NRSV: A leper came to him begging him, and kneeling he said to him, “If you choose, you can make me clean.”
text.Rotherham: And there cometh unto him, a leper, beseeching him and [kneeling],—saying unto him—If thou be willing, thou canst cleanse me;
text.KJV: 1:40 And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling downe to him, and saying vnto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me cleane.
text.DRV: 1:40 And a leper cometh to him beseeching him; and kneeling down saith to him: If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.

text.NRSV: Moved with pity, Jesus stretched out his hand and touched him, and said to him, “I do choose. Be made clean!”
text.Rotherham: and, moved with compassion, he stretched forth the hand, and touched him, and saith unto him—I am willing, Be cleansed!
text.KJV: 1:41 And Iesus mooued with compassion, put foorth his hand, and touched him, and saith vnto him, I will, be thou cleane.
text.DRV: 1:41 And Jesus having compassion on him, stretched forth his hand; and touching him, he saith unto him: I will. Be thou made clean.

text.NRSV: Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was made clean.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed;
text.KJV: 1:42 And assoone as he had spoken, immediately the leprosie departed from him, and he was cleansed.
text.DRV: 1:42 And when he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was made clean.

text.NRSV: After sternly warning him he sent him away at once,
text.Rotherham: and, strictly charging him, straightway, he urged him forth;
text.KJV: 1:43 And he straitly charged him, and forth with sent him away,
text.DRV: 1:43 And he threatened him, and forthwith cast him forth.

text.NRSV: saying to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
text.Rotherham: and saith unto him—Mind! unto no one, say aught,—but withdraw, thyself, show unto the priest, and offer for thy cleansing what things Moses enjoined, for a witness unto them.
text.KJV: 1:44 And saith vnto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but goe thy way, shew thy selfe to the Priest, and offer for thy clensing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony vnto them.
text.DRV: 1:44 And he saith to him: See thou tell nobody, but go, shew thyself to the high Priest, and offer for thy cleansing the things that Moyses commanded, for a testimony to them.

text.NRSV: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely, and to spread the word, so that Jesus could no longer go into a town openly, but stayed out in deserted places; and people came to him from every quarter.
text.Rotherham: But, he, going forth, began to be proclaiming many things, and blazing abroad the story, so that, no longer, was it possible for him, openly, into a city, to enter,—but, outside, in desert places, [was he], and they were coming unto him from every quarter.
text.KJV: 1:45 But he went out, and beganne to publish it much, and to blase abroad the matter: insomuch that Iesus could no more openly enter into the citie, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from euery quarter.
text.DRV: 1:45 But he being gone forth, began to publish, and to blase abroad the word: so that now he could not openly go into the city, but was abroad in desert places, and they came together unto him from all sides.

text.NRSV: When he returned to Capernaum after some days, it was reported that he was at home.
text.Rotherham: And, entering again into Capernaum, after some days, it was heard say—He is in a house;
text.KJV: 2:1 And againe hee entred into Capernaum after some dayes, and it was noysed that he was in the house.
text.DRV: 2:1 And again he entered into Capharnaum after some days, and it was heard that he was in the house,

text.NRSV: So many gathered around that there was no longer room for them, not even in front of the door; and he was speaking the word to them.
text.Rotherham: and many were gathered together, so that no longer was there room even in the approaches to the door,—and he began speaking unto them the word;
text.KJV: 2:2 And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no roome to receiue them, no not so much as about the doore: and he preached the word vnto them.
text.DRV: 2:2 and many came together, so that there was no place; no not at the door; & he spake to them the Word.

text.NRSV: Then some people came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them.
text.Rotherham: and they come, bearing unto him a paralytic, upborne by four,—
text.KJV: 2:3 And they come vnto him, bringing one sicke of the palsie, which was borne of foure.
text.DRV: 2:3 And they came to him bringing one sick of the palsy, who was carried of four.

text.NRSV: And when they could not bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, they removed the roof above him; and after having dug through it, they let down the mat on which the paralytic lay.
text.Rotherham: and, not being able to get near him by reason of the multitude, they uncovered the roof where he was, and, having broken it up, they began letting down the couch whereon the paralytic was lying;
text.KJV: 2:4 And when they could not come nigh vnto him for preasse, they vncouered the roofe where he was: and when they had broken it vp, they let downe the bed wherin the sick of the palsie lay.
text.DRV: 2:4 And when they could not offer him unto him for the multitude, they uncovered the roof where he was: and opening it they did let down the couch wherein the sick of the palsy lay.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
text.Rotherham: and, Jesus, seeing their faith, saith unto the paralytic—Child! forgiven are thy sins!
text.KJV: 2:5 When Iesus saw their faith, hee said vnto the sicke of the palsie, Sonne, thy sinnes be forgiuen thee.
text.DRV: 2:5 And when Jesus had seen their faith, he saith to the sick of the palsy: Son, thy sins are forgiven thee.

text.NRSV: Now some of the scribes were sitting there, questioning in their hearts,
text.Rotherham: Now there were certain of the Scribes there, sitting and deliberating in their hearts,—
text.KJV: 2:6 But there were certaine of the Scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
text.DRV: 2:6 And there were certain of the Scribes sitting there and thinking in their hearts:

text.NRSV: “Why does this fellow speak in this way? It is blasphemy! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
text.Rotherham: Why doth, this, man thus talk? he is speaking profanely! Who can forgive sins, save one, God?
text.KJV: 2:7 Why doeth this man thus speake blasphemies? Who can forgiue sinnes but God onely?
text.DRV: 2:7 Why doth he speak so? He blasphemeth. Who can forgive sins but only God?

text.NRSV: At once Jesus perceived in his spirit that they were discussing these questions among themselves; and he said to them, “Why do you raise such questions in your hearts?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, straightway, taking note in his spirit, that [thus] they are deliberating within themselves, saith [unto them]—Why, these things, are ye deliberating in your hearts?
text.KJV: 2:8 And immediatly, when Iesus perceiued in his Spirit, that they so reasoned within themselues, he said vnto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
text.DRV: 2:8 Which by and by Jesus knowing in his Spirit, that they so thought within themselves, saith to them: Why think you these things in your hearts?

text.NRSV: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Stand up and take your mat and walk’?
text.Rotherham: Which is easier—to say unto the paralytic, Forgiven are thy sins, or to say, Rise, [and] take up thy couch, and be walking?
text.KJV: 2:9 Whether is it easier to say to the sicke of the palsie, Thy sinnes be forgiuen thee: or to say, Arise, and take vp thy bed and walke?
text.DRV: 2:9 Whether is easier, to say to the sick of the palsy: Thy sins are forgiven thee; or to say: Arise, take up thy couch, and walk?

text.NRSV: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the paralytic—
text.Rotherham: But, that ye may know that the Son of Man hath authority to be forgiving sins upon the earth, he saith to the paralytic:
text.KJV: 2:10 But that yee may know that the Sonne of man hath power on earth to forgiue sinnes, (Hee saith to the sicke of the palsie,)
text.DRV: 2:10 But that you may know that the Son of man hath power in earth to forgive sins (he saith to the sick of the palsy)

text.NRSV: “I say to you, stand up, take your mat and go to your home.”
text.Rotherham: To thee, I say, Rise, take up thy couch, and be going thy way unto thy house.
text.KJV: 2:11 I say vnto thee, Arise, & take vp thy bed, & goe thy way into thine house.
text.DRV: 2:11 Isay to thee, arise, take up thy couch, and go into thy house.

text.NRSV: And he stood up, and immediately took the mat and went out before all of them; so that they were all amazed and glorified God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
text.Rotherham: And he arose, and, straightway taking up the couch, went forth before all,—so that all were beside themselves and were glorifying God, [saying]—Thus, we never saw it!
text.KJV: 2:12 And immediatly he arose, tooke vp the bed, and went foorth before them all, insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, Wee neuer saw it on this fashion.
text.DRV: 2:12 And forthwith he arose; and taking up his couch, went his way in the sight of all, so that all marveled, and glorified God, saying: That we never saw the like.

text.NRSV: Jesus went out again beside the sea; the whole crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text.Rotherham: And he went forth again by the sea,—and all the multitude was coming unto him, and he began teaching them.
text.KJV: 2:13 And he went foorth againe by the sea side, and all the multitude resorted vnto him, and he taught them.
text.DRV: 2:13 And he went forth again to the sea; and all the multitude came to him, and he taught them.

text.NRSV: As he was walking along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, passing by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting over the tax-office,—and saith unto him—Be following me! And, arising, he followed him.
text.KJV: 2:14 And as he passed by, he saw Leui the son of Alpheus sitting at the receit of Custome, and said vnto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.
text.DRV: 2:14 And when he passed by, he saw Levi of Alphæus sitting at the custom place; and he saith to him: Follow me. And rising up he followed him.

text.NRSV: And as he sat at dinner in Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were also sitting with Jesus and his disciples—for there were many who followed him.
text.Rotherham: And it cometh to pass, that he is reclining in his house, and many tax-collectors and sinners were reclining with Jesus and his disciples; for there were many, and they began following him.
text.KJV: 2:15 And it came to passe, that as Iesus sate at meate in his house, many Publicanes and sinners sate also together with Iesus and his disciples: for there were many, & they followed him.
text.DRV: 2:15 And it came to pass, as he sat at meat in his house, many Publicans and sinners did sit down together with Jesus and his Disciples. For they were many, who also followed him.

text.NRSV: When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they said to his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and Pharisees, seeing that he was eating with the sinners and the tax-collectors, began saying unto his disciples—With the tax-collectors and sinners, is he eating?
text.KJV: 2:16 And when the Scribes and Pharisees saw him eate with Publicanes and sinners, they said vnto his disciples, How is it that hee eateth and drinketh with Publicanes and sinners?
text.DRV: 2:16 And the Scribes & the Pharisees seeing that he did eat with Publicans and sinners, said to his Disciples: Why doth your Master eat & drink with Publicans and sinners?

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this, he said to them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick; I have come to call not the righteous but sinners.”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing it, Jesus saith unto them—No need, have the strong of a physician, but they who are sick, I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.
text.KJV: 2:17 When Iesus heard it, he saith vnto them, They that are whole, haue no need of the Physition, but they that are sicke: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
text.DRV: 2:17 Jesus hearing this, saith to them: The whole have not need of a Physician, but they that are ill at ease. For I came not to call the just, but sinners.

text.NRSV: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting; and people came and said to him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
text.Rotherham: And the disciples of John and the Pharisees were fasting; and they come and say unto him—For what cause, do, the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees, fast, whereas, thy disciples, fast not?
text.KJV: 2:18 And the disciples of Iohn, and of the Pharisees vsed to fast; and they come, and say vnto him, Why doe the disciples of Iohn, and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
text.DRV: 2:18 And the Disciples of John and the Pharisees did use to fast: and they come, and say to him: Why do the Disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast; but thy Disciples do not fast?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot fast while the bridegroom is with them, can they? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them—Is it possible for the sons of the bridechamber, while the bridegroom is with them, to be fasting? So long as they have the bridegroom with them, it is impossible, to fast.
text.KJV: 2:19 And Iesus said vnto them, Can the children of the bride-chamber fast, while the Bridegrome is with them? As long as they haue the Bridegrome with them, they cannot fast.
text.DRV: 2:19 And Jesus said to them: Why, can the children of the marriage fast, as long as the bridegroom is with them? So long time as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.

text.NRSV: The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast on that day.
text.Rotherham: But there will come days, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and, then, they will fast, in that day.
text.KJV: 2:20 But the dayes will come, when the Bridegrome shall bee taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those dayes.
text.DRV: 2:20 But the days will come when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them; and then they shall fast in those days.

text.NRSV: “No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak; otherwise, the patch pulls away from it, the new from the old, and a worse tear is made.
text.Rotherham: No one, seweth a patch of unshrunk cloth upon an old mantle,—otherwise, at least, the shrinking teareth away from it—the new from the old—and, a worse rent is made.
text.KJV: 2:21 No man also soweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it vp, taketh away from the old, & the rent is made worse.
text.DRV: 2:21 Nobody soweth a piece of raw cloth to an old garment: otherwise he taketh away the new piecing from the old, and there is made a greater rent.

text.NRSV: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and so are the skins; but one puts new wine into fresh wineskins.”
text.Rotherham: And, no one, poureth new wine into fold skins,—otherwise, at least, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and, the skins. [But new wine is for unused skins.]
text.KJV: 2:22 And no man putteth new wine into old bottles, else the new wine doeth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will bee marred: But new wine must bee put into new bottles.
text.DRV: 2:22 And nobody putteth new wine into old bottles: otherwise the wine bursteth the bottles, and the wine will he shed, and the bottles will be lost. But new wine must be put into new bottles.

text.NRSV: One sabbath he was going through the grainfields; and as they made their way his disciples began to pluck heads of grain.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that he, on the Sabbath, was passing through the cornfields, and his disciples began to be going forward, plucking the ears of corn.
text.KJV: 2:23 And it came to passe, that he went thorow the corne fields on the Sabbath day, & his disciples began as they went, to plucke the eares of corne.
text.DRV: 2:23 And it came to pass again when he walked through the corn on the Sabboths, and his Disciples began to go forward and to pluck the ears.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?”
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees, were saying,—See! why are they doing, on the Sabbath, what is not allowed?
text.KJV: 2:24 And the Pharisees saide vnto him, Behold, why do they on the Sabbath day that which is not lawfull?
text.DRV: 2:24 And the Pharisees said to him: Behold, why do they on the Sabboths that which is not lawful?

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need of food?
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Have ye never read what David did, when he had need and hungered,—he, and they who were with him:
text.KJV: 2:25 And he said vnto them, Haue ye neuer read what Dauid did, when hee had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
text.DRV: 2:25 And he said to them: Did you never read what David did, when he was in necessity, and himself was an hungered and they that were with him?

text.NRSV: How he entered the house of God, when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and he gave some to his companions?”
text.Rotherham: [how] he entered into the house of God, while Abiathar was High-priest, and, the presence-bread, did eat,—which it is not allowed to eat, save unto the priests,—and gave, unto them also who were with him?
text.KJV: 2:26 How hee went into the house of God in the dayes of Abiathar the high Priest, and did eate the Shew-bread, which is not lawfull to eate, but for the Priests, and gaue also to them which were with him?
text.DRV: 2:26 how he entered into the house of God under Abiathar the high Priest, and did eat the loaves of Proposition, which it was not lawful to eat but for the Priests, and did give unto them which were with him?

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “The sabbath was made for humankind, and not humankind for the sabbath;
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—The Sabbath, for man, was made, and not, man, for, the Sabbath:
text.KJV: 2:27 And hee said vnto them, The Sabbath was made for man, and not man for the Sabbath:
text.DRV: 2:27 And he said to them: The Sabboth was made for man, and not man for the Sabboth.

text.NRSV: so the Son of Man is lord even of the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: So that the Son of Man is, Lord, even of the Sabbath.
text.KJV: 2:28 Therefore the Sonne of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
text.DRV: 2:28 Therefore the Son of man is Lord of the Sabboth also.

text.NRSV: Again he entered the synagogue, and a man was there who had a withered hand.
text.Rotherham: And he entered again into a synagogue, and there-was there, a man having, his hand, withered;
text.KJV: 3:1 And he entred againe into the Synagogue, and there was a man there which had a withered hand:
text.DRV: 3:1 And he entered again into the Synagogue, and there was a man there that had a withered hand.

text.NRSV: They watched him to see whether he would cure him on the sabbath, so that they might accuse him.
text.Rotherham: and they were narrowly watching him, whether, on the Sabbath, he would cure him, that they might accuse him.
text.KJV: 3:2 And they watched him, whether hee would heale him on the Sabbath day, that they might accuse him.
text.DRV: 3:2 And they watched him whether he would cure on the Sabboths; that they might accuse him.

text.NRSV: And he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Come forward.”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto the man who hath his hand withered, Arise into the midst!
text.KJV: 3:3 And he saith vnto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
text.DRV: 3:3 And he saith to the man that had the withered hand: rise up into the midst.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath, or to do harm, to save life or to kill?” But they were silent.
text.Rotherham: and saith unto them—Is it allowed, on the Sabbath, to do good, or to do evil? To, save, life, or, to slay? but they remained silent.
text.KJV: 3:4 And hee saith vnto them, Is it lawfull to doe good on the Sabbath dayes, or to doe euill? to saue life, or to kill? but they held their peace.
text.DRV: 3:4 And he saith to them: Is it lawful on the Sabboths to do well or ill? to save a soul, or to destroy? but they held their peace.

text.NRSV: He looked around at them with anger; he was grieved at their hardness of heart and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
text.Rotherham: And, looking round upon them with anger, being at the same time grieved on account of the hardening of their heart, he saith unto the man—Stretch forth thy hand! and he stretched it forth, and his hand, was restored.
text.KJV: 3:5 And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieued for the hardnesse of their hearts, He saith vnto the man, Stretch foorth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
text.DRV: 3:5 And looking round about upon them with anger, being sorrowful for the blindness of their heart, he saith to the man: Stretch forth thy hand. And he stretched it forth; and his hand was restored unto him.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees went out and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how to destroy him.
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees, going out straightway with the Herodians, were giving counsel against him, that they should, destroy, him.
text.KJV: 3:6 And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway tooke counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
text.DRV: 3:6 And the Pharisees going forth, immediately made a consultation with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.

text.NRSV: Jesus departed with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed him;
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, with his disciples, retired unto the sea; and, a great throng from Galilee, followed, also from Judaea,
text.KJV: 3:7 But Iesus withdrew himselfe with his disciples to the Sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Iudea,
text.DRV: 3:7 But Jesus with his Disciples retired to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee & Jewry followed him,

text.NRSV: hearing all that he was doing, they came to him in great numbers from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the region around Tyre and Sidon.
text.Rotherham: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and beyond the Jordan, and around Tyre and Zidon, a great throng, hearing whatsoever things he was doing, came unto him.
text.KJV: 3:8 And from Hierusalem, and from Idumea, and from beyond Iordane, and they about Tyre & Sydon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came vnto him.
text.DRV: 3:8 and from Jerusalem, & from Idumæa, and beyond Jordan. And they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, hearing the things which he did, came to him.

text.NRSV: He told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, so that they would not crush him;
text.Rotherham: And he spake unto his disciples that a little boat might attend him, because of the multitude,—that they might not be pressing upon him.
text.KJV: 3:9 And he spake to his disciples that a small ship should wait on him, because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
text.DRV: 3:9 And he spake to his Disciples that a boat might attend on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.

text.NRSV: for he had cured so many that all who had diseases pressed upon him to touch him.
text.Rotherham: For he cured many, so that they were besieging him, that they might touch him—as many as had plagues;
text.KJV: 3:10 For he had healed many, insomuch that they preassed vpon him, for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
text.DRV: 3:10 For he healed many, so that there pressed in upon him for to touch him, as many had hurts.

text.NRSV: Whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and shouted, “You are the Son of God!”
text.Rotherham: and the impure spirits, as soon as they beheld him, were falling down to him, and crying aloud, while he was speaking, Thou, art the Son of God!
text.KJV: 3:11 And vncleane spirits, when they saw him, fell downe before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Sonne of God.
text.DRV: 3:11 And the unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down unto him: and they cried saying:

text.NRSV: But he sternly ordered them not to make him known.
text.Rotherham: and sternly was he rebuking them, lest they should make him manifest.
text.KJV: 3:12 And he straitly charged them, that they should not make him knowen.
text.DRV: 3:12 Thou art the Son of God. And he vehemently charged them that they should not disclose him.

text.NRSV: He went up the mountain and called to him those whom he wanted, and they came to him.
text.Rotherham: And he goeth up into the mountain and calleth near whom, he, pleased,—and they went away unto him;
text.KJV: 3:13 And he goeth vp into a mountaine, and calleth vnto him whom he would: and they came vnto him.
text.DRV: 3:13 And ascending into a mountain, he called unto him whom he would himself, and they came to him.

text.NRSV: And he appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, to be with him, and to be sent out to proclaim the message,
text.Rotherham: and he appointed a twelve, whom also he named, Apostles,—that they might be with him, and that he might be sending them forth to make proclamation,
text.KJV: 3:14 And he ordeined twelue, that they should be with him, and that hee might send them foorth to preach:
text.DRV: 3:14 And he made that twelve should be with him, and that he might send them to preach.

text.NRSV: and to have authority to cast out demons.
text.Rotherham: and to have authority to cast out the demons;
text.KJV: 3:15 And to haue power to heale sickenesses, and to cast out deuils.
text.DRV: 3:15 And he gave them power to cure infirmities, and to cast out devils.

text.NRSV: So he appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
text.Rotherham: and he appointed the twelve (and imposed a name on Simon—) Peter,
text.KJV: 3:16 And Simon he surnamed Peter.
text.DRV: 3:16 And he gave to Simon the name Peter.

text.NRSV: James son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder);
text.Rotherham: and James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James (and imposed on them a name—Boanerges, that is, sons of thunder);
text.KJV: 3:17 And Iames the sonne of Zebedee, and Iohn the brother of Iames (and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sonnes of thunder.)
text.DRV: 3:17 And James of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he called their names, Boanerges, which is, the sons of thunder.

text.NRSV: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean,
text.Rotherham: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the zealot;
text.KJV: 3:18 And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and Iames the sonne of Alpheus, and Thaddeus, and Simon the Canaanite,
text.DRV: 3:18 And Andrew and Philippe, and Barthlemew and Matthew, and Thomas and James of Alphæus, and Thaddæus and Simon Cananæus,

text.NRSV: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
text.Rotherham: and Judas Iscariot, who also delivered him up. And he cometh into a house;
text.KJV: 3:19 And Iudas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.
text.DRV: 3:19 and Judas Iscariote, who also betrayed him.

text.NRSV: Then he went home; and the crowd came together again, so that they could not even eat.
text.Rotherham: and the multitude come together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
text.KJV: 3:20 And the multitude commeth together againe, so that they could not so much as eate bread.
text.DRV: 3:20 And they come to a house; and the multitude resorteth together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.

text.NRSV: When his family heard it, they went out to restrain him, for people were saying, “He has gone out of his mind.”
text.Rotherham: And they who were near to him, hearing of it, went out to secure him,—for they were saying—He is beside himself!
text.KJV: 3:21 And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him, for they said, He is beside himselfe.
text.DRV: 3:21 And when his had heard of it, they went forth to lay hands on him. For they said that he was become mad.

text.NRSV: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul, and by the ruler of the demons he casts out demons.”
text.Rotherham: And, the Scribes who from Jerusalem had come down, were saying—He hath, Beelzebul; and—In the ruler of the demons, casteth he out the demons.
text.KJV: 3:22 ¶ And the Scribes which came downe from Hierusalem, said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the deuils, casteth he out deuils.
text.DRV: 3:22 And the Scribes which were come down from Jerusalem, said: That he hath Beelzebub; and that in the prince of devils he casteth out devils.

text.NRSV: And he called them to him, and spoke to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
text.Rotherham: And, calling them near, in parables, began he to say unto them—How can, Satan, cast, Satan out?
text.KJV: 3:23 And he called them vnto him, and said vnto them in parables, Howe can Satan cast out Satan?
text.DRV: 3:23 And after he had called them together, he said to them in parables: How can Satan cast out Satan?

text.NRSV: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
text.Rotherham: And, if a kingdom against itself be divided, that kingdom cannot be made to stand;
text.KJV: 3:24 And if a kingdome be diuided against it selfe, that kingdome cannot stand.
text.DRV: 3:24 And if a Kingdom be divided against itself, that Kingdom cannot stand.

text.NRSV: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
text.Rotherham: And, if a house against itself be divided, the house shall be unable to stand;
text.KJV: 3:25 And if a house be diuided against it selfe, that house cannot stand.
text.DRV: 3:25 And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

text.NRSV: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but his end has come.
text.Rotherham: And, if, Satan, hath risen up against himself, and become divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
text.KJV: 3:26 And if Satan rise vp against himselfe, and be diuided, hee cannot stand, but hath an end.
text.DRV: 3:26 And if Satan be risen against himself, he is divided, and cannot stand, but hath an end.

text.NRSV: But no one can enter a strong man’s house and plunder his property without first tying up the strong man; then indeed the house can be plundered.
text.Rotherham: But, no one is able into the house of the mighty one to enter, and, his spoils, to carry off, unless, first the mighty one, he bind, and, then his house, will he plunder!
text.KJV: 3:27 No man can enter into a strong mans house, and spoile his goods, except he will first bind the strong man, and then he will spoile his house.
text.DRV: 3:27 Nobody can rifle the vessels of the strong, being entered into his house, unless he first bind the strong, & then shall he rifle his house.

text.NRSV: “Truly I tell you, people will be forgiven for their sins and whatever blasphemies they utter;
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—All things shall be forgiven unto the sons of men,—the sins and the profanities wherewithal they shall profane;
text.KJV: 3:28 Uerely I say vnto you, All sinnes shalbe forgiuen vnto the sonnes of men, and blasphemies, wherewith soeuer they shall blaspheme:
text.DRV: 3:28 Amen I say to you, that all sins shall be forgiven the sons of men, & the blasphemies wherewith they shall blaspheme:

text.NRSV: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit can never have forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin”—
text.Rotherham: But, whosoever shall revile against the Holy Spirit, hath no forgiveness, unto times age-abiding,—but is guilty of an age-abiding sin:
text.KJV: 3:29 But he that shal blaspheme against the holy Ghost, hath neuer forgiuenesse, but is in danger of eternall damnation.
text.DRV: 3:29 But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost, he hath not forgiveness for ever, but shall be guilty of an eternal sin. Because they said: He hath an unclean spirit.

text.NRSV: for they had said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
text.Rotherham: because they were saying—An impure spirit, he hath!
text.KJV: 3:30 Because they said, He hath an vncleane spirit.
text.DRV: 3:30 And there come his mother and brethren; and standing without they sent unto him calling him,

text.NRSV: Then his mother and his brothers came; and standing outside, they sent to him and called him.
text.Rotherham: And there come his mother and his brethren, and, standing, outside, they sent unto him, calling him.
text.KJV: 3:31 ¶ There came then his brethren, and his mother, and standing without, sent vnto him, calling him.
text.DRV: 3:31 And the multitude sat about him; and they say to him:

text.NRSV: A crowd was sitting around him; and they said to him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside, asking for you.”
text.Rotherham: And there was sitting around him a multitude, and they say unto him—Lo! thy mother and thy brethren, outside, are seeking thee!
text.KJV: 3:32 And the multitude sate about him and they said vnto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seeke for thee.
text.DRV: 3:32 Behold thy mother and thy brethren without seek thee.

text.NRSV: And he replied, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
text.Rotherham: And, answering them, he saith—Who are my mother and my brethren?
text.KJV: 3:33 And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
text.DRV: 3:33 And answering them, he said: Who is my mother and my brethren?

text.NRSV: And looking at those who sat around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
text.Rotherham: And, looking around on them who, about him, in a circle, were sitting, he saith, See! my mother and my brethren!
text.KJV: 3:34 And he looked round about on them which sate about him, and saide, Behold my mother and my brethren.
text.DRV: 3:34 And looking upon them which sat round about him, he saith: Behold my mother and my brethren.

text.NRSV: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”
text.Rotherham: Whosoever shall do the will of God, the same, is my brother and sister and mother.
text.KJV: 3:35 For whosoeuer shall doe the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.
text.DRV: 3:35 For whosoever shall do the will of God, he is my brother and my sister and mother.

text.NRSV: Again he began to teach beside the sea. Such a very large crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat on the sea and sat there, while the whole crowd was beside the sea on the land.
text.Rotherham: And, again, began he to be teaching by the sea; and there come together unto him a very great multitude, so that, he, into a boat, entering, was sitting upon the sea,—and, all the multitude, were, near the sea upon the land.
text.KJV: 4:1 And he beganne againe to teach by the Sea side: and there was gathered vnto him a great multitude, so that he entred into a ship, and sate in the Sea: and the whole multitude was by the Sea on the land.
text.DRV: 4:1 And again he began to teach at the sea side; and a great multitude was gathered together unto him so that he went up into a boat, and sat in the sea, and all the multitude about the sea was upon the land.

text.NRSV: He began to teach them many things in parables, and in his teaching he said to them:
text.Rotherham: And he began to teach them, in parables, many things, and was saying unto them, in his teaching—
text.KJV: 4:2 And he taught them many things by parables, and said vnto them in his doctrine,
text.DRV: 4:2 And he taught them in parables many things, and said to them in his doctrine:

text.NRSV: “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
text.Rotherham: Hearken! Lo! the sower went forth to sow;
text.KJV: 4:3 Hearken, Behold, there went out a sower to sow:
text.DRV: 4:3 Hear ye; Behold, the sower went forth to sow.

text.NRSV: And as he sowed, some seed fell on the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some, indeed, fell by the pathway, and the birds came, and devoured it;
text.KJV: 4:4 And it came to passe as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the foules of the aire came, & deuoured it vp.
text.DRV: 4:4 And whiles he soweth, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came, and did eat it.

text.NRSV: Other seed fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil, and it sprang up quickly, since it had no depth of soil.
text.Rotherham: And, some, fell on the rocky places, [even] where it had not much earth,—and, straightway, it sprang forth, by reason of its not having depth of earth;
text.KJV: 4:5 And some fell on stonie ground, where it had not much earth: and immediately it sprang vp, because it had no depth of earth.
text.DRV: 4:5 And othersome fell upon rocky places where it had not much earth: and it shot up immediately, because it had not deepness of earth:

text.NRSV: And when the sun rose, it was scorched; and since it had no root, it withered away.
text.Rotherham: and, when the sun arose, it was scorched,—and, by reason of its not having root, it was dried up;
text.KJV: 4:6 But when the Sunne was vp, it was scorched, and because it had no roote, it withered away.
text.DRV: 4:6 and when the sun was risen, it parched, and because it had not root, it withered.

text.NRSV: Other seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no grain.
text.Rotherham: And, some, fell among the thorns, and the thorns came up and choked it, and, fruit, it yielded not;
text.KJV: 4:7 And some fell among thornes, and the thornes grew vp, and choked it, and it yeelded no fruite.
text.DRV: 4:7 And some fell among thorns: and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded not fruit.

text.NRSV: Other seed fell into good soil and brought forth grain, growing up and increasing and yielding thirty and sixty and a hundredfold.”
text.Rotherham: And, other, fell into the good ground, and was yielding fruit, coming up and growing,—and was bearing, thirtyfold and sixtyfold and a hundredfold,
text.KJV: 4:8 And other fell on good ground, and did yeeld fruite that sprang vp, and increased, and brought foorth some thirtie, & some sixtie, & some an hundred.
text.DRV: 4:8 And some fell upon good ground; and it yielded fruit that grew up & increased, and it brought forth, one thirty, one sixty, and one an hundred.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Let anyone with ears to hear listen!”
text.Rotherham: And he was saying—He that hath ears to hear, let him hear!
text.KJV: 4:9 And he said vnto them, He that hath eares to heare, let him heare.
text.DRV: 4:9 And he said: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: When he was alone, those who were around him along with the twelve asked him about the parables.
text.Rotherham: And when he was alone they who were about him with the twelve questioned him as to the parables;
text.KJV: 4:10 And when hee was alone, they that were about him, with the twelue, asked of him the parable.
text.DRV: 4:10 And when he was alone, the Twelve that were with him, asked him the parable.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “To you has been given the secret of the kingdom of God, but for those outside, everything comes in parables;
text.Rotherham: and he was saying unto them—To you, the sacred secret hath been given of the kingdom of God, whereas, to them who are outside, in parables are all things coming to pass,—that
text.KJV: 4:11 And he said vnto them, Unto you it is giuen to know the mystery of the kingdome of God: but vnto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:
text.DRV: 4:11 And he said to them: To you it is given to know the mystery of the Kingdom of God; but to them that are without, all things are done in parables:

text.NRSV: in order that ‘they may indeed look, but not perceive, and may indeed listen, but not understand; so that they may not turn again and be forgiven.’”
text.Rotherham: They may surely look and yet not see, and surely hear and yet not understand, lest once they should return and it be forgiven them.
text.KJV: 4:12 That seeing they may see, and not perceiue, and hearing they may heare, and not vnderstand, lest at any time they should be conuerted, and their sinnes should be forgiuen them.
text.DRV: 4:12 that seeing they may see, and not see; and hearing they may hear, and not understand: lest at any time they should be converted and their sins be forgiven them.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Know ye not this parable? How then will ye get to know all the parables?
text.KJV: 4:13 And he said vnto them, Know ye not this parable? And how then will you know all parables?
text.DRV: 4:13 And he saith to them: Do you not know this parable? And how shall you know all parables?

text.NRSV: The sower sows the word.
text.Rotherham: The sower, soweth the word;
text.KJV: 4:14 ¶ The Sower soweth the word.
text.DRV: 4:14 He that soweth, soweth the word.

text.NRSV: These are the ones on the path where the word is sown: when they hear, Satan immediately comes and takes away the word that is sown in them.
text.Rotherham: And these are they beside the pathway where the word is sown,—and, as soon as they hear, straightway, cometh Satan, and snatcheth away the word which hath been sown into them;
text.KJV: 4:15 And these are they by the way side, where the word is sowen, but when they haue heard, Satan commeth immediately, and taketh away the word that was sowen in their hearts.
text.DRV: 4:15 And they by the way side, are these: where the word is sown, and when they shall have heard, immediately cometh Satan, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.

text.NRSV: And these are the ones sown on rocky ground: when they hear the word, they immediately receive it with joy.
text.Rotherham: And these are likewise they upon the rocky places sown,—who, as soon as they hear the word, straightway, with joy receive it,
text.KJV: 4:16 And these are they likewise which are sowen on stonie ground, who when they haue heard the word, immediately receiue it with gladnesse:
text.DRV: 4:16 And they likewise that are sown upon the rocky places, are these: who when they hear the word, immediately with joy receive it;

text.NRSV: But they have no root, and endure only for a while; then, when trouble or persecution arises on account of the word, immediately they fall away.
text.Rotherham: and have no root in themselves, but, only for a season are,—afterwards, when there ariseth tribulation or persecution by reason of the word, straightway, they find cause of stumbling;
text.KJV: 4:17 And haue no roote in themselues, and so endure but for a time: afterward when affliction or persecution ariseth for the words sake, immediately they are offended.
text.DRV: 4:17 And they have not root in themselves, but are temporal: afterward when tribulation is risen and persecution for the word, forthwith they are scandalized.

text.NRSV: And others are those sown among the thorns: these are the ones who hear the word,
text.Rotherham: And others are they who among thorns are sown,—these are they who hear the word,
text.KJV: 4:18 And these are they which are sowen among thorns: such as heare the word,
text.DRV: 4:18 And other there be that are sown among thorns: these are they that hear the word,

text.NRSV: but the cares of the world, and the lure of wealth, and the desire for other things come in and choke the word, and it yields nothing.
text.Rotherham: and, the anxieties of the age, and the deceit of wealth, and the covetings about the remaining things, entering in, choke up the word, and, unfruitful, it becometh;
text.KJV: 4:19 And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulnesse of riches, and the lusts of other things entring in, choke the word, and it becommeth vnfruitfull.
text.DRV: 4:19 and the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches, and concupiscences about other things entering in choke the word, and it is made fruitless.

text.NRSV: And these are the ones sown on the good soil: they hear the word and accept it and bear fruit, thirty and sixty and a hundredfold.”
text.Rotherham: And those yonder are they who on the good ground are sown,—who, indeed, hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit—thirtyfold and sixtyfold and a hundredfold.
text.KJV: 4:20 And these are they which are sowen on good ground, such as heare the word, and receiue it, & bring foorth fruit, some thirty fold, some sixtie, and some an hundred.
text.DRV: 4:20 And these are they that were sown upon the good ground, which hear the word, and receive it, and yield fruit one thirty, one sixty, and one an hundred.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Is a lamp brought in to be put under the bushel basket, or under the bed, and not on the lampstand?
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—Doth the lamp come that under the measure it should be put, or under the couch? Is it not that upon the lampstand it may be put?
text.KJV: 4:21 ¶ And he said vnto them, Is a candle brought to be put vnder a bushell, or vnder a bed? & not to be set on a candlesticke?
text.DRV: 4:21 And he said to them: Cometh a candle to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? And not to be put upon the candlestick?

text.NRSV: For there is nothing hidden, except to be disclosed; nor is anything secret, except to come to light.
text.Rotherham: For it is not hidden, save that it may be made visible; neither did it get hidden away, but that it might come into a place where it could be seen.
text.KJV: 4:22 For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested: neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.
text.DRV: 4:22 For there is nothing hid, which shall not be made manifest: neither was any thing made secret, but that it shall come to light.

text.NRSV: Let anyone with ears to hear listen!”
text.Rotherham: If any one hath ears to hear, let him hear.
text.KJV: 4:23 If any man haue eares to heare, let him heare.
text.DRV: 4:23 If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Pay attention to what you hear; the measure you give will be the measure you get, and still more will be given you.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—Be taking heed what ye are hearing:—with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured unto you, and added unto you;
text.KJV: 4:24 And he said vnto them, Take heed what you heare: With what measure ye mete, it shalbe measured to you: And vnto you that heare, shal more be giuen.
text.DRV: 4:24 And he said to them: See what you hear. In what measure you mete, it shall be measured to you again, and more shall be given to you.

text.NRSV: For to those who have, more will be given; and from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.”
text.Rotherham: For, he that hath, it shall be given, unto him, and, he that hath not, even what he hath, shall be taken from him.
text.KJV: 4:25 For he that hath, to him shall be giuen: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken, euen that which he hath.
text.DRV: 4:25 For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, that also which he hath, shall be taken away from him.

text.NRSV: He also said, “The kingdom of God is as if someone would scatter seed on the ground,
text.Rotherham: And he was saying—Thus, is the kingdom of God: As a man may cast seed upon the earth,
text.KJV: 4:26 ¶ And he said, So is the kingdome of God, as if a man should cast seede into the ground,
text.DRV: 4:26 And he said: So is the Kingdom of God, as if a man cast seed into the earth,

text.NRSV: and would sleep and rise night and day, and the seed would sprout and grow, he does not know how.
text.Rotherham: and be sleeping and rising, night and day,—and the seed be sprouting, and lengthening itself,—how, he, knoweth not:
text.KJV: 4:27 And should sleepe, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring, and grow vp, he knoweth not how.
text.DRV: 4:27 and sleep, and rise up night and day, and the seed spring, and grow up whiles he knoweth not.

text.NRSV: The earth produces of itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full grain in the head.
text.Rotherham: of itself, the earth beareth fruit,—first, a blade, afterwards, an ear, after that, full corn in the ear;
text.KJV: 4:28 For the earth bringeth foorth fruite of herselfe, first the blade, then the eare, after that the full corne in the eare.
text.DRV: 4:28 For the earth of itself bringeth forth fruit, first the blade, then the ear, afterward the full corn in the ear.

text.NRSV: But when the grain is ripe, at once he goes in with his sickle, because the harvest has come.”
text.Rotherham: but, as soon as the fruit yieldeth itself up, straightway, he sendeth forth the sickle, because standing by is the harvest.—
text.KJV: 4:29 But when the fruite is brought foorth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the haruest is come.
text.DRV: 4:29 And when the fruit hath brought out itself, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because harvest is come.

text.NRSV: He also said, “With what can we compare the kingdom of God, or what parable shall we use for it?
text.Rotherham: And he was saying—How shall we liken the kingdom of God, or, in what parable, shall we put it?
text.KJV: 4:30 ¶ And he said, Wherunto shal we liken the kingdome of God? Or with what comparison shall we compare it?
text.DRV: 4:30 And he said: To what shall we liken the Kingdom of God? or to what parable shall we compare it?

text.NRSV: It is like a mustard seed, which, when sown upon the ground, is the smallest of all the seeds on earth;
text.Rotherham: As a grain of mustard seed,—which, whensoever it may be sown upon the earth, is less than all the seeds that are upon the earth;
text.KJV: 4:31 It is like a graine of mustard seed: which when it is sowen in the earth, is lesse then all the seedes that be in the earth.
text.DRV: 4:31 As a mustard seed; which when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that are in the earth:

text.NRSV: yet when it is sown it grows up and becomes the greatest of all shrubs, and puts forth large branches, so that the birds of the air can make nests in its shade.”
text.Rotherham: and, as soon as it is sown, springeth up, and becometh greater than all garden plants, and produceth large branches, so that, under the shade thereof, the birds of heaven can find shelter.
text.KJV: 4:32 But when it is sowen, it groweth vp, and becommeth greater then all herbes, & shooteth out great branches, so that the fowles of the aire may lodge vnder the shadow of it.
text.DRV: 4:32 And when it is sown, it riseth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and maketh great boughs, so that the birds of the air may dwell under the shadow thereof.

text.NRSV: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it;
text.Rotherham: And, with many such parables as these, was he speaking unto them the word,—according as they were able to hear;
text.KJV: 4:33 And with many such parables spake hee the word vnto them, as they were able to heare it.
text.DRV: 4:33 And with many such parables he spake to them the word, according as they were able to hear:

text.NRSV: he did not speak to them except in parables, but he explained everything in private to his own disciples.
text.Rotherham: but, without a parable, was he not speaking unto them,—privately, however, unto his own disciples, was he explaining all things.
text.KJV: 4:34 But without a parable spake he not vnto them, and when they were alone, hee expounded all things to his disciples.
text.DRV: 4:34 and without parable he did not speak unto them; but apart, he explicated all things to his Disciples.

text.NRSV: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let us go across to the other side.”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them, on that day, when evening came, Let us cross over unto the other side;
text.KJV: 4:35 And the same day, when the Euen was come, he saith vnto them, Let vs passe ouer vnto the other side.
text.DRV: 4:35 And he saith to them in that day, when evening was come: Let us pass over to the other side.

text.NRSV: And leaving the crowd, they took him with them in the boat, just as he was. Other boats were with him.
text.Rotherham: and, dismissing the multitude, they take him with them, as he was, in the boat, and, other boats, were with him.
text.KJV: 4:36 And when they had sent away the multitude, they tooke him, euen as he was in the ship, and there were also with him other litle ships.
text.DRV: 4:36 And dismissing the multitude, they take him so as he was in the boat: and there were other boats with him.

text.NRSV: A great windstorm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so that the boat was already being swamped.
text.Rotherham: And there ariseth a great tempest of wind,—and, the waves, were dashing over into the boat, so that, already being filled, was the boat.
text.KJV: 4:37 And there arose a great storme of wind, and the waues beat into the ship, so that it was now full.
text.DRV: 4:37 And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the boat, so that the boat was filled.

text.NRSV: But he was in the stern, asleep on the cushion; and they woke him up and said to him, “Teacher, do you not care that we are perishing?”
text.Rotherham: And he was in the stern, on the cushion, sleeping. And they arouse him, and say unto him, Teacher! carest thou not that we perish?
text.KJV: 4:38 And he was in the hinder part of the ship asleepe on a pillow: and they awake him, and say vnto him, Master, carest thou not, that we perish?
text.DRV: 4:38 And he was in the hinder part of the boat, sleeping upon a pillow; and they raise him, and say to him: Master, doth it not pertain to thee that we perish?

text.NRSV: He woke up and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” Then the wind ceased, and there was a dead calm.
text.Rotherham: And, roused up, he rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea—Hush! be still! and the wind lulled, and it became a great calm.
text.KJV: 4:39 And hee arose, and rebuked the winde, and said vnto the sea, Peace, be still: and the winde ceased, and there was a great calme.
text.DRV: 4:39 And rising up he threatened the wind, and said to the sea: Peace, be still. And the wind ceased; and there was made a great calm.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Why are you afraid? Have you still no faith?”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Why, are ye, fearful? Not yet, have ye faith?
text.KJV: 4:40 And he said vnto them, Why are ye so fearefull? How is it that you haue no faith?
text.DRV: 4:40 And he said to them: Why are you fearful? Neither yet have you faith? And they feared with great fear: and they said one to another, who is this (thinkest thou) that both wind and sea obey him?

text.NRSV: And they were filled with great awe and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”
text.Rotherham: And they were caused to fear a great fear, and were saying one to another—Who then is, this, that, both the wind and the sea, give ear unto him?
text.KJV: 4:41 And they feared exceedingly, and saide one to another, What maner of man is this, that euen the winde and the sea obey him?

text.NRSV: They came to the other side of the sea, to the country of the Gerasenes.
text.Rotherham: And they came unto the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gerasenes.
text.KJV: 5:1 And they came ouer vnto the other side of the sea, into the countrey of the Gadarenes.
text.DRV: 5:1 And they came beyond the strait of the sea into the country of the Gerasens.

text.NRSV: And when he had stepped out of the boat, immediately a man out of the tombs with an unclean spirit met him.
text.Rotherham: And, when he went forth out of the boat, [straightway], there met him, from amongst the tombs, a man, in an impure spirit:
text.KJV: 5:2 And when hee was come out of the ship, immediatly there met him out of the tombes, a man with an vncleane spirit,
text.DRV: 5:2 And as he went out of the boat, immediately there met him out of the sepulchres a man in an unclean spirit,

text.NRSV: He lived among the tombs; and no one could restrain him any more, even with a chain;
text.Rotherham: who had his dwelling among the tombs, and, not even with a chain, any longer, was anyone able to bind him,—
text.KJV: 5:3 Who had his dwelling among the tombs, and no man could binde him, no not with chaines:
text.DRV: 5:3 that had his dwelling in the sepulchres, and neither with chains could any man now bind him:

text.NRSV: for he had often been restrained with shackles and chains, but the chains he wrenched apart, and the shackles he broke in pieces; and no one had the strength to subdue him.
text.Rotherham: because of his having been, many times, with fetters and chains, bound, and the chains having been plucked asunder by him, and the fetters smashed, and no one was mighty enough to tame him;
text.KJV: 5:4 Because that hee had bene often bound with fetters and chaines, and the chaines had bene plucked asunder by him, and the fetters broken in pieces: neither could any man tame him.
text.DRV: 5:4 For being often bound with fetters and chains, he had burst the chains, and broken the fetters, and nobody could tame him.

text.NRSV: Night and day among the tombs and on the mountains he was always howling and bruising himself with stones.
text.Rotherham: and, continually, night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, was he crying aloud, and cutting himself in pieces with stones.
text.KJV: 5:5 And alwayes night and day, hee was in the mountaines, and in the tombes, crying, and cutting himselfe with stones.
text.DRV: 5:5 And he was always day and night in the sepulchres and in the mountains, crying and cutting himself with stones.

text.NRSV: When he saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and bowed down before him;
text.Rotherham: And, seeing Jesus from afar, he ran and bowed down to him;
text.KJV: 5:6 But when hee saw Iesus afarre off, he came and worshipped him,
text.DRV: 5:6 And seeing Jesus a far off, he ran and adored him:

text.NRSV: and he shouted at the top of his voice, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, do not torment me.”
text.Rotherham: and, crying out with a loud voice, saith—What have in common with thee? O Jesus, Son of God Most High? I adjure thee by God,—Do not torment me!
text.KJV: 5:7 And cried with a lowd voice, and said, What haue I to doe with thee, Iesus, thou Sonne of the most high God? I adiure thee by God, that thou torment me not.
text.DRV: 5:7 And crying with a great voice, said: What to me and thee Jesus, the Son of God most high: I adjure thee by God that thou torment me not.

text.NRSV: For he had said to him, “Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!”
text.Rotherham: For he was saying to him—Go forth thou impure spirit, out of the man;
text.KJV: 5:8 (For he said vnto him, Come out of the man, thou vncleane spirit.)
text.DRV: 5:8 For he said unto him: Go out of the man thou unclean spirit.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” He replied, “My name is Legion; for we are many.”
text.Rotherham: and he was questioning him—What is thy name? and he saith to him—Legion, is my name, because we are, many;
