text.KJV: 5:9 And he asked him, What is thy name? And hee answered, saying, My name is Legion: for we are many.
text.DRV: 5:9 And he asked him, what is thy name? And he saith to him: My name is Legion; because we are many.

text.NRSV: He begged him earnestly not to send them out of the country.
text.Rotherham: and he was beseeching him much that he would not send them forth outside the country.
text.KJV: 5:10 And hee besought him much, that he would not send them away out of the countrey.
text.DRV: 5:10 And he besought him much, that he would not expel him out of the country.

text.NRSV: Now on the hillside a large herd of swine was feeding;
text.Rotherham: Now there was there, near the mountain, a large herd of swine, feeding;
text.KJV: 5:11 Now there was there nigh vnto the mountaines a great herd of swine, feeding.
text.DRV: 5:11 And there was there about the mountain a great herd of swine, feeding.

text.NRSV: and the unclean spirits begged him, “Send us into the swine; let us enter them.”
text.Rotherham: and they besought him, saying—Send us into the swine, that, into them, we may enter;
text.KJV: 5:12 And all the deuils besought him, saying, Send vs into the swine, that we may enter into them.
text.DRV: 5:12 And the spirits besought him, saying: Send us into the swine, that we may enter into them.

text.NRSV: So he gave them permission. And the unclean spirits came out and entered the swine; and the herd, numbering about two thousand, rushed down the steep bank into the sea, and were drowned in the sea.
text.Rotherham: and he suffered them. And the impure spirits, going out, entered into the swine, and the herd rushed down the cliff into the sea, about two thousand, and were choked in the sea.
text.KJV: 5:13 And forthwith Iesus gaue them leaue. And the vncleane spirits went out, and entred into the swine, and the herd ranne violently downe a steepe place into the sea (they were about two thousand) and were choked in the sea.
text.DRV: 5:13 And Jesus immediately granted unto them. And the unclean spirits going out entered into the swine: and the herd with great violence was carried headlong into the sea, about two thousand, and were stifled in the sea.

text.NRSV: The herdsmen ran off and told it in the city and in the country. Then people came to see what it was that had happened.
text.Rotherham: And, they who had been feeding them, fled, and bare tidings into the city and into the fields,—and they came to see what it was that had happened.
text.KJV: 5:14 And they that fed the swine fled, and tolde it in the citie, and in the countrey. And they went out to see what it was that was done.
text.DRV: 5:14 And they that fed them, fled and carried news into the city and into the fields. And they went forth to see what was done:

text.NRSV: They came to Jesus and saw the demoniac sitting there, clothed and in his right mind, the very man who had had the legion; and they were afraid.
text.Rotherham: And they come unto Jesus, and view the demonized man, clothed and of sound mind, him who had had the legion,—and they were struck with fear.
text.KJV: 5:15 And they come to Iesus, and see him that was possessed with the deuill, and had the Legion, sitting, and clothed, and in his right minde: and they were afraid.
text.DRV: 5:15 And they come to Jesus, and they see him that was vexed of the devil, sitting, clothed, and well in his wits: & they were afraid.

text.NRSV: Those who had seen what had happened to the demoniac and to the swine reported it.
text.Rotherham: And the beholders narrated to them, how it happened to the demonized man, and concerning the swine.
text.KJV: 5:16 And they that saw it, tolde them how it befell to him that was possessed with the deuill, and also concerning the swine.
text.DRV: 5:16 And they that had seen it, told them, in what manner he had been dealt withal that had the devil; & of the swine.

text.NRSV: Then they began to beg Jesus to leave their neighborhood.
text.Rotherham: And they began to beseech him, to depart from their bounds.
text.KJV: 5:17 And they began to pray him to depart out of their coasts.
text.DRV: 5:17 And they began to desire him, that he would depart from their coasts.

text.NRSV: As he was getting into the boat, the man who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.
text.Rotherham: And, as he was entering into the boat, he who had been demonized was beseeching him, that, with him, he might be.
text.KJV: 5:18 And when hee was come into the ship, he that had bene possessed with the deuill prayed him that hee might bee with him.
text.DRV: 5:18 And when he went up into the boat, he that had been vexed of the devil, began to beseech him that he might be with him:

text.NRSV: But Jesus refused, and said to him, “Go home to your friends, and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and what mercy he has shown you.”
text.Rotherham: And he suffered him not, but saith unto him—Withdraw into thy house, unto thine own, and bear tidings unto them, how many things the Lord for thee hath done, and hath had mercy on thee.
text.KJV: 5:19 Howbeit Iesus suffered him not, but saith vnto him, Goe home to thy friends, and tel them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.
text.DRV: 5:19 And he admitted him not, but saith to him: Go into thy house to thine, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had mercy upon thee.

text.NRSV: And he went away and began to proclaim in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him; and everyone was amazed.
text.Rotherham: And he departed, and began proclaiming, in the Decapolis, how many things Jesus had done for him,—and, all, were marvelling.
text.KJV: 5:20 And hee departed, and began to publish in Decapolis, how great things Iesus had done for him: and all men did marueile.
text.DRV: 5:20 And he went his way, and began to publish in Decapolis how great things Jesus had done to him: and all marveled.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had crossed again in the boat to the other side, a large crowd gathered around him; and he was by the sea.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus crossing over in the boat again unto the other side, there was gathered a large multitude unto him, and he was by the lake.
text.KJV: 5:21 And when Iesus was passed ouer againe by ship vnto the other side, much people gathered vnto him, and he was nigh vnto the Sea.
text.DRV: 5:21 And when Jesus had passed in boat again over the strait, a great multitude assembled together unto him, and he was about the sea.

text.NRSV: Then one of the leaders of the synagogue named Jairus came and, when he saw him, fell at his feet
text.Rotherham: And there cometh one of the synagogue-rulers, by name Jairus, and, seeing him, falleth at his feet,
text.KJV: 5:22 And behold, there commeth one of the Rulers of the Synagogue, Iairus by name, and when he saw him, he fell at his feete,
text.DRV: 5:22 And there cometh one of the Arch-synagogues, named Jairus: and seeing him, he falleth down at his feet.

text.NRSV: and begged him repeatedly, “My little daughter is at the point of death. Come and lay your hands on her, so that she may be made well, and live.”
text.Rotherham: and beseecheth him much, saying—My little daughter is at her last!—that, coming, thou wouldest lay thy hands upon her, that she may be made well, and live;
text.KJV: 5:23 And besought him greatly, saying, My litle daughter lieth at the point of death, I pray thee come and lay thy hands on her, that shee may be healed, and she shall liue.
text.DRV: 5:23 And besought him much, saying: That my daughter is at the point of death, come, impose thy hands upon her, that she may be safe and live.

text.NRSV: So he went with him. And a large crowd followed him and pressed in on him.
text.Rotherham: and he departed with him. And there was following him a large multitude, and they were pressing upon him.
text.KJV: 5:24 And Iesus went with him, and much people followed him, and thronged him.
text.DRV: 5:24 And he went with him, and a great multitude followed him, and they thronged him.

text.NRSV: Now there was a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman, who had had a flow of blood twelve years,
text.KJV: 5:25 And a certaine woman which had an issue of blood twelue yeeres,
text.DRV: 5:25 And a woman which was in an issue of blood twelve years,

text.NRSV: She had endured much under many physicians, and had spent all that she had; and she was no better, but rather grew worse.
text.Rotherham: and suffered much from many physicians, and spent all her means, and profited, nothing,—but had, rather, become worse,
text.KJV: 5:26 And had suffered many things of many Physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was nothing bettered, but rather grew worse,
text.DRV: 5:26 and had suffered many things of many Physicians, and had bestowed all that she had, neither was any thing the better, but was rather worse:

text.NRSV: She had heard about Jesus, and came up behind him in the crowd and touched his cloak;
text.Rotherham: hearing the things about Jesus, came in the crowd from behind, and touched his mantle;
text.KJV: 5:27 When shee had heard of Iesus, came in the prease behinde, and touched his garment.
text.DRV: 5:27 when she had heard of Jesus, she came in the press behind him, and touched his garment.

text.NRSV: for she said, “If I but touch his clothes, I will be made well.”
text.Rotherham: for she was saying—If I can touch so much as his mantle, I shall be made well;
text.KJV: 5:28 For she said, If I may touch but his clothes, I shalbe whole.
text.DRV: 5:28 For she said: That if I shall touch but his garment, I shall be safe.

text.NRSV: Immediately her hemorrhage stopped; and she felt in her body that she was healed of her disease.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, the fountain of her blood was dried up, and she knew, in her body, that she was healed from the plague.
text.KJV: 5:29 And straightway the fountaine of her blood was dried vp: and she felt in her body that she was healed of that plague.
text.DRV: 5:29 And forthwith the fountain of her blood was dried; and she felt in her body that she was healed of the malady.

text.NRSV: Immediately aware that power had gone forth from him, Jesus turned about in the crowd and said, “Who touched my clothes?”
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, Jesus, taking note in himself of the power which, out of him, went forth, turning round in the crowd, was saying—Who hath touched my garments?
text.KJV: 5:30 And Iesus immediatly knowing in himselfe that vertue had gone out of him, turned him about in the preasse, and said, Who touched my clothes?
text.DRV: 5:30 And immediately Jesus knowing in himself the virtue that had proceeded from him, turning to the multitude, said: Who hath touched my garments?

text.NRSV: And his disciples said to him, “You see the crowd pressing in on you; how can you say, ‘Who touched me?’”
text.Rotherham: and his disciples were saying to him—Thou seest the crowd pressing upon thee,—and sayest thou—Who hath touched me?
text.KJV: 5:31 And his disciples said vnto him, Thou seest the multitude thronging thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
text.DRV: 5:31 And his Disciples said to him: Thou seest the multitude thronging thee, & sayest thou, who hath touched me?

text.NRSV: He looked around to see who had done it.
text.Rotherham: and he was looking round to see her who, this thing had done.
text.KJV: 5:32 And he looked round about to see her that had done this thing.
text.DRV: 5:32 And he looked about to see her that had done this.

text.NRSV: But the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came in fear and trembling, fell down before him, and told him the whole truth.
text.Rotherham: And, the woman, overcome with fear, and trembling, knowing what had happened to her, came, and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.
text.KJV: 5:33 But the woman fearing and trembling, knowing what was done in her, came and fell downe before him, and tolde him all the trueth.
text.DRV: 5:33 But the woman fearing and trembling, knowing what was done in her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.

text.NRSV: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace, and be healed of your disease.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said to her—Daughter! thy faith hath made thee well; withdraw into peace, and be whole from thy plague.
text.KJV: 5:34 And he said vnto her, Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole, goe in peace, and be whole of thy plague.
text.DRV: 5:34 And he said to her: Daughter, thy faith hath made thee safe, go in peace, and be whole of thy malady.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking, some people came from the leader’s house to say, “Your daughter is dead. Why trouble the teacher any further?”
text.Rotherham: While yet he is speaking, they come from the synagogue-ruler’s, saying—Thy daughter, is dead! Why, further, annoy the teacher?
text.KJV: 5:35 While hee yet spake, there came from the Ruler of the Synagogues house, certaine which said, Thy daughter is dead, why troublest thou the Master any further?
text.DRV: 5:35 As he was yet speaking, they come to the Archsynagogue, saying: That thy daughter is dead: why doest thou trouble the Master any further?

text.NRSV: But overhearing what they said, Jesus said to the leader of the synagogue, “Do not be afraid, only believe.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, overhearing the word being spoken, saith unto the synagogue-ruler—Do not fear, only have faith!
text.KJV: 5:36 Assoone as Iesus heard the word that was spoken, he saith vnto the Ruler of the Synagogue, Be not afraid, onely beleeue.
text.DRV: 5:36 But Jesus having heard the word that was spoken, saith to the Archsynagogue: Fear not, only believe.

text.NRSV: He allowed no one to follow him except Peter, James, and John, the brother of James.
text.Rotherham: And he suffered no one to follow with him, save Peter and James and John the brother of James.
text.KJV: 5:37 And he suffered no man to follow him, saue Peter, & Iames, and Iohn the brother of Iames.
text.DRV: 5:37 And he admitted not any man to follow him, but Peter & James and John the brother of James.

text.NRSV: When they came to the house of the leader of the synagogue, he saw a commotion, people weeping and wailing loudly.
text.Rotherham: And they come into the house of the synagogue-ruler, and he observeth a tumult, and persons weeping and wailing greatly;
text.KJV: 5:38 And hee commeth to the house of the Ruler of the Synagogue, and seeth the tumult, and them that wept and wailed greatly.
text.DRV: 5:38 And they come to the Archsynagogue's house, and he seeth a tumult, and folk weeping and wailing much.

text.NRSV: When he had entered, he said to them, “Why do you make a commotion and weep? The child is not dead but sleeping.”
text.Rotherham: and, entering, he saith unto them—Why are ye making a tumult, and weeping? The child, is, not dead, but, is sleeping;
text.KJV: 5:39 And when he was come in, hee saith vnto them, Why make yee this adoe, and weepe? the damosell is not dead, but sleepeth.
text.DRV: 5:39 And going in, he saith to them: Why make you this ado and weep? the wench is not dead, but sleepeth.

text.NRSV: And they laughed at him. Then he put them all outside, and took the child’s father and mother and those who were with him, and went in where the child was.
text.Rotherham: and they were deriding him. But, he, thrusting all forth, taketh with him the father of the child, and the mother, and them who were with him,—and entereth where the child was;
text.KJV: 5:40 And they laughed him to scorne: but when he had put them all out, hee taketh the father and the mother of the damosell, and them that were with him, and entreth in where the damosell was lying.
text.DRV: 5:40 And they derided him. But he having put forth all, taketh the father and the mother of the wench, and them that were with him, and they go in where the wench was lying.

text.NRSV: He took her by the hand and said to her, “Talitha cum,” which means, “Little girl, get up!”
text.Rotherham: and, grasping the hand of the child, he saith unto her—Talitha, koum! which is, when translated—O damsel! to thee, I say, Arise!
text.KJV: 5:41 And he tooke the damosell by the hand, and said vnto her, Talitha cumi, which is, being interpreted, Damosell (I say vnto thee) Arise.
text.DRV: 5:41 And holding the wench's hand, he saith to her: Talitha cumi, which is being interpreted, wench (I say to thee) arise.

text.NRSV: And immediately the girl got up and began to walk about (she was twelve years of age). At this they were overcome with amazement.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, the damsel arose, and was walking about; for she was twelve years of age. And they were beside themselves, straightway, with a great transport;
text.KJV: 5:42 And straightway the damosell arose, and walked, for shee was of the age of twelue yeeres: and they were astonished with a great astonishment.
text.DRV: 5:42 And forthwith the wench rose up, and walked, and she was twelve years old: and they were astonished with great astonishment.

text.NRSV: He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and told them to give her something to eat.
text.Rotherham: and he commanded them, again and again, that, no one, should get to know this thing,—and bade that food should be given her to eat.
text.KJV: 5:43 And hee charged them straitly, that no man should know it: and commanded that some thing should be giuen her to eate.
text.DRV: 5:43 And he commanded them earnestly that nobody should know it: and he bade that something should be given her to eat.

text.NRSV: He left that place and came to his hometown, and his disciples followed him.
text.Rotherham: And he went forth from thence, and cometh into his own city,—and his disciples follow him.
text.KJV: 6:1 And hee went out from thence, and came into his owne countrey, and his disciples follow him.
text.DRV: 6:1 And going out from thence, he went into his country; and his Disciples followed him.

text.NRSV: On the sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were astounded. They said, “Where did this man get all this? What is this wisdom that has been given to him? What deeds of power are being done by his hands!
text.Rotherham: And, when it was Sabbath, he began to be teaching in the synagogue, and, the greater part, as they heard, were being struck with astonishment, saying—Whence hath this man these things?—and—What the wisdom which hath been given to this man?—and—Such mighty works as these, through his hands, are coming to pass!
text.KJV: 6:2 And when the Sabbath day was come, he began to teach in the Synagogue: and many hearing him, were astonished, saying, From whence hath this man these things? And what wisedome is this which is giuen vnto him, that euen such mightie workes are wrought by his hands?
text.DRV: 6:2 And when the Sabboth was come, he began to teach in the Synagogue: and many hearing him were in admiration at his doctrine, saying: How came this fellow by all these things? & what wisdom is this that is given to him, and such virtues as are wrought by his hands?

text.NRSV: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary and brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon, and are not his sisters here with us?” And they took offense at him.
text.Rotherham: Is not, this, the carpenter? the son of Mary, the brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon? And are not his sisters here with us?—and they were finding cause of stumbling in him.
text.KJV: 6:3 Is not this the carpenter, the sonne of Mary, the brother of Iames and Ioses, and of Iuda, and Simon? And are not his sisters heere with vs? And they were offended at him.
text.DRV: 6:3 Is not this the Carpenter, the son of Mary, the brother of James, and Joseph, and Jude, and Simon? Why, are not also his sisters here with us? And they were scandalized in him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Prophets are not without honor, except in their hometown, and among their own kin, and in their own house.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus was saying unto them—A prophet is not without honour, save in his own city and among his kinsfolk, and in his house;
text.KJV: 6:4 But Iesus sayde vnto them, A Prophet is not without honour, but in his owne countrey, and among his owne kinne, and in his owne house.
text.DRV: 6:4 And Jesus said to them: That there is not a Prophet without honour, but in his own country, and in his own house, and in his own kindred.

text.NRSV: And he could do no deed of power there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people and cured them.
text.Rotherham: and he could not, there, do so much as a single mighty work,—save, on a few sick, he laid his hands and cured them.
text.KJV: 6:5 And he could there doe no mightie worke, saue that he laid his hands vpon a few sicke folke, and healed them.
text.DRV: 6:5 And he could not do any miracle there, but only cured a few that were sick, imposing his hands.

text.NRSV: And he was amazed at their unbelief. Then he went about among the villages teaching.
text.Rotherham: And he marvelled because of their unbelief. And he was going round the villages in a circuit, teaching.
text.KJV: 6:6 And he marueiled because of their vnbeliefe. And he went round about the villages, teaching.
text.DRV: 6:6 And he marveled because of their incredulity, and he went about the towns in circuit teaching.

text.NRSV: He called the twelve and began to send them out two by two, and gave them authority over the unclean spirits.
text.Rotherham: And he calleth near the twelve, and began to be sending them forth two and two,—and was giving them authority over the impure spirits:
text.KJV: 6:7 ¶ And he calleth vnto him the twelue, and began to send them foorth, by two and two, and gaue them power ouer vncleane spirits,
text.DRV: 6:7 And he called the Twelve; and began to send them two and two, and gave them power over unclean spirits.

text.NRSV: He ordered them to take nothing for their journey except a staff; no bread, no bag, no money in their belts;
text.Rotherham: and charged them that they should take, nothing, for a journey, save a staff only,—no bread, no satchel, no copper, for the belt;
text.KJV: 6:8 And commanded them that they should take nothing for their iourney, saue a staffe onely: no scrip, no bread, no money in their purse:
text.DRV: 6:8 And he commanded them that they should take nothing for the way, but a rod only: not scrip, not bread, nor money in their purse,

text.NRSV: but to wear sandals and not to put on two tunics.
text.Rotherham: but having bound on light sandals, and not to put on, two tunics;
text.KJV: 6:9 But be shod with sandales: and not put on two coats.
text.DRV: 6:9 but shod with sandals, and that they should not put on two coats.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Wherever you enter a house, stay there until you leave the place.
text.Rotherham: and he was saying unto them—Wheresoever ye shall enter into a house, there, abide, until ye go forth from thence;
text.KJV: 6:10 And he said vnto them, In what place soeuer yee enter into an house, there abide til ye depart from that place.
text.DRV: 6:10 And he said to them: Withersoever you shall enter into an house, there tarry till you depart thence.

text.NRSV: If any place will not welcome you and they refuse to hear you, as you leave, shake off the dust that is on your feet as a testimony against them.”
text.Rotherham: And, whatsoever place shall not welcome you nor hearken unto you, when ye are going forth from thence, shake off the dust that is under you feet, for a witness against them.
text.KJV: 6:11 And whosoeuer shall not receiue you, nor heare you, when yee depart thence, shake off the dust vnder your feet, for a testimonie against them: Uerely I say vnto you, it shalbe more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of iudgement, then for that citie.
text.DRV: 6:11 And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you; going forth from thence shake off the dust from your feet for a testimony to them.

text.NRSV: So they went out and proclaimed that all should repent.
text.Rotherham: And they went forth and made proclamation, in order that men should repent;
text.KJV: 6:12 And they went out, and preached that men should repent.
text.DRV: 6:12 And going forth they preached that they should do penance:

text.NRSV: They cast out many demons, and anointed with oil many who were sick and cured them.
text.Rotherham: and, many demons, were they casting out,—and were anointing with oil, many sick, and were curing them.
text.KJV: 6:13 And they cast out many deuils, and anointed with oyle many, that were sicke, and healed them.
text.DRV: 6:13 And they cast out many devils, and anointed with oil many sick, and healed them.

text.NRSV: King Herod heard of it, for Jesus’ name had become known. Some were saying, “John the baptizer has been raised from the dead; and for this reason these powers are at work in him.”
text.Rotherham: And King Herod heard,—for, famous, had become his name; and he was saying—John the Immerser hath arisen from among the dead, and, for this cause, are the powers working mightily in him;
text.KJV: 6:14 And king Herod heard of him (for his name was spread abroad:) and hee said that Iohn the Baptist was risen from the dead, and therefore mightie workes doe shew foorth themselues in him.
text.DRV: 6:14 And king Herod heard (for his name was made manifest) and he said: That John the Baptist is risen again from the dead, and therefore virtues work in him.

text.NRSV: But others said, “It is Elijah.” And others said, “It is a prophet, like one of the prophets of old.”
text.Rotherham: But, others, were saying—It is Elijah, and, others were saying—A prophet, like one of the prophets!
text.KJV: 6:15 Others said, That it is Elias. And others said, That it is a Prophet, or as one of the Prophets.
text.DRV: 6:15 And others said: That it is Elias. But others said: That it is a Prophet, as one of the Prophets.

text.NRSV: But when Herod heard of it, he said, “John, whom I beheaded, has been raised.”
text.Rotherham: But, when Herod heard, he was saying—He whom I beheaded—John, the same, hath been raised.
text.KJV: 6:16 But when Herod heard thereof, he said, It is Iohn, whome I beheaded, he is risen from the dead.
text.DRV: 6:16 Which Herod hearing, said: John whom I beheaded, he is risen again from the dead.

text.NRSV: For Herod himself had sent men who arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, because Herod had married her.
text.Rotherham: For, Herod himself, had sent and secured John and bound him in prison, for the sake of Herodias the wife of Philip his brother,—for, her, had he married,
text.KJV: 6:17 For Herod himselfe had sent forth and laid hold vpon Iohn, and bound him in prison for Herodias sake, his brother Philips wife, for hee had maried her.
text.DRV: 6:17 For the said Herod sent and apprehended John, and bound him in prison for Herodias the wife of Philippe his brother, because he had married her.

text.NRSV: For John had been telling Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”
text.Rotherham: For John had been saying to Herod—It is not allowed thee, to have, the wife of thy brother.
text.KJV: 6:18 For Iohn had said vnto Herod, It is not lawfull for thee to haue thy brothers wife.
text.DRV: 6:18 For John said to Herod: It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother's wife,

text.NRSV: And Herodias had a grudge against him, and wanted to kill him. But she could not,
text.Rotherham: And, Herodias, was cherishing [a grudge] against him, and wishing, to slay him,—and could not;
text.KJV: 6:19 Therfore Herodias had a quarrel against him, & would haue killed him, but she could not.
text.DRV: 6:19 And Herodias lay in wait for him: and was desirous to kill him, and could not.

text.NRSV: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and he protected him. When he heard him, he was greatly perplexed; and yet he liked to listen to him.
text.Rotherham: for, Herod, stood in fear of John, knowing him to be a man righteous and holy,—and was keeping him safe; and, when he heard him, he paid earnest heed, and, with pleasure, used to listen to him.
text.KJV: 6:20 For Herod feared Iohn, knowing that he was a iust man, and an holy, and obserued him: and when he heard him, hee did many things, and heard him gladly.
text.DRV: 6:20 For Herod feared John, knowing him to be a just and holy man: and he kept him, and by hearing him did many things: and he heard him gladly.

text.NRSV: But an opportunity came when Herod on his birthday gave a banquet for his courtiers and officers and for the leaders of Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And, an opportune day arriving, when Herod on his birthday made a feast for his nobles, and for the rulers of thousands and for the first men of Galilee,
text.KJV: 6:21 And when a conuenient day was come, that Herod on his birth day made a supper to his lords, high captaines, and chiefe estates of Galilee:
text.DRV: 6:21 And when a convenient day was fallen, Herod made the supper of his birth-day to the Princes, and the Tribunes, and the chief of Galilee.

text.NRSV: When his daughter Herodias came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his guests; and the king said to the girl, “Ask me for whatever you wish, and I will give it.”
text.Rotherham: when the daughter of this very Herodias came in and danced, she pleased Herod and those reclining together, and, the king, said unto the damsel—Ask me what thou wilt, and I will give it thee;
text.KJV: 6:22 And when the daughter of the said Herodias came in, and danced, and pleased Herod, and them that sate with him, the king said vnto the damosell, Aske of me whatsoeuer thou wilt, and I will giue it thee.
text.DRV: 6:22 And when the daughter of the same Herodias came in, and had danced, and pleased Herod, and them that sat with him at the table, the king said to the damsel: Ask of me what thou wilt, and I will give it thee.

text.NRSV: And he solemnly swore to her, “Whatever you ask me, I will give you, even half of my kingdom.”
text.Rotherham: and he took an oath to her—Whatsoever thou shalt ask me, I will give thee, unto half my kingdom.
text.KJV: 6:23 And he sware vnto her, Whatsoeuer thou shalt aske of me, I will giue it thee, vnto the halfe of my kingdome.
text.DRV: 6:23 And he sware to her: That whatsoever thou shalt ask I will give thee, though the half of my kingdom.

text.NRSV: She went out and said to her mother, “What should I ask for?” She replied, “The head of John the baptizer.”
text.Rotherham: And, going out, she said unto her mother—What shall I ask? and she said—The head of John the Immerser;
text.KJV: 6:24 And she went forth, and said vnto her mother, What shall I aske? And she said, The head of Iohn þ Baptist.
text.DRV: 6:24 Who when she was gone forth, said to her mother, what shall I ask? But she said: The head of John the Baptist.

text.NRSV: Immediately she rushed back to the king and requested, “I want you to give me at once the head of John the Baptist on a platter.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming in straightway, with hast, unto the king, she asked, saying—I desire, that, forthwith, thou give me, upon a charger, the head of John the Immerser.
text.KJV: 6:25 And she came in straightway with haste, vnto the king, and asked, saying, I will that thou giue me by and by in a charger, the head of Iohn the Baptist.
text.DRV: 6:25 And when she was gone in by and by with haste to the king, she asketh saying: I will that forthwith thou give me in a platter the head of John the Baptist.

text.NRSV: The king was deeply grieved; yet out of regard for his oaths and for the guests, he did not want to refuse her.
text.Rotherham: And, though the king was, very grieved, yet, by reason of the oaths, and of them who were reclining, he would not refuse her.
text.KJV: 6:26 And the king was exceeding sory, yet for his othes sake, and for their sakes which sate with him, hee would not reiect her.
text.DRV: 6:26 And the king was stroken sad. Because of his oath and for them that sat together at table he would not displease her:

text.NRSV: Immediately the king sent a soldier of the guard with orders to bring John’s head. He went and beheaded him in the prison,
text.Rotherham: And the king, straightway, sending off a guard, gave orders to bring his head.
text.KJV: 6:27 And immediatly the king sent an executioner, and commaunded his head to be brought, and he went, and beheaded him in the prison,
text.DRV: 6:27 but sending the hangman, commanded that his head should be brought in a platter.

text.NRSV: brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the girl. Then the girl gave it to her mother.
text.Rotherham: And, departing, he beheaded him in the prison, and brought his head upon a charger, and gave it unto the damsel—and, the damsel, gave it unto her mother.
text.KJV: 6:28 And brought his head in a charger, and gaue it to the damosell, and the damosell gaue it to her mother.
text.DRV: 6:28 And he beheaded him in the prison, and brought his head in a platter: and gave it to the damsel, and the damsel gave it to her mother.

text.NRSV: When his disciples heard about it, they came and took his body, and laid it in a tomb.
text.Rotherham: And, hearing of it, his disciples went and took away his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
text.KJV: 6:29 And when his disciples heard of it, they came and tooke vp his corpse, and laid it in a tombe.
text.DRV: 6:29 Which his Disciples hearing came, and took his body; and they put it in a monument.

text.NRSV: The apostles gathered around Jesus, and told him all that they had done and taught.
text.Rotherham: And the apostles gather themselves together unto Jesus, and reported unto him all things, as many as they had done, and as many as they had taught.
text.KJV: 6:30 And the Apostles gathered themselues together vnto Iesus, and tolde him all things, both what they had done, and what they had taught.
text.DRV: 6:30 And the Apostles gathering together unto Jesus, made relation to him of all things that they had done and taught.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Come away to a deserted place all by yourselves and rest a while.” For many were coming and going, and they had no leisure even to eat.
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Come, ye yourselves, apart, into a desert place, and rest yourselves a little. For they who were coming and they who were going were many, and, not even to eat, were they finding fitting opportunity.
text.KJV: 6:31 And he said vnto them, Come yee your selues apart into a desert place, and rest a while. For there were many comming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eate.
text.DRV: 6:31 And he said to them: Come apart into the desert place, and rest a little. For there were that came and went, many: and they had not so much as space to eat.

text.NRSV: And they went away in the boat to a deserted place by themselves.
text.Rotherham: And they departed, in the boat, into a desert place, apart.
text.KJV: 6:32 And they departed into a desert place by ship priuately.
text.DRV: 6:32 And going up into the boat, they went into a desert place apart.

text.NRSV: Now many saw them going and recognized them, and they hurried there on foot from all the towns and arrived ahead of them.
text.Rotherham: And many saw them going away, and took note of it, and, afoot, from all the cities, ran they together thither,—and outwent them.
text.KJV: 6:33 And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ranne afoote thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together vnto him.
text.DRV: 6:33 And they saw them going away, and many knew: and they ran flocking thither on foot from all cities, and prevented them.

text.NRSV: As he went ashore, he saw a great crowd; and he had compassion for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd; and he began to teach them many things.
text.Rotherham: And, coming forth, he saw, a great multitude, and was moved with compassion towards them, because they were like sheep having no shepherd, and he began to be teaching them many things.
text.KJV: 6:34 And Iesus when he came out, saw much people, and was moued with compassion toward them, because they were as sheepe not hauing a shepherd: and hee beganne to teach them many things.
text.DRV: 6:34 And going forth, Jesus saw a great multitude; and he had compassion on them, because they were as sheep not having a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.

text.NRSV: When it grew late, his disciples came to him and said, “This is a deserted place, and the hour is now very late;
text.Rotherham: And, already, a late hour, having arrived, his disciples came unto him and were saying, A desert, is the place, and it is, already, a late hour:
text.KJV: 6:35 And when the day was now far spent, his Disciples came vnto him, and said, This is a desert place, and now the time is farre passed.
text.DRV: 6:35 And when the day was now far spent, his Disciples came to him, saying: This is a desert place, and the hour is now past:

text.NRSV: send them away so that they may go into the surrounding country and villages and buy something for themselves to eat.”
text.Rotherham: dismiss them, that, departing into the surrounding hamlets and villages, they may buy themselves something to eat.
text.KJV: 6:36 Send them away, that they may goe into the countrey round about, and into the villages, and buy themselues bread: for they haue nothing to eate.
text.DRV: 6:36 dismiss them, that going out into the next villages and towns, they may buy themselves meats to eat.

text.NRSV: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They said to him, “Are we to go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread, and give it to them to eat?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering said to them—Ye, give them, to eat. And they say unto him—Shall we depart and buy two hundred denaries’ worth of loaves, and give them to eat?
text.KJV: 6:37 He answered and said vnto them, Giue yee them to eate. And they say vnto him, Shall we goe and buy two hundred penniworth of bread, and giue them to eate?
text.DRV: 6:37 And he answering said: Give ye them to eat. And they said to him: Let us go and buy bread for two hundred pence, and we will give them to eat.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “How many loaves have you? Go and see.” When they had found out, they said, “Five, and two fish.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, saith unto them—How many loaves, have ye? Go, see! And, getting to know, they say—Five, and, two fishes.
text.KJV: 6:38 He saith vnto them, How many loaues haue yee? goe, and see. And when they knew, they say, Fiue, and two fishes.
text.DRV: 6:38 And he saith to them: How many loaves have you? Go and see. And when they knew, they say: Five, and two fishes.

text.NRSV: Then he ordered them to get all the people to sit down in groups on the green grass.
text.Rotherham: And he gave them orders, that all should be made recline, in parties, upon, the green grass.
text.KJV: 6:39 And he commanded them to make all sit downe by companies vpon the greene grasse.
text.DRV: 6:39 And he commanded them that they should make all sit down, by companies upon the green grass.

text.NRSV: So they sat down in groups of hundreds and of fifties.
text.Rotherham: And they fell back, in companies, by hundreds and by fifties.
text.KJV: 6:40 And they sate downe in rankes by hundreds, and by fifties.
text.DRV: 6:40 And they sat down in ranks by hundreds and fifties.

text.NRSV: Taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven, and blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people; and he divided the two fish among them all.
text.Rotherham: And, taking the five loaves, and the two fishes, looking up into heaven, he blessed, and brake up the loaves, and began giving unto the disciples, that they might set before them, and, the two fishes, divided he, unto all.
text.KJV: 6:41 And when he had taken the fiue loaues, and the two fishes, he looked vp to heauen, and blessed, and brake the loaues, and gaue them to his disciples to set before them; and the two fishes diuided he among them all.
text.DRV: 6:41 And when he had taken the five loaves, and the two fishes: looking up unto Heaven, he blessed, and brake the loaves, and gave to his Disciples to set before them: and the two fishes he divided to all.

text.NRSV: And all ate and were filled;
text.Rotherham: And they did all eat, and were filled.
text.KJV: 6:42 And they did all eate, and were filled.
text.DRV: 6:42 And all did eat, and had their fill.

text.NRSV: and they took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and of the fish.
text.Rotherham: And they took up broken pieces, twelve baskets, full measure, and from the fishes.
text.KJV: 6:43 And they tooke vp twelue baskets full of the fragments, and of the fishes.
text.DRV: 6:43 And they took up the leavings, twelve full baskets of fragments, and of the fishes.

text.NRSV: Those who had eaten the loaves numbered five thousand men.
text.Rotherham: And they who did eat the loaves were, five thousand men.
text.KJV: 6:44 And they that did eate of the loaues, were about fiue thousand men.
text.DRV: 6:44 And they that did eat, were five thousand men.

text.NRSV: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat and go on ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, constrained he his disciples to enter into the boat, and be going forward to the other side, unto Bethsaida,—while, he, was dismissing the multitude.
text.KJV: 6:45 And straightway he constrained his disciples to get into the ship, and to goe to the other side before vnto Bethsaida, while he sent away the people.
text.DRV: 6:45 And immediately he compelled his Disciples to go up into the boat, that they might go before him beyond the strait to Bethsaida: whiles himself did dismiss the people.

text.NRSV: After saying farewell to them, he went up on the mountain to pray.
text.Rotherham: And, bidding them farewell, he departed into the mountain to pray.
text.KJV: 6:46 And when hee had sent them away, he departed into a mountaine to pray.
text.DRV: 6:46 And when he had dismissed them, he went into the mountain to pray.

text.NRSV: When evening came, the boat was out on the sea, and he was alone on the land.
text.Rotherham: And, when, evening, came, the boat was in the midst of the sea, and, he alone, on the land.
text.KJV: 6:47 And when Euen was come, the ship was in the midst of the Sea, and he alone on the land.
text.DRV: 6:47 And when it was late, the boat was in the midst of the sea, and himself alone on the land.

text.NRSV: When he saw that they were straining at the oars against an adverse wind, he came towards them early in the morning, walking on the sea. He intended to pass them by.
text.Rotherham: And, seeing them distressed in the rowing, for the wind was against them, about the fourth watch of the night, he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea,—and was wishing to pass by them.
text.KJV: 6:48 And he saw them toiling in rowing (for the wind was contrary vnto them:) and about the fourth watch of the night, he commeth vnto them, walking vpon the Sea, and would haue passed by them.
text.DRV: 6:48 And seeing them labouring in rowing (for the wind was against them) and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh to them walking upon the sea, and he would have passed by them.

text.NRSV: But when they saw him walking on the sea, they thought it was a ghost and cried out;
text.Rotherham: But, they, seeing him, upon the sea, walking, supposed that it was an apparition, and cried out aloud;
text.KJV: 6:49 But when they saw him walking vpon the Sea, they supposed it had bene a spirit, and cried out.
text.DRV: 6:49 But they seeing him walking upon the sea, thought it was a ghost, and cried out.

text.NRSV: for they all saw him and were terrified. But immediately he spoke to them and said, “Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid.”
text.Rotherham: for, all, saw him, and were troubled. But, he, straightway, talked with them, and saith unto them—Take courage! it is, I—be not afraid!
text.KJV: 6:50 (For they all saw him, and were troubled) and immediately hee talked with them, and saith vnto them, Be of good cheere, It is I, be not afraid.
text.DRV: 6:50 For all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and said to them: Have confidence, it is I, fear ye not.

text.NRSV: Then he got into the boat with them and the wind ceased. And they were utterly astounded,
text.Rotherham: And he went up unto them, into the boat,—and the wind lulled. And, exceedingly, within themselves, were they astonished;
text.KJV: 6:51 And hee went vp vnto them into the ship, and the wind ceased: and they were sore amazed in themselues beyond measure, and wondered.
text.DRV: 6:51 And he went up to them into the ship, and the wind ceased: and they were far more astonished within themselves:

text.NRSV: for they did not understand about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.
text.Rotherham: for they understood not by the loaves,—but their, heart, had been, hardened.
text.KJV: 6:52 For they considered not the miracle of the loaues, for their heart was hardened.
text.DRV: 6:52 for they understood not concerning the loaves; for their heart was blinded.

text.NRSV: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret and moored the boat.
text.Rotherham: And crossing over unto the land, they came unto Gennesaret, and cast anchor near.
text.KJV: 6:53 And when they had passed ouer, they came into the land of Genesareth, and drew to the shore.
text.DRV: 6:53 And when they had passed over, they came into the land of Genesareth, and set to the shore.

text.NRSV: When they got out of the boat, people at once recognized him,
text.Rotherham: And, when they had, come forth, out of the boat, straightway, recognizing him,
text.KJV: 6:54 And when they were come out of the ship, straightway they knew him,
text.DRV: 6:54 And when they were gone out of the boat, incontinent they knew him:

text.NRSV: and hurried about that whole region and began to bring the sick on mats to wherever they heard he was.
text.Rotherham: the people ran round the whole of that country, and began to be carrying round, upon couches, them who were sick, wherever they heard that he was.
text.KJV: 6:55 And ran through that whole region round about, and beganne to carrie about in beds, those that were sicke, where they heard he was.
text.DRV: 6:55 and running through that whole country they began to carry about in couches those that were ill at ease, where they heard he was.

text.NRSV: And wherever he went, into villages or cities or farms, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might touch even the fringe of his cloak; and all who touched it were healed.
text.Rotherham: And, wheresoever he was entering into villages, or into cities, or into hamlets, in the marketplaces, laid they the sick, and were beseeching him, that, only the fringe of his mantle, they might touch, and, as many soever as touched it, were being made well.
text.KJV: 6:56 And whithersouer he entred, into villages, or cities, or countrie, they laide the sicke in the streetes, & besought him that they might touch if it were but the border of his garment: and as many as touched him, were made whole.
text.DRV: 6:56 And whithersoever he entered into towns or into villages or cities, they laid the sick in the streets, and besought him that they might touch but the hem of his garment: and as many as touched him, were made whole.

text.Base: Now when the Pharisees and some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around him,
text.KJV: 7:1 Then came together vnto him the Pharises, and certain of the Scribes, which came from Hierusalem.
text.DRV: 7:1 And there assemble together unto him the Pharisees and certain of the Scribes, coming from Jerusalem.

text.Base: they noticed that some of his disciples were eating with defiled hands, that is, without washing them.
text.KJV: 7:2 And when they saw some of his disciples eate bread with defiled (that is to say, with vnwashen) hands, they found fault.
text.DRV: 7:2 And when they had seen certain of his Disciples eat bread with common hands, that is, not washed, they blamed them.

text.Base: (For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, do not eat unless they give their hands a ceremonial washing, keeping the tradition of the elders;
text.KJV: 7:3 For the Pharises and all the Iewes, except they wash their hands oft, eate not, holding the tradition of the elders.
text.DRV: 7:3 For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, unless they often wash their hands, eat not, holding the tradition of the Ancients:

text.Base: and they do not eat anything from the market unless they wash it; and there are also many other traditions that they observe, the washing of cups, pots, and bronze kettles.)
text.KJV: 7:4 And when they come from the market, except they wash, they eate not. And many other things there be, which they haue receiued to hold, as the washing of cups and pots, brasen vessels, and of tables.
text.DRV: 7:4 And from the market, unless they be washed, they eat not: and many other things there be that were delivered unto them to observe, the washings of cups and cruses, and of brazen vessels & beds.

text.Base: So the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why do your disciples not live according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with defiled hands?”
text.KJV: 7:5 Then the Pharises and Scribes asked him, Why walke not thy disciples according to the tradition of the Elders, but eate bread with vnwashen hands?
text.DRV: 7:5 And the Pharisees and Scribes asked him: Why do not thy Disciples walk according to the tradition of the Ancients, but they eat bread with common hands?

text.Base: He said to them, “Isaiah prophesied rightly about you hypocrites, as it is written, ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me;
text.KJV: 7:6 He answered and said vnto them, Well hath Esaias prophesied of you Hypocrites, as it is written, This people honoureth mee with their lips, but their heart is farre from me.
text.DRV: 7:6 But he answering, said to them: Well did Esay Prophesy of you Hypocrites, as it is written: This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.

text.Base: in vain do they worship me, teaching human precepts as doctrines.’
text.KJV: 7:7 Howbeit in vaine doe they worship me, teaching for doctrines, the commandements of men.
text.DRV: 7:7 And in vain do they worship me, teaching doctrines precepts of men.

text.Base: You abandon the commandment of God and hold to human tradition.”
text.KJV: 7:8 For laying aside the Commandement of God, yee hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots, and cups: and many other such like things ye doe.
text.DRV: 7:8 For leaving the commandment of God, you hold the traditions of men, the washings of cruses and cups: & many other things you do like to these.

text.Base: Then he said to them, “You have a fine way of rejecting the commandment of God in order to keep your tradition!
text.KJV: 7:9 And he said vnto them, Full well ye reiect the Commandement of God, that ye may keepe your owne tradition.
text.DRV: 7:9 And he said to them, well do you frustrate the precept of God, that you may observe your own tradition.

text.Base: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother’; and, ‘Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must surely die.’
text.KJV: 7:10 For Moses said, Honour thy father & thy mother: and who so curseth father or mother, let him die the death.
text.DRV: 7:10 For Moyses said: Honour thy father and thy mother; and, He that shall curse father or mother, dying let him die.

text.Base: But you say that if anyone tells father or mother, ‘Whatever support you might have had from me is Corban’ (that is, an offering to God)—
text.KJV: 7:11 But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoeuer thou mightest be profited by me: he shalbe free.
text.DRV: 7:11 But you say: If a man say to father or mother, Corban (which is a gift) whatsoever proceedeth from me, shall profit thee:

text.Base: then you no longer permit doing anything for a father or mother,
text.KJV: 7:12 And ye suffer him no more to doe ought for his father, or his mother:
text.DRV: 7:12 And further you suffer him not to do ought for his father or mother,

text.Base: thus making void the word of God through your tradition that you have handed on. And you do many things like this.”
text.KJV: 7:13 Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye haue deliuered: And many such like things doe ye.
text.DRV: 7:13 defeating the word of God for your own tradition which you have given forth. And many other things of this sort you do.

text.Base: Then he called the crowd again and said to them, “Listen to me, all of you, and understand:
text.KJV: 7:14 ¶ And when he had called all the people vnto him, hee said vnto them, Hearken vnto me euery one of you, and vnderstand.
text.DRV: 7:14 And calling again the multitude unto him, he said to them: Hear me all you, and understand.

text.Base: there is nothing outside a person that by going in can defile, but the things that come out are what defile.”
text.KJV: 7:15 There is nothing from without a man that entring into him, can defile him: but the things which come out of of him, those are they that defile the man.
text.DRV: 7:15 Nothing is without a man entering into him, that can defile him. But the things that proceed from a man those are they that make a man common.

text.Base: When he had left the crowd and entered the house, his disciples asked him about the parable.
text.KJV: 7:17 And when hee was entred into the house from the people, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.
text.DRV: 7:17 And when he was entered into the house from the multitude, his Disciples asked him the parable.

text.Base: He said to them, “Then do you also fail to understand? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile,
text.KJV: 7:18 And he saith vnto them, Are ye so without vnderstanding also? Doe yee not perceiue that whatsoeuer thing from without entreth into the man, it cannot defile him,
text.DRV: 7:18 And he saith to them: So are you also unskillful? Understand you not that every thing from without, entering into a man, cannot make him common:

text.Base: since it enters, not the heart but the stomach, and goes out into the sewer?” (Thus he declared all foods clean.)
text.KJV: 7:19 Because it entreth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?
text.DRV: 7:19 because it entereth not into his heart, but goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the privy, purging all the meats?

text.Base: And he said, “It is what comes out of a person that defiles.
text.KJV: 7:20 And he said, That which commeth out of the man, that defileth the man.
text.DRV: 7:20 But he said that the things which come forth from a man, they make a man common.

text.Base: For it is from within, from the human heart, that evil intentions come: fornication, theft, murder,
text.KJV: 7:21 For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed euill thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,
text.DRV: 7:21 For from within out of the heart of men proceed evil cogitations, adulteries, fornications, murders,

text.Base: adultery, avarice, wickedness, deceit, licentiousness, envy, slander, pride, folly.
text.KJV: 7:22 Thefts, couetousnesse, wickednesse, deceit, lasciuiousnesse, an euill eye, blasphemie, pride, foolishnesse:
text.DRV: 7:22 thefts, avarices, wickedness, guile, impudicities, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness.

text.Base: All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person.”
text.KJV: 7:23 All these euill things come from within, and defile the man.
text.DRV: 7:23 All these evils proceed from within, and make a man common.

text.Base: From there he set out and went away to the region of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know he was there. Yet he could not escape notice,
text.KJV: 7:24 ¶ And from thence he arose, and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entred into an house, and would haue no man know it, but hee could not be hid.
text.DRV: 7:24 And rising from thence he went into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon: and entering into a house, he would that no man should know, and he could not be hid.

text.Base: but a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit immediately heard about him, and she came and bowed down at his feet.
text.KJV: 7:25 For a certaine woman, whose yong daughter had an vncleane spirit, heard of him, and came and fell at his feete.
text.DRV: 7:25 For a woman immediately as she heard of him, whose daughter had an unclean spirit, entered in, and fell down at his feet.

text.Base: Now the woman was a Gentile, of Syrophoenician origin. She begged him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
text.KJV: 7:26 (The woman was a Greek: a Syrophenician by nation:) and she besought him that he would cast forth the deuill out of her daughter.
text.DRV: 7:26 For the woman was a Gentile, a Syrophænician born. And she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.

text.Base: He said to her, “Let the children be fed first, for it is not fair to take the children’s food and throw it to the dogs.”
text.KJV: 7:27 But Iesus said vnto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the childrens bread, and to cast it vnto the dogges.
text.DRV: 7:27 Who said to her: Suffer first the children to be filled. For it is not good to take the children's bread, and cast it to the dogs.

text.Base: But she answered him, “Sir, even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
text.KJV: 7:28 And she answered and said vnto him, Yes Lord, yet the dogges vnder the table eat of the childrens crummes.
text.DRV: 7:28 But she answered, and said to him: Yea Lord; for the whelps also eat under the table of the crumbs of the children.

text.Base: Then he said to her, “For saying that, you may go—the demon has left your daughter.”
text.KJV: 7:29 And hee said vnto her, For this saying, goe thy way, the deuill is gone out of thy daughter.
text.DRV: 7:29 And he said to her: For this saying go thy way, the devil is gone out of thy daughter.

text.Base: So she went home, found the child lying on the bed, and the demon gone.
text.KJV: 7:30 And when shee was come to her house, she found the deuill gone out, and her daughter laied vpon the bed.
text.DRV: 7:30 And when she was departed into her house, she found the maid lying upon the bed, and the devil gone out.

text.Base: Then he returned from the region of Tyre, and went by way of Sidon towards the Sea of Galilee, in the region of the Decapolis.
text.KJV: 7:31 ¶ And againe departing from the coastes of Tyre and Sidon, he came vnto the sea of Galilee, thorow the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.
text.DRV: 7:31 And again going out of the coasts of Tyre, he came by Sidon to the sea of Galilee through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.

text.Base: They brought to him a deaf man who had an impediment in his speech; and they begged him to lay his hand on him.
text.KJV: 7:32 And they bring vnto him one that was deafe, and had an impediment in his speech: and they beseech him to put his hand vpon him.
text.DRV: 7:32 And they bring to him one deaf and dumb; and they besought him that he would impose his hand upon him.

text.Base: He took him aside in private, away from the crowd, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat and touched his tongue.
text.KJV: 7:33 And he tooke him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his eares, and he spit, and touched his tongue,
text.DRV: 7:33 And taking him from the multitude apart, he put his fingers into his ears, and spitting, touched his tongue;

text.Base: Then looking up to heaven, he sighed and said to him, “Ephphatha,” that is, “Be opened.”
text.KJV: 7:34 And looking vp to heauen, hee sighed, and saith vnto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.
text.DRV: 7:34 And looking up unto Heaven, he groaned, and said to him: Ephphetha, which is, Be thou opened.

text.Base: And immediately his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he spoke plainly.
text.KJV: 7:35 And straightway his eares were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake plaine.
text.DRV: 7:35 And immediately his ears were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake right.

text.Base: Then Jesus ordered them to tell no one; but the more he ordered them, the more zealously they proclaimed it.
text.KJV: 7:36 And hee charged them that they should tell no man: but the more hee charged them, so much the more a great deale they published it,
text.DRV: 7:36 And he commanded them not to tell anybody. But how much he commanded them, so much the more a great deal did they publish it.

text.Base: They were astounded beyond measure, saying, “He has done everything well; he even makes the deaf to hear and the mute to speak.”
text.KJV: 7:37 And were beyond measure astonished, saying, Hee hath done all things well: hee maketh both the deafe to heare, and the dumbe to speake.
text.DRV: 7:37 And so much the more did they wonder, saying: He hath done all things well; he hath made both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.

text.NRSV: In those days when there was again a great crowd without anything to eat, he called his disciples and said to them,
text.Rotherham: In those days, there being, again a large multitude, and they not having any thing to eat, calling near the disciples, he saith unto them—
text.KJV: 8:1 In those dayes the multitude being very great, and hauing nothing to eat. Iesus called his disciples vnto him, & saith vnto them,
text.DRV: 8:1 IN those days again when there was a great multitude, and had not what to eat: calling his Disciples together, he saith to them:

text.NRSV: “I have compassion for the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat.
text.Rotherham: I am moved with compassion towards the multitude, because, already three days, abide they with me, and they have nothing to eat;
text.KJV: 8:2 I haue compassion on the multitude, because they haue now bene with me three daies, and haue nothing to eat:
text.DRV: 8:2 I have compassion upon the multitude, because lo three days they now endure with me, neither have what to eat.

text.NRSV: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they will faint on the way—and some of them have come from a great distance.”
text.Rotherham: and, if dismiss them fasting unto their home, they will be exhausted in the way,—and, certain of them, are, from afar.
text.KJV: 8:3 And if I send them away fasting to their owne houses, they will faint by the way: for diuers of them came from farre.
text.DRV: 8:3 And if I dismiss them fasting into their home, they will faint in the way; for some of them came far off.

text.NRSV: His disciples replied, “How can one feed these people with bread here in the desert?”
text.Rotherham: And his disciples answered him—Whence shall any one be able, here, to fill, these, with bread, in a desert?
text.KJV: 8:4 And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfie these men with bread here in the wildernes?
text.DRV: 8:4 And his Disciples answered him: Whence may a man fill them here with bread in the wilderness?

text.NRSV: He asked them, “How many loaves have you?” They said, “Seven.”
text.Rotherham: And he was questioning them—How many loaves have ye? and they said—Seven.
text.KJV: 8:5 And hee asked them, How many loaues haue ye? And they said, Seuen.
text.DRV: 8:5 And he asked them: How many loaves have ye? Who said: Seven.

text.NRSV: Then he ordered the crowd to sit down on the ground; and he took the seven loaves, and after giving thanks he broke them and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they distributed them to the crowd.
text.Rotherham: And he sendeth word to the multitude, to fall back upon the ground. And, taking the seven loaves, giving thanks, he brake, and was giving unto his disciples, that they might be setting before them; and they set before the multitude.
text.KJV: 8:6 And he commanded the people to sit downe on the ground: and he tooke the seuen loaues, and gaue thanks, and brake, and gaue to his disciples to set before them: and they did set them before the people.
text.DRV: 8:6 And he commanded the multitude to sit down upon the ground. And taking the seven loaves, giving thanks he brake, and gave to his Disciples for to set before them, and they did set them before the multitude.

text.NRSV: They had also a few small fish; and after blessing them, he ordered that these too should be distributed.
text.Rotherham: And they had a few small fishes; and, blessing them, he bade them set, these also, before them.
text.KJV: 8:7 And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commaunded to set them also before them.
text.DRV: 8:7 And they had a few little fishes; and he blessed them, and commanded them to be set before them.

text.NRSV: They ate and were filled; and they took up the broken pieces left over, seven baskets full.
text.Rotherham: And they did eat, and were filled, and took up remainders of broken pieces, seven hampers.
text.KJV: 8:8 So they did eate, and were filled: and they tooke vp, of the broken meate that was left, seuen baskets.
text.DRV: 8:8 And they did eat and were filled, and they took up that which was left of the fragments, seven maunds.

text.NRSV: Now there were about four thousand people. And he sent them away.
text.Rotherham: Now they were about four thousand; and he dismissed them.
text.KJV: 8:9 And they that had eaten were about foure thousand, and he sent them away.
text.DRV: 8:9 And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he dismissed them.

text.NRSV: And immediately he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, entering into the boat with his disciples, he came into the parts of Dalmanutha;
text.KJV: 8:10 ¶ And straightway he entred into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.
text.DRV: 8:10 And immediately going up into the boat with his Disciples, he came into the quarters of Dalmanutha.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees came and began to argue with him, asking him for a sign from heaven, to test him.
text.Rotherham: and forth came the Pharisees, and began discussing with him, seeking of him, a sign from the heaven, testing him.
text.KJV: 8:11 And the Pharisees came foorth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a signe from heauen, tempting him.
text.DRV: 8:11 And the Pharisees went forth, and began to question with him, asking of him a sign from Heaven, tempting him.

text.NRSV: And he sighed deeply in his spirit and said, “Why does this generation ask for a sign? Truly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.”
text.Rotherham: And, deeply sighing in his spirit, he saith—Why, doth this generation seek, a sign? Verily, I say—there shall, not be given, to this generation, a sign.
text.KJV: 8:12 And he sighed deepely in his spirit, and saith, Why doeth this generation seeke after a signe? Uerely I say vnto you, There shall no signe be giuen vnto this generation.
text.DRV: 8:12 And groaning in spirit, he saith: Why doth this Generation ask a sign? Amen I say to you, If a sign shall be given to this generation.

text.NRSV: And he left them, and getting into the boat again, he went across to the other side.
text.Rotherham: And leaving them, again, embarking, he departed unto the other side.
text.KJV: 8:13 And he left them, & entring into the ship againe, departed to the other side.
text.DRV: 8:13 And leaving them, he went up again into the boat, and passed beyond the strait.

text.NRSV: Now the disciples had forgotten to bring any bread; and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text.Rotherham: And they forgot to take loaves, and, save one loaf, they had nothing with them in the boat.
text.KJV: 8:14 ¶ Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more then one loafe.
text.DRV: 8:14 And they forgot to take bread: and they had but one loaf with them in the boat.

text.NRSV: And he cautioned them, saying, “Watch out—beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod.”
text.Rotherham: And he began charging them, saying—Mind! beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod!
text.KJV: 8:15 And hee charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leauen of the Pharisees, and of the leauen of Herode.
text.DRV: 8:15 And he commanded them, saying: Look well and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and the leaven of Herod.

text.NRSV: They said to one another, “It is because we have no bread.”
text.Rotherham: And they began deliberating one with another, because they had no loaves.
text.KJV: 8:16 And they reasoned among themselues, saying, It is, because we haue no bread.
text.DRV: 8:16 And they reasoned among themselves saying: Because we have not bread.

text.NRSV: And becoming aware of it, Jesus said to them, “Why are you talking about having no bread? Do you still not perceive or understand? Are your hearts hardened?
text.Rotherham: And, taking note, he saith unto them—Why do ye deliberate, because ye have no, loaves? Not yet perceive ye, neither understand,—keep ye your hearts, hardened?
text.KJV: 8:17 And when Iesus knew it, he saith vnto them, Why reason ye, because yee haue no bread? Perceiue ye not yet, neither vnderstand? Haue yee your heart yet hardened?
text.DRV: 8:17 Which Jesus knowing, saith to them: Why do you reason, because you have not bread? do you not yet know nor understand? Yet have ye your heart blinded?

text.NRSV: Do you have eyes, and fail to see? Do you have ears, and fail to hear? And do you not remember?
text.Rotherham: Eyes having, see ye not? and ears having, hear ye not? and remember ye not
text.KJV: 8:18 Hauing eyes, see ye not? and hauing eares heare ye not? And doe ye not remember?
text.DRV: 8:18 having eyes see you not? and having ears hear you not? Neither do you remember?

text.NRSV: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you collect?” They said to him, “Twelve.”
text.Rotherham: when, the five loaves, I brake unto the five thousand, how many baskets, full of broken pieces, ye took up? They say unto him—Twelve,—
text.KJV: 8:19 When I brake the fiue loaues among fiue thousand, how many baskets full of fragments tooke yee vp? They say vnto him, Twelue.
text.DRV: 8:19 When I brake five loaves among five thousand; and how many baskets full of fragments took you up? They say to him, Twelve.

text.NRSV: “And the seven for the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you collect?” And they said to him, “Seven.”
text.Rotherham: When the seven, unto the four thousand, how many hampers, full measure, of broken pieces, ye took up? And they say unto him—Seven. And he was saying unto them—
text.KJV: 8:20 And when the seuen among foure thousand: how many baskets full of fragments tooke ye vp? And they said, Seuen.
text.DRV: 8:20 When also seven loaves among four thousand, how many maunds of fragments took you up? And they say to him, Seven.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Do you not yet understand?”
text.Rotherham: Not yet, do ye understand?
text.KJV: 8:21 And he said vnto them, How is it that ye doe not vnderstand?
text.DRV: 8:21 And he said to them: How do you not yet understand?

text.NRSV: They came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to him and begged him to touch him.
text.Rotherham: And they come into Bethsaida. And they bring unto him one blind, and beseech him that him, he would touch.
text.KJV: 8:22 ¶ And he commeth to Bethsaida, and they bring a blind man vnto him, and besought him to touch him:
text.DRV: 8:22 And they come to Bethsaida; and they bring to him one blind, and desired him that he would touch him.

text.NRSV: He took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village; and when he had put saliva on his eyes and laid his hands on him, he asked him, “Can you see anything?”
text.Rotherham: And, laying hold of the hand of the blind man, he brought him forth outside the village, and, spitting into his eyes, laying his hands upon him, he was asking him—Anything, seest thou?
text.KJV: 8:23 And he tooke the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the towne, and when he had spit on his eyes, & put his hands vpon him, he asked him, if hee saw ought.
text.DRV: 8:23 And taking the hand of the blind, he led him forth out of the town; and spitting into his eyes, imposing his hands, he asked him if he saw any thing.

text.NRSV: And the man looked up and said, “I can see people, but they look like trees, walking.”
text.Rotherham: and, looking up, he was saying—I see men, because, like trees, I behold them walking
text.KJV: 8:24 And he looked vp, and saide, I see men as trees, walking.
text.DRV: 8:24 And looking up, he said: I see men as it were trees, walking.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus laid his hands on his eyes again; and he looked intently and his sight was restored, and he saw everything clearly.
text.Rotherham: Then again, put he his hands upon his eyes, and he saw clearly, and was restored, and was seeing distinctly, in broad splendour, all things together.
text.KJV: 8:25 After that hee put his handes againe vpon his eies, and made him look vp: and he was restored, and saw euery man clearely.
text.DRV: 8:25 After that again he imposed his hands upon his eyes, and he began to see, and was restored, so that he saw all things clearly.

text.NRSV: Then he sent him away to his home, saying, “Do not even go into the village.”
text.Rotherham: And he sent him away unto his house, saying—Not even into the village, mayest thou enter.
text.KJV: 8:26 And hee sent him away to his house, saying, Neither goe into the towne, nor tell it to any in the towne.
text.DRV: 8:26 And he sent him into his house, saying: Go into thy house; and if thou enter into the town, tell nobody.

text.NRSV: Jesus went on with his disciples to the villages of Caesarea Philippi; and on the way he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that I am?”
text.Rotherham: And forth went Jesus and his disciples, into the villages of Caesarea of Philip. And, in the way, he was questioning his disciples, saying unto them—Who, are men saying that I am?
text.KJV: 8:27 ¶ And Iesus went out, and his disciples, into the townes of Cesarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying vnto them, Whom doe men say that I am?
text.DRV: 8:27 And Jesus went forth and his Disciples into the towns of Cæsarea-Philippi; and in the way he asked his Disciples, saying to them: Whom do men say that I am?

text.NRSV: And they answered him, “John the Baptist; and others, Elijah; and still others, one of the prophets.”
text.Rotherham: And they answered him, saying—John the Immerser, and, others, Elijah,—and, others, One of the prophets.
text.KJV: 8:28 And they answered, Iohn the Baptist: but some say, Elias: & others, one of the Prophets.
text.DRV: 8:28 Who answered him, saying: John the Baptist, some Elias, & other some as it were one of the Prophets.

text.NRSV: He asked them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered him, “You are the Messiah.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, went on to question them—But, who, say, ye, that I am? Peter, answering, saith unto him—Thou, art, the Christ.
text.KJV: 8:29 And hee saith vnto them, But whom say yee that I am? And Peter answereth and saith vnto him, Thou art the Christ.
text.DRV: 8:29 Then he saith to them: But whom do you say that I am? Peter answering said to him: Thou art Christ.

text.NRSV: And he sternly ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
text.Rotherham: And he straitly charged them, that, no one, they should tell concerning him.
text.KJV: 8:30 And he charged them that they should tell no man of him.
text.DRV: 8:30 And he threatened them that they should not tell any man of him.

text.NRSV: Then he began to teach them that the Son of Man must undergo great suffering, and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
text.Rotherham: And he began to be teaching them—The Son of Man, must needs suffer many things, and be rejected by the Elders and the High-priests and the Scribes,—and be slain; and, after three days, arise.
text.KJV: 8:31 And hee beganne to teach them, that the Sonne of man must suffer many things, and be reiected of the Elders, and of the chiefe Priests, & Scribes, and be killed, & after three dayes rise againe.
text.DRV: 8:31 And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the Ancients and of the high Priests & the Scribes, and be killed: and after three days rise again.

text.NRSV: He said all this quite openly. And Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
text.Rotherham: And, openly, was he speaking the word. And Peter, taking him aside, began to rebuke him.
text.KJV: 8:32 And he spake that saying openly. And Peter tooke him, and beganne to rebuke him.
text.DRV: 8:32 And he spake the word openly. And Peter taking him, began to rebuke him.

text.NRSV: But turning and looking at his disciples, he rebuked Peter and said, “Get behind me, Satan! For you are setting your mind not on divine things but on human things.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, turning round and looking upon his disciples, rebuked Peter, and saith—Withdraw behind me, Satan! because thou art not regarding the things, of God, but the things, of men.
text.KJV: 8:33 But when he had turned about, and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou sauourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.
text.DRV: 8:33 Who turning, and seeing his Disciples, threatened Peter, saying: Go behind me Satan, because thou savourest not the things that are of God, but that are of men.

text.NRSV: He called the crowd with his disciples, and said to them, “If any want to become my followers, let them deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me.
text.Rotherham: And, calling near the multitude with his disciples, he said unto them—If anyone willeth, after me, to come, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and be following me.
text.KJV: 8:34 ¶ And when he had called the people vnto him, with his disciples also, he said vnto them, Whosoeuer will come after me, let him denie himselfe, and take vp his crosse and follow mee.
text.DRV: 8:34 And calling the multitude together with his Disciples, he said to them: If any man will follow me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

text.NRSV: For those who want to save their life will lose it, and those who lose their life for my sake, and for the sake of the gospel, will find it.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever willeth, his own life, to save, shall lose it, but, whosoever shall lose his life for the sake [of me and] of the glad-message, shall save it;
text.KJV: 8:35 For whosoeuer will saue his life shall lose it, but whosoeuer shall lose his life for my sake and the Gospels, the same shall saue it.
text.DRV: 8:35 For he that will save his life, shall lose it; and he that shall lose his life for me and the Gospel, shall save it.

text.NRSV: For what will it profit them to gain the whole world and forfeit their life?
text.Rotherham: For, what doth it profit a man, to gain the whole world, and be made to forfeit his life?
text.KJV: 8:36 For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gaine the whole world, and lose his owne soule?
text.DRV: 8:36 For what shall it profit a man, if he gain the whole world, & suffer damage of his soul?
