text.NRSV: Indeed, what can they give in return for their life?
text.Rotherham: For what can a man, give, in exchange for his life?
text.KJV: 8:37 Or what shall a man giue in exchange for his soule?
text.DRV: 8:37 Or what permutation shall a man give for his soul?

text.NRSV: Those who are ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, of them the Son of Man will also be ashamed when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.”
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man also, will be ashamed, of him, whensoever he shall come, in the glory of his Father, with the holy messengers.
text.KJV: 8:38 Whosoeuer therefore shall be ashamed of me, and of my words, in this adulterous and sinfull generation, of him also shall the Sonne of man bee ashamed, when he commeth in the glory of his Father, with the holy Angels.
text.DRV: 8:38 For he that shall be ashamed of me, and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of man also will be ashamed of him, when he shall come in the glory of his Father with the holy Angels.

text.Base: And he said to them, “Truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see that the kingdom of God has come with power.”
text.KJV: 9:1 And hee said vnto them, Uerely I say vnto you, that there be some of them that stand here, which shal not taste of death, till they haue seene the kingdome of God come with power.
text.DRV: 9:1 And after six days Jesus taketh Peter and James and John, and bringeth them alone into a high mountain apart, and was transfigured before them.

text.Base: Six days later, Jesus took with him Peter and James and John, and led them up a high mountain apart, by themselves. And he was transfigured before them,
text.KJV: 9:2 ¶ And after sixe dayes, Iesus taketh with him Peter, and Iames, and Iohn, and leadeth them vp into an high mountaine apart by themselues: and he was transfigured before them.
text.DRV: 9:2 And his garments were made glistering and white exceedingly as snow, the like whereof a fuller cannot make white upon the earth.

text.Base: and his clothes became dazzling white, such as no one on earth could bleach them.
text.KJV: 9:3 And his raiment became shining, exceeding white as snow: so as no Fuller on earth can white them.
text.DRV: 9:3 And there appeared to them Elias with Moyses; and they were talking with Jesus.

text.Base: And there appeared to them Elijah with Moses, who were talking with Jesus.
text.KJV: 9:4 And there appeared vnto them Elias with Moses: and they were talking with Iesus.
text.DRV: 9:4 And Peter answering, said to Jesus: Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tabernacles, one for thee, and one for Moyses, and one for Elias.

text.Base: Then Peter said to Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; let us make three dwellings, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text.KJV: 9:5 And Peter answered, and saide to Iesus, Master, it is good for vs to bee here, and let vs make three Tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
text.DRV: 9:5 For he knew not what he said; for they were frighted with fear:

text.Base: He did not know what to say, for they were terrified.
text.KJV: 9:6 For he wist not what to say, for they were sore afraid.
text.DRV: 9:6 And there was a cloud overshadowing them, and a voice came out of the cloud, saying: This is my Son most dear: hear ye him.

text.Base: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and from the cloud there came a voice, “This is my Son, the Beloved; listen to him!”
text.KJV: 9:7 And there was a cloud that ouershadowed them: and a voyce came out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloued Sonne: heare him.
text.DRV: 9:7 And immediately looking about, they saw no man any more but Jesus only with them.

text.Base: Suddenly when they looked around, they saw no one with them any more, but only Jesus.
text.KJV: 9:8 And suddenly when they had looked round about, they saw no man any more, saue Iesus only with themselues.
text.DRV: 9:8 And as they descended from the mountain, he commanded them that they should not tell any man what things they had seen, but when the Son of man shall be risen again from the dead.

text.Base: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them to tell no one about what they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
text.KJV: 9:9 And as they came downe from the mountaine, he charged them that they should tell no man, what things they had seene, till the Sonne of man were risen from the dead.
text.DRV: 9:9 And they kept in the word with themselves; questioning together what that should be, when he shall be risen from the dead.

text.Base: So they kept the matter to themselves, questioning what this rising from the dead could mean.
text.KJV: 9:10 And they kept that saying with themselues, questioning one with another, what the rising from the dead should meane.
text.DRV: 9:10 And they asked him, saying: What say the Pharisees then and the Scribes, that Elias must come first?

text.Base: Then they asked him, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
text.KJV: 9:11 ¶ And they asked him, saying, Why say the Scribes that Elias must first come?
text.DRV: 9:11 Who answering said to them: Elias when he cometh first, shall restore all things: and how it is written of the Son of man, that he shall suffer many things and be contemned.

text.Base: He said to them, “Elijah is indeed coming first to restore all things. How then is it written about the Son of Man, that he is to go through many sufferings and be treated with contempt?
text.KJV: 9:12 And he answered, and told them, Elias verely commeth first, and restoreth al things, and how it is written of the Sonne of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.
text.DRV: 9:12 But I say to you that Elias also is come (and they have done to him whatsoever they would) as it is written of him.

text.Base: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they did to him whatever they pleased, as it is written of him.”
text.KJV: 9:13 But I say vnto you, that Elias is indeed come, and they haue done vnto him whatsoeuer they listed, as it is written of him.
text.DRV: 9:13 And coming to his Disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the Scribes questioning with them.

text.Base: When they came to the disciples, they saw a great crowd around them, and some scribes arguing with them.
text.KJV: 9:14 ¶ And when he came to his disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the Scribes questioning with them.
text.DRV: 9:14 And forthwith all the people seeing Jesus, was astonished, and much afraid: and running to him, saluted him.

text.Base: When the whole crowd saw him, they were immediately overcome with awe, and they ran forward to greet him.
text.KJV: 9:15 And straightway all the people, when they beheld him, were greatly amazed, & running to him, saluted him.
text.DRV: 9:15 And he asked them, what do you question of among you?

text.Base: He asked them, “What are you arguing about with them?”
text.KJV: 9:16 And he asked the Scribes, What question ye with them?
text.DRV: 9:16 And one of the multitude answering, said: Master, I have brought my son to thee, having a dumb spirit,

text.Base: Someone from the crowd answered him, “Teacher, I brought you my son; he has a spirit that makes him unable to speak;
text.KJV: 9:17 And one of the multitude answered, and said, Master, I haue broughe vnto thee my son, which hath a dumbe spirit:
text.DRV: 9:17 Who, wheresoever he taketh him, dasheth him, and he foameth, and gnasheth with the teeth, and withereth: and I spake to thy Disciples to cast him out, and they could not.

text.Base: and whenever it seizes him, it dashes him down; and he foams and grinds his teeth and becomes rigid; and I asked your disciples to cast it out, but they could not do so.”
text.KJV: 9:18 And wheresoeuer he taketh him, he teareth him, & he fometh, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples, that they should cast him out, and they could not.
text.DRV: 9:18 Who answering them, said: O incredulous generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? Bring him unto me.

text.Base: He answered them, “You faithless generation, how much longer must I be among you? How much longer must I put up with you? Bring him to me.”
text.KJV: 9:19 He answereth him, and saith, O faithlesse generation, how long shall I be with you, how long shall I suffer you? Bring him vnto me.
text.DRV: 9:19 And they brought him. And when he had seen him, immediately the spirit troubled him; and being thrown upon the ground, he tumbled foaming.

text.Base: And they brought the boy to him. When the spirit saw him, immediately it convulsed the boy, and he fell on the ground and rolled about, foaming at the mouth.
text.KJV: 9:20 And they brought him vnto him: and when he saw him, straightway the spirit tare him, and he fel on the ground, and wallowed, foming.
text.DRV: 9:20 And he asked his father: How long time is it since this hath chanced unto him? But he said: From his infancy:

text.Base: Jesus asked the father, “How long has this been happening to him?” And he said, “From childhood.
text.KJV: 9:21 And he asked his father, Howe long is it agoe since this came vnto him? And he said, Of a child.
text.DRV: 9:21 And oftentimes hath he cast him into fire and into waters, to destroy him. But if thou canst any thing, help us, having compassion on us.

text.Base: It has often cast him into the fire and into the water, to destroy him; but if you are able to do anything, have pity on us and help us.”
text.KJV: 9:22 And oft times it hath cast him into the fire, and into the waters to destroy him: but if thou canst doe any thing, haue compassion on vs, and helpe vs.
text.DRV: 9:22 And Jesus said to him: If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.

text.Base: Jesus said to him, “‘If you are able!’—All things can be done for the one who believes.”
text.KJV: 9:23 Iesus said vnto him, If thou canst beleeue, all things are possible to him that beleeueth.
text.DRV: 9:23 And incontinent the father of the boy crying out, with tears said: I do believe Lord; help my incredulity.

text.Base: Immediately the father of the child cried out, “I believe; help my unbelief!”
text.KJV: 9:24 And straightway the father of the child cried out and said with teares, Lord, I beleeue, helpe thou mine vnbeliefe.
text.DRV: 9:24 And when Jesus saw the people running together, he threatened the unclean spirit, saying to him: Deaf and dumb Spirit, I command thee, go out of him, and enter not any more into him.

text.Base: When Jesus saw that a crowd came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, “You spirit that keeps this boy from speaking and hearing, I command you, come out of him, and never enter him again!”
text.KJV: 9:25 When Iesus saw that the people came running together, he rebuked the foule spirit, saying vnto him, Thou dumbe and deafe spirit, I charge thee come out of him, and enter no more into him.
text.DRV: 9:25 And crying out; and greatly tearing him, he went out of him, and he became as dead, so that many said, that he is dead.

text.Base: After crying out and convulsing him terribly, it came out, and the boy was like a corpse, so that most of them said, “He is dead.”
text.KJV: 9:26 And the spirit cried, and rent him sore, and came out of him, and he was as one dead, insomuch that many said, He is dead.
text.DRV: 9:26 But Jesus holding his hand, lifted him up; and he rose.

text.Base: But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and he was able to stand.
text.KJV: 9:27 But Iesus tooke him by the hand, and lifted him vp, and he arose.
text.DRV: 9:27 And when he was entered into the house, his Disciples secretly asked him, why could not we cast him out?

text.Base: When he had entered the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why could we not cast it out?”
text.KJV: 9:28 And when he was come into the house, his disciples asked him priuately, Why could not we cast him out?
text.DRV: 9:28 And he said to them: This kind can go out by nothing but by prayer and fasting.

text.Base: He said to them, “This kind can come out only through prayer.”
text.KJV: 9:29 And hee said vnto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer, and fasting.
text.DRV: 9:29 And departing thence they passed by Galilee, neither would he that any man should know.

text.Base: They went on from there and passed through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know it;
text.KJV: 9:30 ¶ And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee, and he would not þ any man should know it.
text.DRV: 9:30 And he taught his Disciples, and said to them: That the Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men, and they shall kill him, and being killed the third day he shall rise again.

text.Base: for he was teaching his disciples, saying to them, “The Son of Man is to be betrayed into human hands, and they will kill him, and three days after being killed, he will rise again.”
text.KJV: 9:31 For he taught his disciples, and said vnto them, The sonne of man is deliuered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him, and after that he is killed, he shall rise the third day.
text.DRV: 9:31 But they knew not the word: and they were afraid to ask him.

text.Base: But they did not understand what he was saying and were afraid to ask him.
text.KJV: 9:32 But they vnderstood not that saying, and were afraid to aske him.
text.DRV: 9:32 And they came to Capharnaum. Who, when he was in the house, asked them, what did you treat of in the way?

text.Base: Then they came to Capernaum; and when he was in the house he asked them, “What were you arguing about on the way?”
text.KJV: 9:33 ¶ And he came to Capernaum; and being in the house, he asked them, What was it that yee disputed among your selues by the way?
text.DRV: 9:33 But they held their peace, for in the way they had disputed among themselves, which of them should be the greater.

text.Base: But they were silent, for on the way they had argued with one another who was the greatest.
text.KJV: 9:34 But they held their peace: For by the way they had disputed among themselues, who should be the greatest.
text.DRV: 9:34 And sitting down, he called the Twelve, and saith to them: If any man will be first, he shall be last of all, and the minister of all.

text.Base: He sat down, called the twelve, and said to them, “Whoever wants to be first must be last of all and servant of all.”
text.KJV: 9:35 And he sate downe, and called the twelue, and saith vnto them, If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all, and seruant of all.
text.DRV: 9:35 And taking a child, he set him in the midst of them. Whom when he had embraced, he said to them:

text.Base: Then he took a little child and put it among them; and taking it in his arms, he said to them,
text.KJV: 9:36 And he tooke a child, and set him in the midst of them: & when he had taken him in his arms, he said vnto them,
text.DRV: 9:36 Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me. And Whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.

text.Base: “Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes not me but the one who sent me.”
text.KJV: 9:37 Whosoeuer shall receiue one of such children in my Name, receiueth me: and whosoeuer shall receiue me, receiueth not me, but him that sent me.
text.DRV: 9:37 John answered him, saying: Master we saw one casting out devils in thy name, who followeth not us, and we prohibited him.

text.Base: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him, because he was not following us.”
text.KJV: 9:38 ¶ And Iohn answered him, saying, Master, we saw one casting out deuils in thy Name, and he followeth not vs, and we forbade him, because he followeth not vs.
text.DRV: 9:38 But Jesus said: Do not prohibit him. For there is no man that doth a miracle in my name, and can soon speak ill of me.

text.Base: But Jesus said, “Do not stop him; for no one who does a deed of power in my name will be able soon afterward to speak evil of me.
text.KJV: 9:39 But Iesus said, Forbid him not, for there is no man, which shall doe a miracle in my Name, that can lightly speake euill of me.
text.DRV: 9:39 For he that is not against you, is for you.

text.Base: Whoever is not against us is for us.
text.KJV: 9:40 For he that is not against vs, is on our part.
text.DRV: 9:40 For Whosoever shall give you to drink a cup of water in my name, because you are Christ's; amen I say to you, he shall not lose his reward.

text.Base: For truly I tell you, whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you bear the name of Christ will by no means lose the reward.
text.KJV: 9:41 For whosoeuer shall giue you a cup of water to drinke in my Name, because yee belong to Christ: Uerily I say vnto you, he shall not lose his reward.
text.DRV: 9:41 And whosoever shall scandalize one of these little ones believing in me; it is good for him rather if a millstone were put about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.

text.Base: “If any of you put a stumbling block before one of these little ones who believe in me, it would be better for you if a great millstone were hung around your neck and you were thrown into the sea.
text.KJV: 9:42 And whosoeuer shall offend one of these litle ones that beleeue in me, it is better for him, that a milstone were hanged about his necke, and he were cast into the Sea.
text.DRV: 9:42 And if thy hand scandalize thee, cut it off. It is good for thee to enter into life, maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire unquenchable,

text.Base: If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better for you to enter life maimed than to have two hands and to go to hell, to the unquenchable fire.
text.KJV: 9:43 And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: It is better for thee to enter into life maimed, then hauing two hands, to goe into hell, into the fire that neuer shall be quenched:
text.DRV: 9:43 where their worm dieth not, and the fire quencheth not.

text.Base: And if your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better for you to enter life lame than to have two feet and to be thrown into hell.
text.KJV: 9:45 And if thy foote offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, then hauing two feete, to be cast into hell, into the fire that neuer shall be quenched:
text.DRV: 9:45 where their worm dieth not, and the fire quencheth not.

text.Base: And if your eye causes you to stumble, tear it out; it is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than to have two eyes and to be thrown into hell,
text.KJV: 9:47 And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, then hauing two eyes, to be cast into helfire:
text.DRV: 9:47 where their worm dieth not, and the fire quencheth not.

text.Base: where their worm never dies, and the fire is never quenched.
text.KJV: 9:48 Where their worme dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
text.DRV: 9:48 For every one shall be salted with fire: and every victim shall be salted with salt.

text.Base: “For everyone will be salted with fire.
text.KJV: 9:49 For euery one shall be salted with fire, and euery sacrifice shall be salted with salt.
text.DRV: 9:49 Salt is good. But if the salt shall be unseasoned; wherewith will you season it? Have salt in you, and have peace among you.

text.Base: Salt is good; but if salt has lost its saltiness, how can you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”
text.KJV: 9:50 Salt is good: but if the salt haue lost his saltnesse, wherewith will you season it? Haue salt in your selues, and haue peace one with another.

text.NRSV: He left that place and went to the region of Judea and beyond the Jordan. And crowds again gathered around him; and, as was his custom, he again taught them.
text.Rotherham: And, from thence, arising, he cometh into the bounds of Judaea and beyond the Jordan,—and there come together again, multitudes unto him, and, as he had been wont, again, was he teaching them.
text.KJV: 10:1 And he rose from thence, & commeth into the coasts of Iudea by the farther side of Iordan: and the people resort vnto him againe, and as he was wont, he taught them againe.
text.DRV: 10:1 And rising up thence, he cometh into the coasts of Jewry beyond Jordan: and the multitudes assemble again unto him. And as he was accustomed, again he taught them.

text.NRSV: Some Pharisees came, and to test him they asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”
text.Rotherham: And Pharisees coming near were questioning him—whether it is allowed a husband to divorce a wife, testing him.
text.KJV: 10:2 ¶ And the Pharises came to him, and asked him, Is it lawfull for a man to put away his wife? tempting him.
text.DRV: 10:2 And the Pharisees coming near, asked him: Is it lawful for a man to dismiss his wife? Tempting him.

text.NRSV: He answered them, “What did Moses command you?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—What unto you did, Moses, command?
text.KJV: 10:3 And he answered, and saide vnto them, What did Moses command you?
text.DRV: 10:3 But he answering, said to them: What did Moyses command you?

text.NRSV: They said, “Moses allowed a man to write a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—Moses permitted, to write, a roll of dismissal, and to divorce.
text.KJV: 10:4 And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of diuorcement, and to put her away.
text.DRV: 10:4 Who said: Moyses permitted to write a bill of divorce, and to dismiss her.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to them, “Because of your hardness of heart he wrote this commandment for you.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them—In view of your hardness of heart, wrote he for you this commandment;
text.KJV: 10:5 And Iesus answered, and said vnto them, For the hardnesse of your heart, he wrote you this precept.
text.DRV: 10:5 To whom Jesus answering, said: For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.

text.NRSV: But from the beginning of creation, ‘God made them male and female.’
text.Rotherham: But, from the beginning of creation, male and female, made he [them];
text.KJV: 10:6 But from the beginning of the creation, God made them male, and female.
text.DRV: 10:6 But from the beginning of the creation God made them male & female.

text.NRSV: ‘For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,
text.Rotherham: For this cause, shall a man leave behind his father and mother,
text.KJV: 10:7 For this cause shall a man leaue his father and mother, and cleaue to his wife,
text.DRV: 10:7 For this cause, man shall leave his father & mother; and shall cleave to his wife.

text.NRSV: and the two shall become one flesh.’ So they are no longer two, but one flesh.
text.Rotherham: and, the two, shall become, one flesh; so that, no longer, are they two, but, one flesh.
text.KJV: 10:8 And they twaine shalbe one flesh: so then they are no more twaine, but one flesh.
text.DRV: 10:8 And they two shall be in one flesh. Therefore now they are not two, but one flesh.

text.NRSV: Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
text.Rotherham: What then, God, hath yoked together, let, a man, not put asunder.
text.KJV: 10:9 What therefore God hath ioyned together, let not man put asunder.
text.DRV: 10:9 That therefore which God hath joined together, let no man separate.

text.NRSV: Then in the house the disciples asked him again about this matter.
text.Rotherham: And, [coming] into the house again, the disciples, concerning this, were questioning him;
text.KJV: 10:10 And in the house his disciples asked him againe of the same matter.
text.DRV: 10:10 And in the house again his Disciples asked him of the same thing.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her;
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them—Whosoever shall divorce his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her;
text.KJV: 10:11 And he saith vnto them, Whosoeuer shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.
text.DRV: 10:11 And he saith to them: Whosoever dismisseth his wife and marrieth another, committeth adultery upon her.

text.NRSV: and if she divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.”
text.Rotherham: And, if, she, divorcing her husband, marry another, she committeth adultery.
text.KJV: 10:12 And if a woman shall put away her husband, and bee married to another, she committeth adulterie.
text.DRV: 10:12 And if the wife dismiss her husband, and marry another, she committeth adultery.

text.NRSV: People were bringing little children to him in order that he might touch them; and the disciples spoke sternly to them.
text.Rotherham: And they were bringing unto him children, that he might, touch, them,—the disciples, however, were rebuking them.
text.KJV: 10:13 ¶ And they brought yong children to him, that he should touch them, and his disciples rebuked those that brought them.
text.DRV: 10:13 And they offered to him young children, that he might touch them. And the Disciples threatened those that offered them.

text.NRSV: But when Jesus saw this, he was indignant and said to them, “Let the little children come to me; do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of God belongs.”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, seeing it, was greatly displeased, and said unto them—Suffer the children to come unto me,—do not hinder them; for, of such, is the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: 10:14 But when Iesus saw it, hee was much displeased, and said vnto them, Suffer the little children to come vnto mee, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdome of God.
text.DRV: 10:14 Whom when Jesus saw, he took it ill, and said to them: Suffer the little children to come unto me, and prohibit them not. For the Kingdom of God is for such.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will never enter it.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—Whosoever shall not welcome the kingdom of God, as a child, in nowise shall enter thereinto.
text.KJV: 10:15 Uerily I say vnto you, Whosoeuer shall not receiue the kingdome of God as a little childe, he shall not enter therein.
text.DRV: 10:15 Amen I say to you, whosoever receiveth not the Kingdom of God as a little child, shall not enter into it.

text.NRSV: And he took them up in his arms, laid his hands on them, and blessed them.
text.Rotherham: And, folding them in his arms, he was blessing them, having laid his hands upon them.
text.KJV: 10:16 And hee tooke them vp in his armes, put his handes vpon them, and blessed them.
text.DRV: 10:16 And embracing them, and imposing hands upon them, he blessed them.

text.NRSV: As he was setting out on a journey, a man ran up and knelt before him, and asked him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was going forth into a road, one, running, and kneeling before him, was questioning him—Good Teacher! what shall I do that, life age—abiding, I may inherit?
text.KJV: 10:17 ¶ And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled to him, and asked him, Good master, what shall I doe that I may inherit eternall life?
text.DRV: 10:17 And when he was gone forth in the way, a certain man running forth & kneeling before him asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may receive life everlasting?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Why callest thou me, good? None, is good, save one—God.
text.KJV: 10:18 And Iesus said vnto him, Why callest thou me good? There is no man good, but one, that is God.
text.DRV: 10:18 And Jesus said to him, why callest thou me good? None is good but one, God.

text.NRSV: You know the commandments: ‘You shall not murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness; You shall not defraud; Honor your father and mother.’”
text.Rotherham: The commandments, thou knowest,—Do not commit murder, Do not commit adultery, Do not steal; Do not bear false witness, Do not defraud, Honour thy father and mother.
text.KJV: 10:19 Thou knowest the Commandements, Doe not commit adulterie, Doe not kill, Doe not steale, Doe not beare false witnesse, Defraud not, Honour thy father, and mother.
text.DRV: 10:19 Thou knowest the commandments, Commit not adultery, Kill not, Steal not, Bear not false witness, do no fraud, Honour thy father and mother.

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Teacher, I have kept all these since my youth.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him—Teacher! all these things, have I kept, from my youth.
text.KJV: 10:20 And hee answered, and saide vnto him, Master, all these haue I obserued from my youth.
text.DRV: 10:20 But he answering, said to him: Master all these things I have observed from my youth.

text.NRSV: Jesus, looking at him, loved him and said, “You lack one thing; go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, looking at him, loved him, and said unto him—One thing, unto thee, is wanting;—Withdraw! whatsoever thou hast, sell, and give unto [the] destitute,—and thou shalt have treasure in heaven; and come! be following me.
text.KJV: 10:21 Then Iesus beholding him, loued him, and said vnto him, One thing thou lackest; Goe thy way, sell whatsoeuer thou hast, and giue to the poore, and thou shalt haue treasure in heauen, and come, take vp the crosse & folow me.
text.DRV: 10:21 And Jesus beholding him, loved him, and said to him: One thing is wanting unto thee: go, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in Heaven; and come, follow me.

text.NRSV: When he heard this, he was shocked and went away grieving, for he had many possessions.
text.Rotherham: And, he, becoming gloomy because of the word, departed sorrowing, for he was holding, many possessions.
text.KJV: 10:22 And hee was sad at that saying, and went away grieued: for hee had great possessions.
text.DRV: 10:22 Who being stroken sad at the word, went away sorrowful. For he had many possessions.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “How hard it will be for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!”
text.Rotherham: And, looking around, Jesus saith unto his disciples—With what difficulty, shall, they who have money, enter, into the kingdom of God!
text.KJV: 10:23 ¶ And Iesus looked round about, and saith vnto his disciples, How hardly shall they that haue riches enter into the kingdome of God?
text.DRV: 10:23 And Jesus looking about, saith to his Disciples: How hardly shall they that have money, enter into the Kingdom of God!

text.NRSV: And the disciples were perplexed at these words. But Jesus said to them again, “Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!
text.Rotherham: And, the disciples, were in amazement, because of the words. But, Jesus, again answering, saith unto them—Children! how difficult, it is, to enter, into the kingdom of God!
text.KJV: 10:24 And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Iesus answereth againe, and saith vnto them, Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches, to enter into the kingdom of God?
text.DRV: 10:24 And the Disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus again answering, saith to them: Children, how hard is it for them that trust in money, to enter into the Kingdom of God!

text.NRSV: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: It is, easier, for a camel, through the eye of a needle, to pass, than for, a rich man, into the kingdom of God, to enter.
text.KJV: 10:25 It is easier for a camel to goe thorow the eye of a needle, then for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
text.DRV: 10:25 It is easier for a camel to pass through a needle's eye, than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: They were greatly astounded and said to one another, “Then who can be saved?”
text.Rotherham: And, they, were being exceedingly struck with astonishment, saying unto him—Who, then, can, be saved?
text.KJV: 10:26 And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselues, Who then can be saued?
text.DRV: 10:26 Who marveled more, saying to themselves: And who can be saved?

text.NRSV: Jesus looked at them and said, “For mortals it is impossible, but not for God; for God all things are possible.”
text.Rotherham: Looking at them, Jesus saith—With men, impossible, but not, with God; for, all things, are possible, with God
text.KJV: 10:27 And Iesus looking vpon them, saith, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.
text.DRV: 10:27 And Jesus beholding them saith: With men it is impossible; but not with God. For all things are possible with God.

text.NRSV: Peter began to say to him, “Look, we have left everything and followed you.”
text.Rotherham: Peter began to be saying unto him—Lo! we, have left all, and followed thee!
text.KJV: 10:28 ¶ Then Peter began to say vnto him, Loe, we haue left all, and haue followed thee.
text.DRV: 10:28 And Peter began to say unto him: Behold, we have left all things, and have followed thee.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields, for my sake and for the sake of the good news,
text.Rotherham: Jesus said—Verily, I say unto you—There is, no one, who hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or mother, or father, or children, or lands, for the sake of me, and [for the sake of] the glad-message,
text.KJV: 10:29 And Iesus answered, and said, Uerily I say vnto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the Gospels,
text.DRV: 10:29 Jesus answering, said: Amen I say to you, there is no man which hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or children, or lands for me and for the Gospel,

text.NRSV: who will not receive a hundredfold now in this age—houses, brothers and sisters, mothers and children, and fields, with persecutions—and in the age to come eternal life.
text.Rotherham: who shall not receive a hundredfold, now, in this season, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands,—with persecutions, and, in the age that is coming, life age-abiding.
text.KJV: 10:30 But hee shall receiue an hundred fold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternall life:
text.DRV: 10:30 that shall not receive an hundred times so much now in this time; houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions: and in the world to come life everlasting.

text.NRSV: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.”
text.Rotherham: But, many, shall be—first-last, and [the] Last-first.
text.KJV: 10:31 But many that are first, shall be last: and the last, first.
text.DRV: 10:31 But many that are first, shall be last; and the last, first.

text.NRSV: They were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking ahead of them; they were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. He took the twelve aside again and began to tell them what was to happen to him,
text.Rotherham: Now they were in the way, going up unto Jerusalem, and Jesus was going on before them,—and they were in amazement; and, they who followed, were in fear. And, taking unto himself, again, the twelve, he began to be saying unto them, as to the things about to befall him—
text.KJV: 10:32 ¶ And they were in the way going vp to Hierusalem: and Iesus went before them, and they were amazed, and as they followed, they were afraid: and he tooke againe the twelue, and began to tell them what things should happen vnto him,
text.DRV: 10:32 And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem: and Jesus went before them, and they were astonished; and following were afraid. And taking again the Twelve, he began to tell them the things that should befall him.

text.NRSV: saying, “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death; then they will hand him over to the Gentiles;
text.Rotherham: Lo! we are going up unto Jerusalem; and, the Son of Man, will be delivered up, unto the Chief-priests and the Scribes,—and they will condemn him to death, and deliver him up unto the nations;
text.KJV: 10:33 Saying, Behold, we go vp to Hierusalem, and the Sonne of man shall be deliuered vnto the chiefe Priests, and vnto the Scribes: and they shall condemne him to death, and shall deliuer him to the Gentiles.
text.DRV: 10:33 That, behold we go up to Jerusalem, and the Son of man shall be betrayed to the chief Priests, and to the Scribes and Ancients, and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: they will mock him, and spit upon him, and flog him, and kill him; and after three days he will rise again.”
text.Rotherham: And they will mock him, and spit upon him, and scourge him, and, slay;—and, after three days, will he, arise.
text.KJV: 10:34 And they shall mocke him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit vpon him, and shall kill him, and the third day he shall rise againe.
text.DRV: 10:34 and they shall mock him, and spit on him, and scourge him, and kill him, and the third day he shall rise again.

text.NRSV: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came forward to him and said to him, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask of you.”
text.Rotherham: And there come near unto him, James and John, the [two] sons of Zebedee,—saying unto him—Teacher! we desire, that, whatsoever we shall ask thee, thou wilt do for us.
text.KJV: 10:35 ¶ And Iames, and Iohn the sonnes of Zebedee come vnto him, saying, Master, we would þ thou shouldest do for vs whatsoeuer we shall desire.
text.DRV: 10:35 And there came to him James and John the sons of Zebedee, saying: Master, we will that what thing soever we shall ask, thou do it to us.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “What is it you want me to do for you?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—What desire ye, I should do for you?
text.KJV: 10:36 And hee saide vnto them, What would ye that I should doe for you?
text.DRV: 10:36 But he said to them: What will you that I do to you?

text.NRSV: And they said to him, “Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your glory.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said unto him—Grant us, that, one on thy right hand, and one on thy left, we may sit, in thy glory.
text.KJV: 10:37 They said vnto him, Grant vnto vs that wee may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.
text.DRV: 10:37 And they said: Grant to us, that we may sit, one on thy right hand & the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to them, “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, or be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them—Ye know not what ye are asking: Are ye able to drink the cup that, I, am to drink? or to be immersed, with the immersion wherewith, I, am to be immersed?
text.KJV: 10:38 But Iesus said vnto them, Yee know not what ye aske: Can ye drinke of the cup that I drinke of? and be baptized with the baptisme that I am baptized with?
text.DRV: 10:38 And Jesus said to them: You wot not what you ask. Can you drink the cup that I drink; or be baptized with the baptism wherewith I am baptized?

text.NRSV: They replied, “We are able.” Then Jesus said to them, “The cup that I drink you will drink; and with the baptism with which I am baptized, you will be baptized;
text.Rotherham: And, they, said unto him—We are able. And, Jesus, said unto them—The cup which, I, am to drink, ye shall drink, and, with the immersion wherewith, I, am to be immersed, shall ye be immersed;
text.KJV: 10:39 And they said vnto him, Wee can. And Iesus said vnto them, Ye shall indeed drinke of the cup that I drinke of: and with the baptisme that I am baptized withall, shall ye be baptized:
text.DRV: 10:39 But they said to him, we can. And Jesus said to them: The cup indeed which I drink, you shall drink; and with the Baptism wherewith I am baptized, shall you be baptized:

text.NRSV: but to sit at my right hand or at my left is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared.”
text.Rotherham: But, to sit on my right hand and on my left, is not mine to give, except unto them for whom it hath been prepared.
text.KJV: 10:40 But to sit on my right hand and on my left hand, is not mine to giue, but it shall be giuen to them for whom it is prepared.
text.DRV: 10:40 but to sit on my right hand or on my left, is not mine to give unto you, but to whom it is prepared.

text.NRSV: When the ten heard this, they began to be angry with James and John.
text.Rotherham: And the ten, hearing, began to be sorely displeased, concerning James and John.
text.KJV: 10:41 And when the ten heard it, they beganne to bee much displeased with Iames and Iohn.
text.DRV: 10:41 And the ten hearing, began to be displeased at James and John.

text.NRSV: So Jesus called them and said to them, “You know that among the Gentiles those whom they recognize as their rulers lord it over them, and their great ones are tyrants over them.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, calling them near, saith unto them—Ye know that, they who think to rule the nations, lord it over them, and, their great ones, wield authority over them;
text.KJV: 10:42 But Iesus called them to him, and saith vnto them, Yee know that they which are accompted to rule ouer the Gentiles, exercise Lordship ouer them: and their great ones exercise authoritie vpon them.
text.DRV: 10:42 And Jesus calling them, saith to them: You know that they which seem to rule over the Gentiles, overrule them: and their Princes have power over them.

text.NRSV: But it is not so among you; but whoever wishes to become great among you must be your servant,
text.Rotherham: But, not so, it is, among you. But, whosoever desireth to become, great, among you, shall be, your minister,
text.KJV: 10:43 But so shall it not be among you: but whosoeuer will bee great among you, shall be your minister:
text.DRV: 10:43 But it is not so in you. But whosoever will be greater, shall be your minister;

text.NRSV: and whoever wishes to be first among you must be slave of all.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever desireth, among you, to be, first, shall be, servant of all;
text.KJV: 10:44 And whosoeuer of you will bee the chiefest, shalbe seruant of all.
text.DRV: 10:44 and whosoever will be first among you, shall be the servant of all.

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
text.Rotherham: For, even the Son of Man, came not to be ministered unto, but minister,—and to give his life, a ransom instead of many.
text.KJV: 10:45 For euen the Sonne of man came not to bee ministred vnto, but to minister, and to giue his life a ransome for many.
text.DRV: 10:45 For the Son of man also is not come to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a redemption for many.

text.NRSV: They came to Jericho. As he and his disciples and a large crowd were leaving Jericho, Bartimaeus son of Timaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the roadside.
text.Rotherham: And they come into Jericho. And, as he was journeying forth from Jericho, and his disciples, and a considerable multitude, the son of Timaeus, blind Bar-Timaeus, a beggar, was sitting beside the road.
text.KJV: 10:46 ¶ And they came to Iericho: and as he went out of Iericho with his disciples, and a great number of people; blinde Bartimeus, the son of Timeus, sate by the high wayes side, begging.
text.DRV: 10:46 And they come to Jericho: and when he departed from Jericho, and his Disciples, and a very great multitude, the son of Timæus, Bar-timæus the blind man, sat by the way side begging.

text.NRSV: When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to shout out and say, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing that it was, Jesus the Nazarene, he began to be crying aloud, and saying—O Son of David! Jesus! have mercy on me.
text.KJV: 10:47 And when he heard that it was Iesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, and say, Iesus thou Sonne of Dauid, haue mercie on me.
text.DRV: 10:47 Who when he had heard, that it is Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry, and to say: Jesus, son of David, have mercy upon me.

text.NRSV: Many sternly ordered him to be quiet, but he cried out even more loudly, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And many were rebuking him, that he might hold his peace; but, he, so much the more, was crying aloud, O Son of David, have mercy on me.
text.KJV: 10:48 And many charged him, that he should hold his peace: But he cried the more a great deale, Thou Sonne of Dauid, haue mercy on me.
text.DRV: 10:48 And many threatened him, to hold his peace. But he cried much more, Son of David, have mercy upon me.

text.NRSV: Jesus stood still and said, “Call him here.” And they called the blind man, saying to him, “Take heart; get up, he is calling you.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming to a stand, Jesus said—Call him! And they call the blind man, saying unto him—Take courage! rise! he calleth thee!
text.KJV: 10:49 And Iesus stood still, and commanded him to bee called: and they call the blinde man, saying vnto him, Be of good comfort, rise, he calleth thee.
text.DRV: 10:49 And Jesus standing still commanded him to be called. And they call the blind man, saying to him: Be of better comfort, arise, he calleth thee.

text.NRSV: So throwing off his cloak, he sprang up and came to Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, he, throwing off his mantle—springing to his feet, came unto Jesus.
text.KJV: 10:50 And hee casting away his garment, rose, and came to Iesus.
text.DRV: 10:50 Who casting off his garment leapt up, and came to him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man said to him, “My teacher, let me see again.”
text.Rotherham: And, answering him, Jesus said—What desirest thou, I should do for thee? And, the blind man, said unto him—Rabboni! that may recover sight.
text.KJV: 10:51 And Iesus answered, and said vnto him, What wilt thou that I should doe vnto thee? The blinde man said vnto him, Lord, that I might receiue my sight.
text.DRV: 10:51 And Jesus answering, said to him: What wilt thou that I do unto thee? And the blind man said to him: Rabboni, that I may see.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Go; your faith has made you well.” Immediately he regained his sight and followed him on the way.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Go thy way! thy faith, hath saved thee. And, straightway, he recovered sight, and was following him in the road.
text.KJV: 10:52 And Iesus saide vnto him, Goe thy way, thy faith hath made thee whole: And immediatly hee receiued his sight, & followed Iesus in the way.
text.DRV: 10:52 And Jesus said to him: Go thy ways, thy faith hath made thee safe. And forthwith he saw, and followed him in the way.

text.Base: When they were approaching Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany, near the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples
text.KJV: 11:1 And when they came nigh to Hierusalem, vnto Bethphage, and Bethanie, at the mount of Oliues, hee sendeth foorth two of his disciples,
text.DRV: 11:1 And when they came nigh unto Jerusalem and Bethania to Mount-olivet, he sendeth two of his Disciples,

text.Base: and said to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately as you enter it, you will find tied there a colt that has never been ridden; untie it and bring it.
text.KJV: 11:2 And saith vnto them, Goe your way into the village ouer against you, and assoone as ye bee entred into it, yee shall finde a colt tied, whereon neuer man sate, loose him, and bring him.
text.DRV: 11:2 and saith to them: Go into the town that is against you, and immediately entering in thither, you shall find a colt tied, upon which no man yet hath sitten: loose him, and bring him.

text.Base: If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ just say this, ‘The Lord needs it and will send it back here immediately.’”
text.KJV: 11:3 And if any man say vnto you, Why doe yee this? Say yee, that the Lord hath need of him: and straightway he will send him hither.
text.DRV: 11:3 And if any man shall say to you, what do you? Say that he is needful for our Lord: and incontinent he will send him hither.

text.Base: They went away and found a colt tied near a door, outside in the street. As they were untying it,
text.KJV: 11:4 And they went their way, and found the colt tied by the doore without, in a place where two wayes met: and they loose him.
text.DRV: 11:4 And going their ways, they found the colt tied before the gate without in the meeting of two ways: and they loose him.

text.Base: some of the bystanders said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
text.KJV: 11:5 And certaine of them that stood there, said vnto them, What doe ye loosing the colt?
text.DRV: 11:5 And certain of them that stood there, said to them: What do you loosing the colt?

text.Base: They told them what Jesus had said; and they allowed them to take it.
text.KJV: 11:6 And they said vnto them euen as Iesus had commanded: and they let them goe.
text.DRV: 11:6 Who said to them as Jesus had commanded them; and they did let him go with them.

text.Base: Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks on it; and he sat on it.
text.KJV: 11:7 And they brought the colt to Iesus, and cast their garments on him, and he sate vpon him.
text.DRV: 11:7 And they brought the colt to Jesus; and they lay their garments upon him, and he sat upon him.

text.Base: Many people spread their cloaks on the road, and others spread leafy branches that they had cut in the fields.
text.KJV: 11:8 And many spread their garments in the way: and others cut downe branches of the trees, and strawed them in the way.
text.DRV: 11:8 And many spread their garments in the way: and others did cut boughs from the trees, and strawed them in the way.

text.Base: Then those who went ahead and those who followed were shouting, “Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
text.KJV: 11:9 And they that went before, and they that followed, cryed, saying, Hosanna, blessed is hee that commeth in the Name of the Lord.
text.DRV: 11:9 And they that went before and they that followed, cried saying: Hosanna, blessed is he that cometh in the name of our Lord.

text.Base: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest heaven!”
text.KJV: 11:10 Blessed be the kingdome of our father Dauid, that commeth in the Name of the Lord, Hosanna in the highest.
text.DRV: 11:10 Blessed is the Kingdom of our father David that cometh, Hosanna in the highest.

text.Base: Then he entered Jerusalem and went into the temple; and when he had looked around at everything, as it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
text.KJV: 11:11 And Iesus entred into Hierusalem, and into the Temple, and when hee had looked round about vpon all things, & now the euentide was come, he went out vnto Bethanie with the twelue.
text.DRV: 11:11 And he entered Jerusalem into the temple; and having viewed all things round about, when now the evening hour was come, he went forth into Bethania with the Twelve.

text.Base: On the following day, when they came from Bethany, he was hungry.
text.KJV: 11:12 ¶ And on the morow when they were come from Bethanie, hee was hungry.
text.DRV: 11:12 And the next day when they departed from Bethania, he was an hungered.

text.Base: Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to see whether perhaps he would find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
text.KJV: 11:13 And seeing a figtree a farre off, hauing leaues, hee came, if haply hee might find any thing thereon, & when he came to it, hee found nothing but leaues: for the time of figs was not yet.
text.DRV: 11:13 And when he had seen a far off a figtree having leaves, he came if happily he could find any thing on it. And when he was come to it, he found nothing but leaves. For it was not the time for figs.

text.Base: He said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again.” And his disciples heard it.
text.KJV: 11:14 And Iesus answered, and said vnto it, No man eate fruite of thee hereafter for euer. And his disciples heard it.
text.DRV: 11:14 And answering he said to it: Now no man eat fruit of thee any more for ever. And his Disciples heard it.

text.Base: Then they came to Jerusalem. And he entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling and those who were buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves;
text.KJV: 11:15 ¶ And they come to Hierusalem, and Iesus went into the Temple, and beganne to cast out them that sold and bought in the Temple, and ouerthrew the tables of the money changers, and the seats of them that sold doues,
text.DRV: 11:15 And they come to Jerusalem. And when he was entered into the temple, he began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple, and the tables of the bankers, and the chairs of them that sold pigeons he overthrew.

text.Base: and he would not allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
text.KJV: 11:16 And would not suffer that any man should carie any vessell thorow the Temple.
text.DRV: 11:16 And he suffered not that any man should carry a vessel through the temple;

text.Base: He was teaching them and saying, “Is it not written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations’? But you have made it a den of robbers.”
text.KJV: 11:17 And he taught, saying vnto them, Is it not written, My house shalbe called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye haue made it a den of theeues.
text.DRV: 11:17 And he taught, saying to them; Is it not written, That my house shall be called the house of prayer to all nations? But you have made it a den of thieves.

text.Base: And when the chief priests and the scribes heard it, they kept looking for a way to kill him; for they were afraid of him, because the whole crowd was spellbound by his teaching.
text.KJV: 11:18 And the Scribes and chiefe Priests heard it, and sought how they might destroy him: for they feared him, because all the people was astonished at his doctrine.
text.DRV: 11:18 Which when the chief Priests and the Scribes had heard, they sought how they might destroy him. For they were afraid of him, because the whole multitude was in admiration upon his doctrine.

text.Base: And when evening came, Jesus and his disciples went out of the city.
text.KJV: 11:19 And when Euen was come, Hee went out of the citie.
text.DRV: 11:19 And when evening was come, he went forth out of the city.

text.Base: In the morning as they passed by, they saw the fig tree withered away to its roots.
text.KJV: 11:20 ¶ And in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried vp from the roots.
text.DRV: 11:20 And when they passed by in the morning, they saw the figtree withered from the roots.

text.Base: Then Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered.”
text.KJV: 11:21 And Peter calling to remembrance saith vnto him, Master, behold, the fig tree which thou cursedst, is withered away.
text.DRV: 11:21 And Peter remembering, said to him: Rabbi, behold the figtree that thou didst curse, is withered.

text.Base: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.
text.KJV: 11:22 And Iesus answering, saith vnto them, Haue faith in God.
text.DRV: 11:22 And Jesus answering saith to them: Have faith of God.

text.Base: Truly I tell you, if you say to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ and do not doubt in your heart, but believe that what you say will come to pass, it will be done for you.
text.KJV: 11:23 For verely I say vnto you, that whosoeuer shall say vnto this mountaine, Bee thou remoued, and bee thou cast into the sea, and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall beleeue that those things which hee saith, shall come to passe: he shal haue whatsoeuer he saith.
text.DRV: 11:23 Amen I say to you, that whosoever shall say to this mountain, Be taken up and be cast into the sea, and shall not stagger in his heart, but believe that whatsoever he saith shall be done; it shall be done unto him.

text.Base: So I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
text.KJV: 11:24 Therfore I say vnto you, What things soeuer ye desire when ye pray, beleeue that ye receiue them, and ye shall haue them.
text.DRV: 11:24 Therefore I say to you, all things, whatsoever you ask, praying, believe that you shall receive, and they shall come unto you.

text.Base: “Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.”
text.KJV: 11:25 And when ye stand, praying, forgiue, if ye haue ought against any: that your Father also which is in heauen, may forgiue you your trespasses.
text.DRV: 11:25 And when you shall stand to pray, forgive if you have ought against any man; that also your Father which is in Heaven, may forgive you your sins.

text.Base: Again they came to Jerusalem. As he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to him
text.KJV: 11:27 ¶ And they come againe to Hierusalem, and as he was walking in the Temple, there come to him the chiefe Priests, and the Scribes, & the Elders,
text.DRV: 11:27 And they come again to Jerusalem. And when he walked in the temple, there come to him the chief Priests & the Scribes and the Ancients,

text.Base: and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them?”
text.KJV: 11:28 And say vnto him, By what authoritie doest thou these things? and who gaue thee this authority to doe these things?
text.DRV: 11:28 and they say to him: In what power doest thou these things? and who hath given thee this power, that thou shouldest do these things?

text.Base: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question; answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
text.KJV: 11:29 And Iesus answered, and saide vnto them, I will also aske of you one question, and answere me, and I will tell you by what authoritie I doe these things.
text.DRV: 11:29 And Jesus answering said to them: I also will ask you one word, and answer you me, and I will tell you in what power I do these things.

text.Base: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin? Answer me.”
text.KJV: 11:30 The baptisme of Iohn, was it from heauen, or of men? Answere me.
text.DRV: 11:30 The baptism of John was it from Heaven, or from men? Answer me.

text.Base: They argued with one another, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
text.KJV: 11:31 And they reasoned with themselues, saying, If we shall say, From heauen, he will say, Why then did ye not beleeue him?
text.DRV: 11:31 But they thought with themselves, saying: If we say, from Heaven; he will say, why then did you not believe him?

text.Base: But shall we say, ‘Of human origin’?”—they were afraid of the crowd, for all regarded John as actually a prophet.
text.KJV: 11:32 But if we shall say, Of men, they feared the people: for all men counted Iohn, that he was a Prophet indeed.
text.DRV: 11:32 If we say, from men, we fear the people. For all accounted John that he was indeed a Prophet.

text.Base: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”
text.KJV: 11:33 And they answered and said vnto Iesus, We cannot tell. And Iesus answering, saith vnto them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I doe these things.
text.DRV: 11:33 And they answering say to Jesus: We know not. And Jesus answering saith to them: Neither do I tell you in what power I do these things.

text.NRSV: Then he began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the wine press, and built a watchtower; then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.
text.Rotherham: And he began to be speaking unto them, in parables,—A man planted, a vineyard, and put round it a wall, and digged a wine-vat, and built a tower,—and let it out to husbandmen; and left home.
text.KJV: 12:1 And hee began to speake vnto them by parables. A certaine man planted a vineyard, and set an hedge about it, and digged a place for the wine fat, and built a towre, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a farre countrey.
text.DRV: 12:1 And he began to speak to them in parables: A man planted a vineyard and made a hedge about it, and digged a trough, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen; and went forth into a strange country.

text.NRSV: When the season came, he sent a slave to the tenants to collect from them his share of the produce of the vineyard.
text.Rotherham: And he sent forth, unto the husbandmen, in the season, a servant, that, from the husbandmen, he might receive of the fruits of the vineyard;
text.KJV: 12:2 And at the season, he sent to the husbandmen a seruant, that he might receiue from the husbandmen of the fruite of the vineyard.
text.DRV: 12:2 And he sent to the husbandmen in season a servant, to receive of the husbandmen, of the fruit of the vineyard.

text.NRSV: But they seized him, and beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: and, taking him, they beat him, and sent him away, empty.
text.KJV: 12:3 And they caught him, and beat him, and sent him away emptie.
text.DRV: 12:3 Who apprehending him, beat him; and sent him away empty.

text.NRSV: And again he sent another slave to them; this one they beat over the head and insulted.
text.Rotherham: And, again, sent he forth unto them, another servant; and, him, they smote on the head, and dishonoured.
text.KJV: 12:4 And againe, hee sent vnto them another seruant; and at him they cast stones, and wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully handled.
text.DRV: 12:4 And again he sent to them another servant; and him they wounded in the head, and used him reproachfully.

text.NRSV: Then he sent another, and that one they killed. And so it was with many others; some they beat, and others they killed.
text.Rotherham: And another sent he forth; and, him, they slew. And many others; some, indeed beating, and, others, slaying.
text.KJV: 12:5 And againe, he sent another, and him they killed: and many others, beating some, and killing some.
text.DRV: 12:5 And again he sent another, & him they killed: and many other, beating certain, and killing others.

text.NRSV: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
text.Rotherham: Yet one, had he, a son beloved: he sent him forth last unto them, saying—They will pay deference unto my son!
text.KJV: 12:6 Hauing yet therefore one sonne his welbeloued, he sent him also last vnto them, saying, They will reuerence my sonne.
text.DRV: 12:6 Therefore having yet one son most dear; him also he sent unto them last saying: That they will reverence my son.

text.NRSV: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
text.Rotherham: But, those husbandmen, unto themselves, said—This, is the heir: Come! let us slay him, and, ours, shall be, the inheritance.
text.KJV: 12:7 But those husbandmen said amongst themselues, This is the heire, come, let vs kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.
text.DRV: 12:7 But the husbandmen said one to another. This is the heir; come, let us kill him; and the inheritance shall be ours.

text.NRSV: So they seized him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
text.Rotherham: And, taking, they slew him, and cast him forth outside the vineyard.
text.KJV: 12:8 And they tooke him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.
text.DRV: 12:8 And apprehending him, they killed him, and cast him forth out of the vineyard.

text.NRSV: What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.
text.Rotherham: What will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come, and destroy the husbandmen, and let the vineyard unto others.
text.KJV: 12:9 What shall therefore the Lord of the vineyard doe? He will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will giue the vineyard vnto others.
text.DRV: 12:9 What therefore will the Lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the husbandmen; and will give the vineyard to others.

text.NRSV: Have you not read this scripture: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
text.Rotherham: Have ye not, this scripture, read—A stone which the builders rejected, the same, hath become head of the corner!
text.KJV: 12:10 And haue ye not read this Scripture? The stone which the builders reiected, is become the head of the corner:
text.DRV: 12:10 Neither have you read this scripture, The stone which the builders rejected; the same is made the head of the corner:

text.NRSV: this was the Lord’s doing, and it is amazing in our eyes’?”
text.Rotherham: From the Lord, hath this come to pass, and is marvellous in our eyes?
text.KJV: 12:11 This was the Lords doing, and it is maruellous in our eies.
text.DRV: 12:11 By our Lord was this done, and it is marvelous in our eyes?

text.NRSV: When they realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowd. So they left him and went away.
text.Rotherham: And they were seeking, to secure, him,—and were in fear of the multitude; for they perceived that, against them, the parable he had spoken. And, leaving him, they departed.
text.KJV: 12:12 And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people, for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.
text.DRV: 12:12 And they sought to lay hands on him, and they feared the multitude. For they knew that he spake this parable to them. And leaving him they went their way.

text.NRSV: Then they sent to him some Pharisees and some Herodians to trap him in what he said.
text.Rotherham: And they send forth unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, that they might, catch, him, in discourse.
text.KJV: 12:13 ¶ And they send vnto him certaine of the Pharises, and of the Herodians, to catch him in his words.
text.DRV: 12:13 And they send to him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians; that they should entrap him in his word.

text.NRSV: And they came and said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are sincere, and show deference to no one; for you do not regard people with partiality, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth. Is it lawful to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?
text.Rotherham: And, coming, they say unto him—Teacher! we know that, true, thou art, and it concerneth thee not about anyone,—for thou lookest not unto the face of men; but, in truth, the way of God, dost teach:—Is it allowable to give tax unto Caesar, or not? Should we give, or should we not give?
text.KJV: 12:14 And when they were come, they say vnto him, Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth. Is it lawfull to giue tribute to Cesar, or not?
text.DRV: 12:14 Who coming, say to him: Master, we know that thou art a true speaker, and carest not for any man; for thou doest not look upon the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth. Is it lawful to give tribute to Cæsar; or shall we not give it?

text.NRSV: Should we pay them, or should we not?” But knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, “Why are you putting me to the test? Bring me a denarius and let me see it.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them—Why are ye, tempting, me? Bring me a denary, that I may see it.
text.KJV: 12:15 Shall we giue, or shall we not giue? But he knowing their hypocrisie, said vnto them, Why tempt yee mee? Bring me a penny that I may see it.
text.DRV: 12:15 Who knowing their subtlety, said to them: Why tempt you me? Bring me a penny that I may see it.

text.NRSV: And they brought one. Then he said to them, “Whose head is this, and whose title?” They answered, “The emperor’s.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, brought one. And he saith unto them—Whose, is this image, and the inscription? And, they, said unto him—Caesar’s.
text.KJV: 12:16 And they brought it: and he saith vnto them, Whose is this image and superscription? And they said vnto him, Cesars.
text.DRV: 12:16 But they brought it him. And he saith to them: Whose is this image, and inscription? They say to him, Cæsar's.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Give to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” And they were utterly amazed at him.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said—the things of Caesar, render, unto Caesar, and, the things of God, unto God. And they were marvelling at him.
text.KJV: 12:17 And Iesus answering, said vnto them, Render to Cesar the things that are Cesars: and to God the things that are Gods. And they maruailed at him.
text.DRV: 12:17 And Jesus answering, said to them: Render therefore the things that are Cæsar's, to Cæsar; and that are God's, to God. And they marveled at him.

text.NRSV: Some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him and asked him a question, saying,
text.Rotherham: And there come Sadducees unto him,—who, indeed say—Resurrection, there is none! and they were questioning him, saying—
text.KJV: 12:18 ¶ Then come vnto him the Sadducees, which say there is no resurrection, and they asked him, saying,
text.DRV: 12:18 And there came to him the Saducees that say there is no resurrection; and they asked him saying: Master,

text.NRSV: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, leaving a wife but no child, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
text.Rotherham: Teacher! Moses, wrote for us, that—If one’s brother die, and leave behind a wife, and leave no child, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
text.KJV: 12:19 Master, Moses wrote vnto vs, If a mans brother die, and leaue his wife behind him, and leaue no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise vp seed vnto his brother.
text.DRV: 12:19 Moyses wrote unto us, that if any man's brother die, and leave his wife, and leave no children, his brother shall take his wife and raise up seed to his brother.

text.NRSV: There were seven brothers; the first married and died childless;
text.Rotherham: Seven brethren, there were: and, the first, took a wife, and, dying, left no seed,—
text.KJV: 12:20 Now there were seuen brethren: and the first tooke a wife, and dying left no seede.
text.DRV: 12:20 There were therefore seven brethren; and the first took a wife, and died leaving no issue.

text.NRSV: and the second married her and died, leaving no child; and the third likewise;
text.Rotherham: And, the second, took her, and died, not leaving behind seed,—and, the third, likewise,—
text.KJV: 12:21 And the second tooke her, and died, neither left he any seed, and the third likewise.
text.DRV: 12:21 And the second took her, and died: and neither this left issue. And the third in like manner.

text.NRSV: none of the seven left a child. Last of all the woman herself died.
text.Rotherham: And, the seven, left no seed; last of all, the woman also, died:—
text.KJV: 12:22 And the seuen had her, and left no seede: last of all the woman died also.
text.DRV: 12:22 And the seven took her in like sort; and did not leave issue. Last of all the woman also died.

text.NRSV: In the resurrection whose wife will she be? For the seven had married her.”
text.Rotherham: In the resurrection,—of, which, of them shall she be, wife? For, the seven, had her to wife.
text.KJV: 12:23 In the resurrection therefore, when they shall rise, whose wife shall she be of them? for the seuen had her to wife.
text.DRV: 12:23 In the resurrection therefore when they shall rise again, whose wife shall she be of these? For the seven had her to wife.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Is not this the reason you are wrong, that you know neither the scriptures nor the power of God?
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Are ye not, for this cause, deceiving yourselves, knowing neither the Scriptures, nor the power of God?
text.KJV: 12:24 And Iesus answering, said vnto them, Doe ye not therefore erre, because yee know not the scriptures, neither the power of God?
text.DRV: 12:24 And Jesus answering, said to them: Do ye not therefore err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power of God?

text.NRSV: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
text.Rotherham: For, when, from among the dead, they rise, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like messengers in the heavens.
text.KJV: 12:25 For when they shall rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are giuen in marriage: but are as the Angels which are in heauen.
text.DRV: 12:25 For when they shall rise again from the dead, they shall neither marry, nor be married, but are as the Angels in Heaven.

text.NRSV: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the story about the bush, how God said to him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
text.Rotherham: But, as touching the dead, that they, do rise,—Have ye not read in the book of Moses, at the Bush, how God spake unto him, saying—I [am] the God of Abraham, and God of Isaac, and God of Jacob:—
text.KJV: 12:26 And as touching the dead, that they rise: haue ye not read in the booke of Moses, how in the bush God spake vnto him, saying, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isahac, and the God of Iacob?
text.DRV: 12:26 And as concerning the dead, that they do rise again, have you not read in the book of Moyses, how in the bush God spake to him, saying: I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?

text.NRSV: He is God not of the dead, but of the living; you are quite wrong.”
text.Rotherham: He is not a God of, dead, men, but of, living. Greatly, are ye deceiving yourselves.
text.KJV: 12:27 Hee is not the God of the dead, but the God of the liuing: yee therefore doe greatly erre.
text.DRV: 12:27 He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You therefore are much deceived.

text.NRSV: One of the scribes came near and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, he asked him, “Which commandment is the first of all?”
text.Rotherham: And one of the Scribes, coming near, hearing them discussing, seeing that, well, he had answered them, began to question him—Which is the chief commandment of all?
text.KJV: 12:28 ¶ And one of the Scribes came, and hauing heard them reasoning together, and perceiuing that he had answered them well, asked him which is the first commandement of all.
text.DRV: 12:28 And there came one of the Scribes that had heard them questioning, and seeing that he had well answered them, asked him which was the first commandment of all.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “The first is, ‘Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God, the Lord is one;
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—The chief is: Hear! O Israel,—The Lord our God, is, one Lord;
text.KJV: 12:29 And Iesus answered him, The first of al the commandements is, Heare, O Israel, the Lord our God is one Lord:
text.DRV: 12:29 And Jesus answered him: That the first commandment of all is, Hear Israel: the Lord thy God, is one God.

text.NRSV: you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.’
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou love the Lord thy God, with all thy heart, and with all thy soul,—and with all thy mind; and with all thy strength.
text.KJV: 12:30 And thou shalt loue the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soule, and with all thy minde, and with all thy strength: This is the first commandement.
text.DRV: 12:30 And thou shalt love the Lord thy God from thy whole heart, and with thy whole soul, and with thy whole mind, and with thy whole power. This is the first commandment.

text.NRSV: The second is this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”
text.Rotherham: The second, is, this—Thou shalt love, thy neighbour, as thyself. Greater than these, other commandment, is there none.
text.KJV: 12:31 And the second is like, namely this, Thou shalt loue thy neighbour as thy selfe: there is none other commandement greater then these.
text.DRV: 12:31 And the second is like to it: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. Another commandment greater than these there is not.

text.NRSV: Then the scribe said to him, “You are right, Teacher; you have truly said that ‘he is one, and besides him there is no other’;
text.Rotherham: The Scribe said unto him—Well, Teacher! in truth, hast thou said—He is, One, and there is none other, than he;
text.KJV: 12:32 And the Scribe said vnto him, Well master, thou hast said the truth: for there is one God, and there is none other but he.
text.DRV: 12:32 And the Scribe said to him: Well Master, thou hast said in truth, that there is one God, and there is none other besides him.

text.NRSV: and ‘to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the strength,’ and ‘to love one’s neighbor as oneself,’—this is much more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
text.Rotherham: And, to love him, with all the heart, and with all the understanding,—and with all the might; and to love one’s neighbour as one’s self, is, abundantly more, than all the whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.
text.KJV: 12:33 And to loue him with all the heart, and with all the vnderstanding, and with all the soule, and with all the strength, and to loue his neighbour as himselfe, is more then all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.
text.DRV: 12:33 And that he be loved from the whole heart, and with the whole understanding, and with the whole soul, and with the whole strength: and to love his neighbour as himself, is a greater thing than all holocausts and sacrifices.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” After that no one dared to ask him any more questions.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, seeing him, that, with intelligence he answered, said unto him—Not far, [art thou] from the kingdom of God! And, no one, any longer, was daring to, question, him.
text.KJV: 12:34 And when Iesus saw that he answered discreetly, hee saide vnto him, Thou art not far from the kingdome of God. And no man after that durst aske him any question.
text.DRV: 12:34 And Jesus seeing that he had answered wisely, said to him: Thou art not far from the Kingdom of God. And no man now durst ask him.
