text.NRSV: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, “How can the scribes say that the Messiah is the son of David?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, answering, was saying, as he taught in the temple—How say the Scribes that, the Christ, is, Son of David?
text.KJV: 12:35 ¶ And Iesus answered, and said, while hee taught in the Temple, How say the Scribes that Christ is the sonne of Dauid?
text.DRV: 12:35 And Jesus answering, said, teaching in the temple: How do the Scribes say, that Christ is the son of David?

text.NRSV: David himself, by the Holy Spirit, declared, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet.”’
text.Rotherham: David himself, hath said, by the Holy Spirit,—The Lord, hath said unto, my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, until I put thy foes beneath thy feet.
text.KJV: 12:36 For Dauid himselfe said by the holy Ghost, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, til I make thine enemies thy footstoole.
text.DRV: 12:36 For David himself saith in the Holy Ghost: Our Lord said to my Lord, sit on my right hand, until I put thine enemies the foot-stool of thy feet.

text.NRSV: David himself calls him Lord; so how can he be his son?” And the large crowd was listening to him with delight.
text.Rotherham: David himself, calleth him, Lord: whence, then, is he, his own son? And, the great multitude, was hearing him gladly.
text.KJV: 12:37 Dauid therefore himselfe calleth him Lord, and whence is hee then his sonne? And the common people heard him gladly.
text.DRV: 12:37 David therefore himself calleth him Lord, and whence is he his son? And a great multitude heard him gladly.

text.NRSV: As he taught, he said, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces,
text.Rotherham: And, in his teaching, he was saying—Beware of the Scribes, who desire, in robes, to be walking about, and salutations in the markets,
text.KJV: 12:38 ¶ And he said vnto them in his doctrine, Beware of the Scribes, which loue to goe in long clothing, and loue salutations in the market places,
text.DRV: 12:38 And he said to them in his doctrine: Take heed of the Scribes that will walk in long robes, and be saluted in the market-place,

text.NRSV: and to have the best seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets!
text.Rotherham: And first seats in the synagogues, and first couches in the chief meals,—
text.KJV: 12:39 And the chiefe seates in the Synagogues, and the vppermost roomes at feasts:
text.DRV: 12:39 And sit in the first chairs in the Synagogues, and love the highest places at suppers:

text.NRSV: They devour widows’ houses and for the sake of appearance say long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
text.Rotherham: Who devour widows’ houses, and, for a pretence, are long in prayer: these, shall receive a more surpassing judgment.
text.KJV: 12:40 Which deuoure widowes houses, and for a pretence make long prayers: These shall receiue greater damnation.
text.DRV: 12:40 Which devour widows' houses under the pretence of long prayer: these shall receive larger judgement.

text.NRSV: He sat down opposite the treasury, and watched the crowd putting money into the treasury. Many rich people put in large sums.
text.Rotherham: And, taking his seat over against the treasury, he was observing how, the multitude, was casting in copper into the treasury, and, man rich, were casting in, much.
text.KJV: 12:41 ¶ And Iesus sate ouer against the treasurie, and beheld how the people cast money into the treasurie: and many that were rich, cast in much.
text.DRV: 12:41 And Jesus sitting over against the treasury, beheld how the multitude did cast money into the treasury, and many rich men did cast in much.

text.NRSV: A poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which are worth a penny.
text.Rotherham: And there came, one destitute, widow, and cast in two mites, which are, a farthing.
text.KJV: 12:42 And there came a certaine poore widow, and she threw in two mites, which make a farthing.
text.DRV: 12:42 And when there came a certain poor widow, she cast in two mites, which is a farthing.

text.NRSV: Then he called his disciples and said to them, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all those who are contributing to the treasury.
text.Rotherham: And, calling near his disciples, he said to them—Verily, I say unto you, this destitute widow, more than they all, hath cast in, of those casting into the treasury;
text.KJV: 12:43 And he called vnto him his disciples, and saith vnto them, Uerily I say vnto you, that this poore widow hath cast more in, then all they which haue cast into the treasurie.
text.DRV: 12:43 And calling his Disciples together, he saith to them, Amen I say to you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than all that have cast into the treasury.

text.NRSV: For all of them have contributed out of their abundance; but she out of her poverty has put in everything she had, all she had to live on.”
text.Rotherham: For, they all, out of their surplus, cast in, but, she, out of her deficiency, all, as much as she had, cast in,—the whole of her living.
text.KJV: 12:44 For all they did cast in of their aboundance: but she of her want, did cast in all that she had, euen all her liuing.
text.DRV: 12:44 For all they of their aboundance have cast in; but she, of her penury hath cast in all that she had, her whole living.

text.NRSV: As he came out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Look, Teacher, what large stones and what large buildings!”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was going forth out of the temple, one of his disciples saith unto him—Teacher! see what manner of stones, and what manner of buildings!
text.KJV: 13:1 And as he went out of the Temple, one of his disciples saith vnto him, Master, see what maner of stones, and what buildings are here.
text.DRV: 13:1 And when he went out of the Temple, one of his Disciples saith to him: Master, behold what manner of stones, and what kind of buildings.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus asked him, “Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left here upon another; all will be thrown down.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Art thou beholding these great buildings? In nowise, shall there be left here, stone upon stone, which shall, in any wise, not be thrown down.
text.KJV: 13:2 And Iesus answering, said vnto him, Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone vpon an other, that shal not be throwen downe.
text.DRV: 13:2 And Jesus answering, said to him: Seest thou all these great buildings? There shall not be left a stone upon a stone, that shall not be destroyed.

text.NRSV: When he was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,
text.Rotherham: And, as he was sitting within the mount of Olives, over against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew were questioning him, privately—
text.KJV: 13:3 And as he sate vpon the mount of Oliues, ouer against the Temple, Peter, and Iames, and Iohn, and Andrew asked him priuately,
text.DRV: 13:3 And when he sat in Mount-olivet against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him apart:

text.NRSV: “Tell us, when will this be, and what will be the sign that all these things are about to be accomplished?”
text.Rotherham: Tell us, When, these things, shall be, and, what the sign, when all these things shall be about to be concluded.
text.KJV: 13:4 Tell vs, when shall these things be? And what shalbe the signe when all these things shalbe fulfilled?
text.DRV: 13:4 Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign when all these things shall begin to be consummate?

text.NRSV: Then Jesus began to say to them, “Beware that no one leads you astray.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, began to be saying unto them—Beware, lest anyone, deceive, you;
text.KJV: 13:5 And Iesus answering them, began to say, Take heed lest any man deceiue you.
text.DRV: 13:5 And Jesus answering began to say to them, See, that no man seduce you.

text.NRSV: Many will come in my name and say, ‘I am he!’ and they will lead many astray.
text.Rotherham: For, many, will come on my name, saying, I, am he! and, will deceive, many.
text.KJV: 13:6 For many shal come in my Name, saying, I am Christ: and shall deceiue many.
text.DRV: 13:6 For many shall come in my name saying that I am he; and they shall seduce many.

text.NRSV: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed; this must take place, but the end is not yet.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye shall hear of wars, and rumours of wars, be not alarmed—it must needs come to pass, but, not yet, is, the end.
text.KJV: 13:7 And when yee shall heare of warres, and rumors of warres, be yee not troubled: For such things must needs be, but the end shall not be yet.
text.DRV: 13:7 And when you shall hear of wars and bruits of wars, fear not. For these things must be, but the end is not yet.

text.NRSV: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. This is but the beginning of the birthpangs.
text.Rotherham: For there will arise—Nation against nation, and, kingdom against kingdom,—there will be earthquakes in places, there will be famines:—
text.KJV: 13:8 For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdome against kingdome: and there shalbe earthquakes in diuers places, and there shall be famines, and troubles: these are the beginnings of sorrowes.
text.DRV: 13:8 For nation shall rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom, and there shall be earth-quakes in places, & famines. These things are the beginning of sorrows.

text.NRSV: “As for yourselves, beware; for they will hand you over to councils; and you will be beaten in synagogues; and you will stand before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them.
text.Rotherham: A beginning of birth-pangs, are these things. But be, ye, taking heed, unto yourselves: they will deliver you up into high-councils, and, in synagogues, shall ye be beaten,—and, before governors and kings, shall ye be set, for my sake, for a witness unto them.
text.KJV: 13:9 ¶ But take heed to your selues: for they shall deliuer you vp to councels, and in the Synagogues ye shall be beaten, and ye shalbe brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them.
text.DRV: 13:9 But look to yourselves. For they shall deliver you up in Councils, and in Synagogues shall you be beaten, and you shall stand before Presidents and kings for my sake, for a testimony unto them.

text.NRSV: And the good news must first be proclaimed to all nations.
text.Rotherham: And, unto all the nations, first, must needs be proclaimed, the glad-message.
text.KJV: 13:10 And the Gospel must first be published among all nations.
text.DRV: 13:10 And into all nations first the Gospel must be preached.

text.NRSV: When they bring you to trial and deliver you up, do not worry beforehand about what you are to say; but say whatever is given you at that time, for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: And, when they are leading you, as they are delivering you up, be not beforehand anxious, what ye shall speak; but, whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, the same, speak,—for, ye, are not the speakers, but the Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: 13:11 But when they shall lead you, and deliuer you vp, take no thought before hand what ye shall speake, neither doe yee premeditate: but whatsoeuer shall bee giuen you in that houre, that speake yee: for it is not yee that speake, but the holy Ghost.
text.DRV: 13:11 And when they shall lead you and deliver you, be not careful beforehand what you shall speak; but that which shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye. For it is not you that speak, but the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death;
text.Rotherham: And, brother, will deliver up, brother, unto death, and, father, child, and, children, will rise up, against parents, and put them to death;
text.KJV: 13:12 Now the brother shall betray the brother to death, and the father the sonne: and children shall rise vp against their parents, and shall cause them to be put to death.
text.DRV: 13:12 And brother shall deliver brother unto death, and the father his Son; and the children shall arise against the parents, and shall work their death.

text.NRSV: and you will be hated by all because of my name. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
text.Rotherham: And ye will be men hated by all, because of my name; but, he that hath endured throughout, the same, shall be saved.
text.KJV: 13:13 And ye shall bee hated of all men for my Names sake: but hee that shall endure vnto the ende, the same shall be saued.
text.DRV: 13:13 And you shall be odious to all men for my name. But he that shall endure unto the end, he shall be saved.

text.NRSV: “But when you see the desolating sacrilege set up where it ought not to be (let the reader understand), then those in Judea must flee to the mountains;
text.Rotherham: But, when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, standing where it ought not, he that readeth, let him, think, then, they who are in Judaea, let them flee into the mountains;
text.KJV: 13:14 ¶ But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the Prophet, standing where it ought not (let him that readeth vnderstand) then let them that be in Iudea, flee to the mountaines:
text.DRV: 13:14 And when you shall see the abomination of desolation, standing where it ought not (he that readeth, let him understand) then they that are in Jewry, let them flee unto the mountains:

text.NRSV: the one on the housetop must not go down or enter the house to take anything away;
text.Rotherham: He that is on the house-top, let him not come down, neither let him enter, to take away anything out of his louse;
text.KJV: 13:15 And let him that is on the house top, not goe downe into the house, neither enter therin, to take any thing out of his house.
text.DRV: 13:15 and he that is on the house-top, let him not go down into the house, nor enter in to take any thing out of his house:

text.NRSV: the one in the field must not turn back to get a coat.
text.Rotherham: And, he that hath gone into the field, let him not turn back unto the things behind, to take away his mantle.
text.KJV: 13:16 And let him that is in the field, not turne backe againe for to take vp his garment.
text.DRV: 13:16 and he that shall be in the field, let him not return back to take his garment.

text.NRSV: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days!
text.Rotherham: But alas for the women with child, and for them who are giving suck,—in those days.
text.KJV: 13:17 But woe to them that are with child, and to them that giue suck in those dayes.
text.DRV: 13:17 And woe to them that are with child and that give suck in those days.

text.NRSV: Pray that it may not be in winter.
text.Rotherham: But be praying, that it may not happen in winter.
text.KJV: 13:18 And pray ye that your flight bee not in the winter.
text.DRV: 13:18 But pray that the things chance not in the winter.

text.NRSV: For in those days there will be suffering, such as has not been from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, no, and never will be.
text.Rotherham: For in those days shall be a tribulation—such, that there hath not happened, the like, from the beginning of creation which God created, until the present time, and shall in nowise happen.
text.KJV: 13:19 For in those dayes shall be affliction, such as was not from the beginning of the creation which God created, vnto this time, neither shall be.
text.DRV: 13:19 For those days shall be such tribulations as were not from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, neither shall be.

text.NRSV: And if the Lord had not cut short those days, no one would be saved; but for the sake of the elect, whom he chose, he has cut short those days.
text.Rotherham: And, save that the Lord hath shortened the days, no flesh should be saved; but, for the sake of the chosen of whom he hath made choice, he hath shortened the days.
text.KJV: 13:20 And except that the Lord had shortened those dayes, no flesh should be saued: but for the elects sake whome he hath chosen, he hath shortned the daies.
text.DRV: 13:20 And unless the Lord had shortened the days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect which he hath elected, he hath shortened the days.

text.NRSV: And if anyone says to you at that time, ‘Look! Here is the Messiah!’ or ‘Look! There he is!’—do not believe it.
text.Rotherham: And, then, if any, unto you, say—See! Here, is the Christ! See! there, do not believe it;
text.KJV: 13:21 And then, if any man shall say to you, Loe, here is Christ, or loe, hee is there: beleeue him not.
text.DRV: 13:21 And then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, lo, there: do not believe.

text.NRSV: False messiahs and false prophets will appear and produce signs and omens, to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.
text.Rotherham: For there will arise, false Christs, and false prophets; and they will show signs and wonders, so as to deceive, if possible, the chosen.
text.KJV: 13:22 For false Christs and false prophets shall rise, and shall shewe signes and wonders, to seduce, if it were possible, euen the elect.
text.DRV: 13:22 For there shall rise up false-Christs and false-Prophets, and they shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce (if it be possible) the elect also.

text.NRSV: But be on your guard; I have foretold everything to you.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, beware: I have foretold you, all things.
text.KJV: 13:23 But take ye heed: behold, I haue foretold you all things.
text.DRV: 13:23 You therefore take heed; behold I have foretold you all things.

text.NRSV: “But in those days, after that suffering, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light,
text.Rotherham: But, in those days, after that tribulation, the sun, shall be darkened, and, the moon, will not give her brightness,
text.KJV: 13:24 ¶ But in those dayes, after that tribulation, the Sunne shalbe darkned, and the Moone shall not giue her light.
text.DRV: 13:24 But in those days after that tribulation the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,

text.NRSV: and the stars will be falling from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
text.Rotherham: And, the stars, will, out of the heavens, be falling,—and, the powers which are in the heavens, will be shaken;
text.KJV: 13:25 And the Starres of heauen shall fall, and the powers that are in heauen shall be shaken.
text.DRV: 13:25 and the stars of Heaven shall be falling down, and the powers that are in Heaven, shall be moved.

text.NRSV: Then they will see ‘the Son of Man coming in clouds’ with great power and glory.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will they see the Son of Man—coming in clouds, with great power and glory.
text.KJV: 13:26 And then shal they see the Sonne of man comming in the cloudes, with great power and glory.
text.DRV: 13:26 And then they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds, with much power and glory.

text.NRSV: Then he will send out the angels, and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will he send forth the messengers, and they will gather together his chosen—out of the four winds, from utmost bound of earth, unto utmost bound of heaven.
text.KJV: 13:27 And then shal he send his Angels, and shall gather together his elect from the foure winds, from the vttermost part of the earth, to the vttermost part of heauen.
text.DRV: 13:27 And then shall he send his Angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of Heaven.

text.NRSV: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near.
text.Rotherham: Now, from the fig-tree, learn ye, the parable:—When, already, her young branch, becometh tender, and, the leaves, are sprouting, ye observe that, near, is, the summer:
text.KJV: 13:28 Now learne a parable of the fig tree. When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaues, ye know that summer is neere:
text.DRV: 13:28 And of the figtree learn ye a parable. When now the bough thereof is tender, and the leaves come forth, you know that summer is very nigh.

text.NRSV: So also, when you see these things happening, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
text.Rotherham: Thus, ye also, when ye shall see these things coming to pass, observe ye, that, near, he is, at the doors.
text.KJV: 13:29 So ye in like maner, when ye shal see these things come to passe, knowe that it is nigh, euen at the doores.
text.DRV: 13:29 So you also when you shall see these things come to pass, know ye that it is very nigh, at the doors.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—In nowise, shall this generation pass away, until all these things, shall happen:
text.KJV: 13:30 Uerely I say vnto you, that this generation shall not passe, till all these things be done.
text.DRV: 13:30 Amen I say to you, that this generation shall not pass, until all these things be done.

text.NRSV: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
text.Rotherham: The heaven and the earth, shall pass away, but, my words, shall not pass away.
text.KJV: 13:31 Heauen and earth shal passe away: but my words shall not passe away.
text.DRV: 13:31 Heaven & earth shall pass, but my words shall not pass.

text.NRSV: “But about that day or hour no one knows, neither the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning that day or hour, no one, knoweth, neither the messengers in heaven, nor the Son,—save the Father.
text.KJV: 13:32 ¶ But of that day and that houre knoweth no man, no not the Angels which are in heauen, neither the Son, but the Father.
text.DRV: 13:32 But of that day or hour no man knoweth, neither the Angels in Heaven, nor the Son, but the Father.

text.NRSV: Beware, keep alert; for you do not know when the time will come.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed, be watching, for ye know not, when, the season [is]:—
text.KJV: 13:33 Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.
text.DRV: 13:33 Take heed, watch, and pray. For you know not when the time is.

text.NRSV: It is like a man going on a journey, when he leaves home and puts his slaves in charge, each with his particular task, and commands the doorkeeper to be on the watch.
text.Rotherham: As a man from home—having left his house, and given his servants the authority, to each one, his work,—and, unto the porter, hath given command, that he should watch:—
text.KJV: 13:34 For the Sonne of man is as a man taking a farre iourney, who left his house, and gaue authority to his seruants, and to euery man his worke, and commanded the porter to watch:
text.DRV: 13:34 Even as a man who being gone into a strange country, left his house; and gave his servants authority over each work, and commanded the porter to watch.

text.NRSV: Therefore, keep awake—for you do not know when the master of the house will come, in the evening, or at midnight, or at cockcrow, or at dawn,
text.Rotherham: Be watching, therefore, for ye know not, when, the master of the house, is coming,—whether at even, or at midnight, or at cock-crowing, or at early morn;—
text.KJV: 13:35 Watch ye therefore (for ye knowe not when the master of the house commeth, at Euen, or at midnight, or at the cocke crowing, or in the morning.)
text.DRV: 13:35 Watch ye therefore (for you know not when the Lord of the house cometh: at even, or at midnight, or at the cock crowing, or in the morning)

text.NRSV: or else he may find you asleep when he comes suddenly.
text.Rotherham: Lest, coming suddenly, he find you, sleeping.
text.KJV: 13:36 Lest comming suddenly, he finde you sleeping.
text.DRV: 13:36 lest coming upon a sudden, he find you sleeping.

text.NRSV: And what I say to you I say to all: Keep awake.”
text.Rotherham: But, what, unto you, I say, unto all, I say:—Be watching.
text.KJV: 13:37 And what I say vnto you, I say vnto all, Watch.
text.DRV: 13:37 And that which I say to you, I say to all, Watch.

text.NRSV: It was two days before the Passover and the festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him;
text.Rotherham: Now it was the Passover and the Unleavened [cakes], after two days. And the High-priests and Scribes were seeking, how, with guile, they might secure, and lay him;
text.KJV: 14:1 After two dayes was the feast of the Passeouer, and of vnleauened bread: and the chiefe Priests, and the Scribes sought how they might take him by craft, and put him to death.
text.DRV: 14:1 And the Pasch was, and the Azymes after two days: and the chief Priests and the Scribes sought how they might by some wile lay hands on him, and kill him.

text.NRSV: for they said, “Not during the festival, or there may be a riot among the people.”
text.Rotherham: for they were saying—Not in the feast, lest there be an uproar of the people.
text.KJV: 14:2 But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an vprore of the people.
text.DRV: 14:2 But they said: Not on the festival day, lest there might be a tumult of the people.

text.NRSV: While he was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at the table, a woman came with an alabaster jar of very costly ointment of nard, and she broke open the jar and poured the ointment on his head.
text.Rotherham: And, he being in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as he was reclining, there came a woman, holding an alabaster-jar of perfume, pure spikenard, very costly; and breaking the jar she was pouring down [the perfume], upon his head.
text.KJV: 14:3 ¶ And being in Bethanie, in the house of Simon the leper, as he sate at meat, there came a woman, hauing an Alabaster boxe of oyntment of spikenard very precious, and shee brake the boxe, and powred it on his head.
text.DRV: 14:3 And when he was at Bethania in the house of Simon the Leper, and sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment, of precious spike-nard; and breaking the alabaster-box, she poured it out upon his head.

text.NRSV: But some were there who said to one another in anger, “Why was the ointment wasted in this way?
text.Rotherham: But there were some much displeased among themselves—To what end hath, this waste of the perfume happened?
text.KJV: 14:4 And there were some that had indignation within themselues, and said, Why was this waste of the oyntment made?
text.DRV: 14:4 But there were certain that had indignation within themselves, and said: Whereto is this waste of the ointment made?

text.NRSV: For this ointment could have been sold for more than three hundred denarii, and the money given to the poor.” And they scolded her.
text.Rotherham: For this perfume could have been sold for above three hundred denaries, and given unto the destitute! and they were indignant with her.
text.KJV: 14:5 For it might haue bene solde for more then three hundred pence, and haue bene giuen to the poore: and they murmured against her.
text.DRV: 14:5 For this ointment might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and given to the poor. And they murmured against her.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said, “Let her alone; why do you trouble her? She has performed a good service for me.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said—Let her alone! Why are ye reproaching, her? A seemly work, hath she wrought, in me;
text.KJV: 14:6 And Iesus said, Let her alone, why trouble you her? Shee hath wrought a good worke on me.
text.DRV: 14:6 But Jesus said: Let her alone, why do you molest her? She hath wrought a good work upon me.

text.NRSV: For you always have the poor with you, and you can show kindness to them whenever you wish; but you will not always have me.
text.Rotherham: For, always, have ye, the destitute, with you, and whensoever ye please, ye can, unto them, [at any time] do well! But, me, not, always, have ye.
text.KJV: 14:7 For ye haue the poore with you alwayes, and whensoeuer ye will yee may doe them good: but me ye haue not alwayes.
text.DRV: 14:7 For the poor you have always with you; and when you will, you may do them good; but me you have not always.

text.NRSV: She has done what she could; she has anointed my body beforehand for its burial.
text.Rotherham: What she had, she used,—She took it beforehand to anoint my body for the burial;
text.KJV: 14:8 She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.
text.DRV: 14:8 That which she had, she hath done; she hath prevented to anoint my body to the burial.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, wherever the good news is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be told in remembrance of her.”
text.Rotherham: And, verily, say unto you—Wheresoever the glad-message shall be proclaimed throughout the whole world,—also what she did, will be told for a memorial of her.
text.KJV: 14:9 Uerely I say vnto you, Wheresoeuer this Gospel shalbe preached thorowout the whole world, this also that she hath done, shall be spoken of for a memoriall of her.
text.DRV: 14:9 Amen I say to you, wheresoever this Gospel shall be preached in the whole world, that also which she hath done, shall be told for a memory of her.

text.NRSV: Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went to the chief priests in order to betray him to them.
text.Rotherham: And ,Judas Iscariot, who was one of he twelve, went away unto the High-priests, that, him, he might deliver up unto them.
text.KJV: 14:10 ¶ And Iudas Iscariot, one of the twelue, went vnto the chiefe Priests, to betray him vnto them.
text.DRV: 14:10 And Judas Iscariote one of the Twelve went his way to the chief Priests, for to betray him to them.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they were greatly pleased, and promised to give him money. So he began to look for an opportunity to betray him.
text.Rotherham: Now, when they heard, they rejoiced, and promised to give him, silver; and he was seeking how, at a favourable opportunity, he might, deliver him up.
text.KJV: 14:11 And when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to giue him money. And he sought how he might conueniently betray him.
text.DRV: 14:11 Who hearing it, were glad; and they promised him that they would give him money. And he sought how he might betray him conveniently.

text.NRSV: On the first day of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb is sacrificed, his disciples said to him, “Where do you want us to go and make the preparations for you to eat the Passover?”
text.Rotherham: And, on the first day of the unleavened cakes, when, the passover, they were slaying, his disciples say unto him—Where wilt thou, we depart and make ready, that thou mayest eat the passover?
text.KJV: 14:12 ¶ And the first day of vnleauened bread, when they killed the Passeouer, his disciples said vnto him, Where wilt thou that we goe, and prepare, that thou mayest eate the Passeouer?
text.DRV: 14:12 And the first day of the Azymes when they sacrificed the Pasch, the Disciples say to him: Whither wilt thou that we go, and prepare for thee to eat the Pasch?

text.NRSV: So he sent two of his disciples, saying to them, “Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him,
text.Rotherham: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them—Go your way into the city, and there will meet you a man, a jar of water, bearing,—follow him;
text.KJV: 14:13 And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith vnto them, Goe yee into the citie, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.
text.DRV: 14:13 And he sendeth two of his Disciples, and saith to them: Go ye into the city; and there shall meet you a man carrying a pitcher of water, follow him;

text.NRSV: and wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher asks, Where is my guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?’
text.Rotherham: and, wheresoever he shall enter, say ye unto the householder—The teacher, saith, Where is my lodging, where, the passover, with my disciples, I may eat?
text.KJV: 14:14 And wheresoeuer he shall goe in, say yee to the good man of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guest chamber, where I shall eate the Passeouer with my disciples?
text.DRV: 14:14 and whithersoever he entereth, say to the Master of the house, that the Master saith, where is my refectory, where I may eat the Pasch with my Disciples?

text.NRSV: He will show you a large room upstairs, furnished and ready. Make preparations for us there.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, unto you, will shew, a large upper-room, spread ready, and, there, make ye ready for us.
text.KJV: 14:15 And he will shew you a large vpper roome furnished, and prepared: there make ready for vs.
text.DRV: 14:15 And he will shew you a great chamber, adorned; and there prepare for us.

text.NRSV: So the disciples set out and went to the city, and found everything as he had told them; and they prepared the Passover meal.
text.Rotherham: And the disciples went forth, and came into the city, and found, according as he had said unto them,—and they made ready the passover.
text.KJV: 14:16 And his disciples went forth, and came into the citie, and found as hee had said vnto them: and they made readie the Passeouer.
text.DRV: 14:16 And his Disciples went their ways, and came into the city; and they found as he had told them, and they prepared the Pasch.

text.NRSV: When it was evening, he came with the twelve.
text.Rotherham: And, when evening arrived, he cometh, with the twelve.
text.KJV: 14:17 And in the euening hee commeth with the twelue.
text.DRV: 14:17 And when even was come, he cometh with the Twelve.

text.NRSV: And when they had taken their places and were eating, Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me, one who is eating with me.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were reclining and eating, Jesus said—Verily, I say unto you—One from among you, will deliver me up, he that is eating with me.
text.KJV: 14:18 And as they sate, and did eat, Iesus said, Uerily I say vnto you, one of you which eateth with me, shall betray mee.
text.DRV: 14:18 And when they were sitting at the table and eating, Jesus said: Amen I say to you, that one of you shall betray me, he that eateth with me.

text.NRSV: They began to be distressed and to say to him one after another, “Surely, not I?”
text.Rotherham: They began to be grieved, and to be saying unto him, one by one,—Can it be, I?
text.KJV: 14:19 And they began to be sorowfull, and to say vnto him, one by one, Is it I? And another said, Is it I?
text.DRV: 14:19 But they began to be sad, and to say to him severally, Is it I?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “It is one of the twelve, one who is dipping bread into the bowl with me.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—One of the twelve, he that is dipping with me into the [one] bowl:
text.KJV: 14:20 And he answered, and saide vnto them, It is one of the twelue, that dippeth with me in the dish.
text.DRV: 14:20 Who said to them: One of the Twelve, he that dippeth with me his hand in the dish.

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that one by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that one not to have been born.”
text.Rotherham: Because, the Son of Man, indeed, goeth his way,—according as it is written concerning him; but alas! for that man, through whom the Son of Man is being delivered up,—Well for him, if that man, had not been born!
text.KJV: 14:21 The sonne of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Sonne of man is betrayed: Good were it for that man, if he had neuer bene borne.
text.DRV: 14:21 And the Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of man shall be betrayed. It were good for him, if that man had not been born.

text.NRSV: While they were eating, he took a loaf of bread, and after blessing it he broke it, gave it to them, and said, “Take; this is my body.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were eating, taking a loaf, he blessed and brake, and gave unto them, and said—Take! this, is, my body;
text.KJV: 14:22 ¶ And as they did eate, Iesus tooke bread, and blessed, and brake it, and gaue to them, and said, Take, eate: this is my body.
text.DRV: 14:22 And whiles they were eating, Jesus took bread: and blessing brake, and gave to them, and said: Take, This is my Body.

text.NRSV: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he gave it to them, and all of them drank from it.
text.Rotherham: And, taking a cup, he offered thanks, and gave unto them; and they, all, drank of it.
text.KJV: 14:23 And he tooke the cup, and when he had giuen thanks, he gaue it to them: and they all dranke of it.
text.DRV: 14:23 And taking the chalice, giving thanks he gave to them. And they all drank of it.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—This, is, my blood of the covenant, that is to be poured out in behalf of many.
text.KJV: 14:24 And he said vnto them, This is my blood of the new Testament, which is shed for many.
text.DRV: 14:24 And he said to them: This is my Blood of The New Testament, That Shall be Shed For Many.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, I will never again drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—No more, will I in anywise drink of the fruit of the vine, until that day when I shall be drinking it new, in the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: 14:25 Uerely I say vnto you, I will drinke no more of the fruit of the Uine, vntill that day that I drinke it new in the kingdome of God.
text.DRV: 14:25 Amen I say to you, that now I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I shall drink it new in the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: When they had sung the hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
text.Rotherham: And, having sung praise, they went forth unto the Mount of Olives.
text.KJV: 14:26 ¶ And when they had sung an hymne, they went out into the mount of Oliues.
text.DRV: 14:26 And an hymn being said, they went forth into Mount-olivet.

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “You will all become deserters; for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto them—Ye, all, will find cause of stumbling, because it is written,—I will smite the shepherd, and, the sheep, will be scattered abroad;
text.KJV: 14:27 And Iesus saith vnto them, All ye shall be offended because of mee this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepheard, and the sheepe shall be scattered.
text.DRV: 14:27 And Jesus saith to them: You shall all be scandalized in me in this night; for it is written, I will strike the Pastor, & the sheep shall be dispersed.

text.NRSV: But after I am raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.”
text.Rotherham: But, after my arising, I will go before you into Galilee.
text.KJV: 14:28 But after that I am risen, I will goe before you into Galilee.
text.DRV: 14:28 But after that I shall be risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “Even though all become deserters, I will not.”
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, said unto him—Even if, all, shall find cause of stumbling, certainly not, I.
text.KJV: 14:29 But Peter said vnto him, Although al shalbe offended, yet wil not I.
text.DRV: 14:29 And Peter said to him: Although all shall be scandalized, yet not I.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Truly I tell you, this day, this very night, before the cock crows twice, you will deny me three times.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto him—Verily, I say unto thee, thou, this day, in this night, before that twice a cock crow, thrice, will deny me.
text.KJV: 14:30 And Iesus saith vnto him, Uerily I say vnto thee, that this day, euen in this night before the cocke crow twise, thou shalt denie me thrise.
text.DRV: 14:30 And Jesus saith to him: Amen I say to thee, that thou this day in this night, before the cock crow twice, shalt thrice deny me.

text.NRSV: But he said vehemently, “Even if I must die with you, I will not deny you.” And all of them said the same.
text.Rotherham: But he, most vehemently, was saying—Even though I must needs die with thee, in nowise, will I, deny thee. Likewise, indeed, were, all besides, saying.
text.KJV: 14:31 But he spake the more vehemently, If I should die with thee, I will not denie thee in any wise. Likewise also said they all.
text.DRV: 14:31 But he spake more vehemently: Although I should die together with thee, I will not deny thee. And in like manner also said they all.

text.NRSV: They went to a place called Gethsemane; and he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
text.Rotherham: And they come into an estate the name of which is Gethsemane, and he saith unto his disciples—Sit ye here, while I pray.
text.KJV: 14:32 And they came to a place which was named Gethsemani, and hee saith to his disciples, Sit yee here, while I shall pray.
text.DRV: 14:32 And they come into a farm-place called Gethsemani. And he saith to his Disciples: Sit you here, until I pray.

text.NRSV: He took with him Peter and James and John, and began to be distressed and agitated.
text.Rotherham: And he taketh Peter and James and John with him, and began to be exceedingly amazed, and in great distress;
text.KJV: 14:33 And hee taketh with him Peter, and Iames, and Iohn, and began to be sore amazed, and to be very heauy,
text.DRV: 14:33 And he taketh Peter and James and John with him; and he began to fear and to be heavy.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “I am deeply grieved, even to death; remain here, and keep awake.”
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them—Encompassed with grief is my soul, unto death: Abide ye here, and be watching.
text.KJV: 14:34 And saith vnto them, My soule is exceeding sorowful vnto death: tarie ye here, and watch.
text.DRV: 14:34 And he saith to them: My soul is sorrowful even unto death; stay here, and watch.

text.NRSV: And going a little farther, he threw himself on the ground and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
text.Rotherham: And, going forward a little, he fell upon the ground, and was praying that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him;
text.KJV: 14:35 And he went forward a litle, and fell on the ground, and prayed, that if it were possible, the houre might passe from him.
text.DRV: 14:35 And when he was gone forward a little, he fell flat upon the ground; and he prayed that if it might be, the hour might pass from him;

text.NRSV: He said, “Abba, Father, for you all things are possible; remove this cup from me; yet, not what I want, but what you want.”
text.Rotherham: and was saying—Abba! O Father! All things, are possible to thee: Bear aside this cup from me; but not what, I, will, but what, thou, [wilt] .
text.KJV: 14:36 And he said, Abba, father, all things are possible vnto thee, take away this cup from me: Neuerthelesse, not that I will, but what thou wilt.
text.DRV: 14:36 and he said: Abba, Father, all things are possible to thee, transfer this chalice from me, but not that which I will, but that which thou.

text.NRSV: He came and found them sleeping; and he said to Peter, “Simon, are you asleep? Could you not keep awake one hour?
text.Rotherham: And he cometh and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter—Simon! art thou sleeping? Hadst thou not strength, one hour, to watch?
text.KJV: 14:37 And hee commeth, and findeth them sleeping, and saith vnto Peter, Simon, sleepest thou? Couldest not thou watch one houre?
text.DRV: 14:37 And he cometh, and findeth them sleeping. And he saith to Peter: Simon, sleepest thou? couldst thou not watch one hour?

text.NRSV: Keep awake and pray that you may not come into the time of trial; the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
text.Rotherham: Be watching and praying, that ye enter not into temptation: the, spirit, indeed, is willing, but, the flesh, weak.
text.KJV: 14:38 Watch ye and pray, lest yee enter into temptation: The spirit truly is ready, but the flesh is weake.
text.DRV: 14:38 Watch ye, and pray that you enter not into tentation. The spirit indeed is prompt, but the flesh infirm.

text.NRSV: And again he went away and prayed, saying the same words.
text.Rotherham: And, again, departing, he prayed, [the same thing, saying].
text.KJV: 14:39 And againe he went away, and prayed, and spake the same words.
text.DRV: 14:39 And going away again, he prayed, saying the self same word.

text.NRSV: And once more he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy; and they did not know what to say to him.
text.Rotherham: And, again, coming, he found them sleeping, for their eyes were being, weighed down,—and they knew not what to answer him.
text.KJV: 14:40 And when he returned, he found them asleepe againe, (for their eies were heauie) neither wist they what to answere him.
text.DRV: 14:40 And returning, again he found them asleep (for their eyes were heavy) and they wist not what they should answer him.

text.NRSV: He came a third time and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? Enough! The hour has come; the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
text.Rotherham: And he cometh the third time, and saith unto them—Ye are sleeping the remaining time and taking your rest:—It is enough! the hour hath come! Lo! the Son of Man is being delivered up into the hands of sinners.
text.KJV: 14:41 And he commeth the third time, and saith vnto them, Sleepe on now, and take your rest: it is enough, the houre is come, behold, the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
text.DRV: 14:41 And he cometh the third time, and saith to them: Sleep ye now, and take rest. It sufficeth: the hour is come; behold the Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of sinners.

text.NRSV: Get up, let us be going. See, my betrayer is at hand.”
text.Rotherham: Be rousing yourselves, let us be leading on! Lo! he that is delivering me up, hath drawn near.
text.KJV: 14:42 Rise vp, let vs goe, Loe, he that betrayeth me, is at hand.
text.DRV: 14:42 Arise, let us go. Behold, he that shall betray me, is at hand.

text.NRSV: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, arrived; and with him there was a crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, while yet he is speaking, Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, cometh near, and, with him, a multitude, with swords and clubs, from the High-priests and the Scribes and the Elders.
text.KJV: 14:43 ¶ And immediately, while hee yet spake, commeth Iudas, one of the twelue, and with him a great multitude with swords, and staues, from the chiefe Priests, and the Scribes, & the Elders.
text.DRV: 14:43 And as he was yet speaking, cometh Judas Iscariote one of the Twelve, and with him a great multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief Priests and the Scribes and the Ancients.

text.NRSV: Now the betrayer had given them a sign, saying, “The one I kiss is the man; arrest him and lead him away under guard.”
text.Rotherham: He that is delivering him up hath given an agreed sign unto them, saying—Whomsoever I shall kiss, he, it is! Secure him, and be leading away, safely.
text.KJV: 14:44 And he that betrayed him, had giuen them a token, saying, Whomsoeuer I shall kisse, that same is he; take him, and lead him away safely.
text.DRV: 14:44 And the betrayer of him had given them a sign, saying: Whomsoever I shall kiss, it is he, lay hold on him, and lead him warily.

text.NRSV: So when he came, he went up to him at once and said, “Rabbi!” and kissed him.
text.Rotherham: And, coming, straightway, stepping forward to him, he saith—Rabbi! and openly kissed him.
text.KJV: 14:45 And assoone as he was come, he goeth straightway to him, and sayeth, Master, Master, and kissed him.
text.DRV: 14:45 And when he was come, immediately going to him, he saith: Rabbi, and he kissed him.

text.NRSV: Then they laid hands on him and arrested him.
text.Rotherham: And, they, thrust their hands on him, and secured him.
text.KJV: 14:46 ¶ And they layed their hands on him, and tooke him.
text.DRV: 14:46 But they laid hands upon him, and held him.

text.NRSV: But one of those who stood by drew his sword and struck the slave of the high priest, cutting off his ear.
text.Rotherham: [A certain] one of the by-standers, drawing a sword, smote the servant of the High priest, and took off his, ear.
text.KJV: 14:47 And one of them that stood by, drew a sword, and smote a seruant of the high Priest, and cut off his eare.
text.DRV: 14:47 And one certain man of the standers about, drawing out a sword, smote the servant of the chief Priest, and cut off his ear.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest me as though I were a robber?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, answering, said unto them—As against a robber, came ye forth, with swords and clubs, to arrest me?
text.KJV: 14:48 And Iesus answered, & said vnto thē, Are ye come out as against a theefe, with swords, & with staues to take me?
text.DRV: 14:48 And Jesus answering, said to them: As to a thief you come out with swords and clubs to apprehend me?

text.NRSV: Day after day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.”
text.Rotherham: Daily, was I with you, in the temple, teaching; and ye secured me not. But, that, the Scriptures, may be fulfilled…
text.KJV: 14:49 I was daily with you in the Temple, teaching, and yee tooke me not; but the Scriptures must be fulfilled.
text.DRV: 14:49 I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you did not lay hands on me. But, that the Scriptures may be fulfilled.

text.NRSV: All of them deserted him and fled.
text.Rotherham: And they, all, forsook, him, and fled.
text.KJV: 14:50 And they all forsooke him, & fled.
text.DRV: 14:50 Then his Disciples leaving him, all fled.

text.NRSV: A certain young man was following him, wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They caught hold of him,
text.Rotherham: And, a certain young man, was following with him, having cast about himself a fine Indian cloth, over his naked body,—and they are securing him;
text.KJV: 14:51 And there followed him a certaine yong man, hauing a linnen cloth cast about his naked body, and the yong men laid hold on him.
text.DRV: 14:51 And a certain young man followed him clothed with sindon upon the bare; and they took him.

text.NRSV: but he left the linen cloth and ran off naked.
text.Rotherham: but, he, leaving behind the cloth, fled naked.
text.KJV: 14:52 And he left the linnen cloth, and fled from them naked.
text.DRV: 14:52 But he casting off the sindon, fled from them naked.

text.NRSV: They took Jesus to the high priest; and all the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes were assembled.
text.Rotherham: And they led away Jesus unto the High-priest; and all the High-priests and the Elders and the Scribes gather together.
text.KJV: 14:53 ¶ And they led Iesus away to the high Priest, and with him were assembled all the chiefe Priests, and the Elders, and the Scribes.
text.DRV: 14:53 And they brought Jesus to the chief Priest; and all the Priests and the Scribes and the Ancients assembled together.

text.NRSV: Peter had followed him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest; and he was sitting with the guards, warming himself at the fire.
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, afar of, followed him, as far as within, into the court of the High-priest; and was sitting with the attendants, and warming himself by the light.
text.KJV: 14:54 And Peter followed him a farre off, euen into the pallace of the high Priest: and he sate with the seruants, and warmed himselfe at the fire.
text.DRV: 14:54 And Peter followed him a far off even in unto the court of the high Priest; and he sat with the servants at the fire, and warmed himself.

text.NRSV: Now the chief priests and the whole council were looking for testimony against Jesus to put him to death; but they found none.
text.Rotherham: Now, the High-priest, and all the High-council, were seeking, against Jesus, testimony, with the intent to put him to death,—and were not finding any;
text.KJV: 14:55 And the chiefe Priests, and all the counsell sought for witnesse against Iesus, to put him to death, & found none.
text.DRV: 14:55 And the chief Priests and all the Council sought testimony against Jesus, that they might put him to death, neither found they.

text.NRSV: For many gave false testimony against him, and their testimony did not agree.
text.Rotherham: for, many, were bearing false-witness against him, and the testimonies were not, agreed.
text.KJV: 14:56 For many bare false witnesse against him, but their witnesse agreed not together.
text.DRV: 14:56 For many spake false witness against him; and the testimonies were not convenient.

text.NRSV: Some stood up and gave false testimony against him, saying,
text.Rotherham: And some, standing up, were bearing false-witness against him, saying—
text.KJV: 14:57 And there arose certaine, and bare false witnesse against him, saying,
text.DRV: 14:57 And certain rising up, bare false witness against him, saying:

text.NRSV: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another, not made with hands.’”
text.Rotherham: We, heard him saying, I, will pull down this shrine, the one made by hand, and, in three days, another, not made by hand, will I raise.
text.KJV: 14:58 We heard him say, I will destroy this Temple that is made with hands, and within three dayes I will build another made without hands.
text.DRV: 14:58 That we heard him say, I will dissolve this temple made with hand, and in three days will I build another not made with hand.

text.NRSV: But even on this point their testimony did not agree.
text.Rotherham: And, not even so, was the testimony, agreed.
text.KJV: 14:59 But neither so did their witnesse agree together.
text.DRV: 14:59 And their testimony was not convenient.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest stood up before them and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer? What is it that they testify against you?”
text.Rotherham: And, the High-priest rising up into the midst, questioned Jesus, saying—Answerest thou, nothing? What are these, against thee, witnessing?
text.KJV: 14:60 And the high Priest stood vp in the mids, and asked Iesus, saying, Answerest thou nothing? What is it which these witnesse against thee?
text.DRV: 14:60 And the high Priest rising up into the midst, asked Jesus, saying: Answerest thou nothing to these things that are objected to thee of these?

text.NRSV: But he was silent and did not answer. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, was silent, and answered, nothing. Again, the High-priest was questioning him, and saith unto him—Art, thou, the Christ the Son of the Blessed?
text.KJV: 14:61 But he held his peace, and answered nothing. Againe, the high Priest asked him, and said vnto him, Art thou the Christ, the sonne of the Blessed?
text.DRV: 14:61 But he held his peace and answered nothing. Again the high Priest asked him, and said to him: Art thou Christ the Son of the blessed God?

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “I am; and ‘you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Power,’ and ‘coming with the clouds of heaven.’”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said—I, am; and ye shall see the Son of Man—sitting, on the right hand, of Power, and coming with the clouds of heaven.
text.KJV: 14:62 And Iesus said, I am: and yee shall see the sonne of man sitting on the right hand of power, and comming in the clouds of heauen.
text.DRV: 14:62 And Jesus saith to him: I am. And you shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming with the clouds of Heaven.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, “Why do we still need witnesses?
text.Rotherham: And, the High-priest, having rent asunder his inner-garments, saith—What, further, need have we, of witnesses?
text.KJV: 14:63 Then the high Priest rent his clothes, and saith, What neede we any further witnesses?
text.DRV: 14:63 And the high Priest renting his garments, saith: What need we witnesses any further?

text.NRSV: You have heard his blasphemy. What is your decision?” All of them condemned him as deserving death.
text.Rotherham: Heard ye the profanity? What, to you, doth it appear? And they, all, condemned him to be, worthy, of, death.
text.KJV: 14:64 Yee haue heard the blasphemy: what thinke yee? And they all condemned him to be guilty of death.
text.DRV: 14:64 You have heard blasphemy. How think you? Who all condemned him to be guilty of death.

text.NRSV: Some began to spit on him, and to blindfold him, and to strike him, saying to him, “Prophesy!” The guards also took him over and slapped him.
text.Rotherham: And some began to be spitting at him, and, covering up his face, and to be buffeting him, and saying to him—Prophesy! And, the attendants, with smart blows, took him.
text.KJV: 14:65 And some beganne to spit on him, and to couer his face, and to buffet him, and to say vnto him, Prophecie: And the seruants did stricke him with the palmes of their hands.
text.DRV: 14:65 And certain began to spit upon him, and to cover his face, and to beat him with buffets, and to say unto him, Prophesy; and the servants gave him blows.

text.NRSV: While Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant-girls of the high priest came by.
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, being, below in the court, there cometh one of the maid-servants of the High-priest;
text.KJV: 14:66 ¶ And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there commeth one of the maides of the high Priest.
text.DRV: 14:66 And when Peter was in the court beneath, there cometh one of the woman-servants of the high Priest.

text.NRSV: When she saw Peter warming himself, she stared at him and said, “You also were with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.”
text.Rotherham: and, seeing Peter warming himself, having looked at him, saith—And, thou, wast, with the Nazarene—with Jesus.
text.KJV: 14:67 And when she saw Peter warning himselfe, she looked vpon him, and said, And thou also wast with Iesus of Nazareth.
text.DRV: 14:67 And when she had seen Peter warming himself, beholding him she saith: And thou wast with Jesus of Nazareth.

text.NRSV: But he denied it, saying, “I do not know or understand what you are talking about.” And he went out into the forecourt. Then the cock crowed.
text.Rotherham: But, he, denied, saying—I neither know [him], nor do I well understand what, thou, sayest; and went out into the porch.
text.KJV: 14:68 But hee denied, saying, I know not, neither vnderstand I what thou sayest. And he went out into the porch, and the cocke crew.
text.DRV: 14:68 But he denied, saying: Neither know I, neither wot I what thou sayest. And he went before the court; and the cock crew.

text.NRSV: And the servant-girl, on seeing him, began again to say to the bystanders, “This man is one of them.”
text.Rotherham: And, the maid-servant, seeing him, began, again, to be saying unto the by-standers—This one, is, from among them.
text.KJV: 14:69 And a maide saw him againe, and beganne to say to them that stood by, This is one of them.
text.DRV: 14:69 And again a wench seeing him, began to say to the standers about, That this fellow is of them.

text.NRSV: But again he denied it. Then after a little while the bystanders again said to Peter, “Certainly you are one of them; for you are a Galilean.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, again, was denying. And, after a little again, the by-standers, were saying unto Peter—Truly, thou, art, from among them; for thou art, a Galilaean, also.
text.KJV: 14:70 And he denied it againe. And a little after, they that stood by said againe to Peter, Surely thou art one of them: for thou art a Galilean, and thy speach agreeth thereto.
text.DRV: 14:70 But he denied again. And after a while again they that stood by, said to Peter: Verily thou art of them; for thou art also a Galilæan.

text.NRSV: But he began to curse, and he swore an oath, “I do not know this man you are talking about.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, began to be cursing and swearing—I know not this man, of whom ye are speaking!
text.KJV: 14:71 But he beganne to curse and to sweare, saying, I know not this man of whom yee speake.
text.DRV: 14:71 But he began to curse and to swear, That I know not this man whom you speak of.

text.NRSV: At that moment the cock crowed for the second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had said to him, “Before the cock crows twice, you will deny me three times.” And he broke down and wept.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, a second time, a cock, crowed; and Peter was reminded of the declaration, how Jesus had said to him—Before a cock, twice, crow, thrice, wilt thou deny me. And, when he thought thereon, he began to weep.
text.KJV: 14:72 And the second time the cocke crew: and Peter called to minde the word that Iesus said vnto him, Before the cockecrow twise, thou shalt denie me thrise. And when he thought thereon, he wept.
text.DRV: 14:72 And immediately the cock crew again. And Peter remembered the word that Jesus had said unto him: Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt thrice deny me. And he began to weep.

text.Base: As soon as it was morning, the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council. They bound Jesus, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate.
text.KJV: 15:1 And straightway in the morning the chiefe Priests helde a consultation with the Elders and Scribes, and the whole Councell, and bound Iesus, and caried him away, and deliuered him to Pilate.
text.DRV: 15:1 And forthwith in the morning the chief Priests with the Ancients and the Scribes & the whole Council, consulting together, binding Jesus led & delivered him to Pilate.

text.Base: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered him, “You say so.”
text.KJV: 15:2 And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Iewes? And hee answering, said vnto him, Thou sayest it.
text.DRV: 15:2 And Pilate asked him: Art thou the King of the Jews? but he answering, said to him: Thou sayest,

text.Base: Then the chief priests accused him of many things.
text.KJV: 15:3 And the chiefe Priests accused him of many things: but hee answered nothing.
text.DRV: 15:3 And the chief Priests accused him in many things.

text.Base: Pilate asked him again, “Have you no answer? See how many charges they bring against you.”
text.KJV: 15:4 And Pilate asked him againe, saying, Answerest thou nothing? behold how many things they witnesse against thee.
text.DRV: 15:4 And Pilate again asked him, saying: Answerest thou nothing? See in how many things they accuse thee.

text.Base: But Jesus made no further answer, so that Pilate was amazed.
text.KJV: 15:5 But Iesus yet answered nothing, so that Pilate marueiled.
text.DRV: 15:5 But Jesus answered nothing more; so that Pilate marveled.

text.Base: Now at the festival he used to release a prisoner for them, anyone for whom they asked.
text.KJV: 15:6 Now at that Feast he released vnto them one prisoner, whomsoeuer they desired.
text.DRV: 15:6 And upon the festival day he was wont to release unto them one of the prisoners whomsoever they had demanded.

text.Base: Now a man called Barabbas was in prison with the rebels who had committed murder during the insurrection.
text.KJV: 15:7 And there was one named Barabbas, which lay bound with them that had made insurrection with him, who had committed murder in the insurrection.
text.DRV: 15:7 And there was one called Barabbas, which was put in prison with seditious persons, who in a sedition had committed murder.

text.Base: So the crowd came and began to ask Pilate to do for them according to his custom.
text.KJV: 15:8 And the multitude crying alowd, began to desire him to doe as he had euer done vnto them.
text.DRV: 15:8 And when the multitude was come up, they began to require, according as always he did unto them.

text.Base: Then he answered them, “Do you want me to release for you the King of the Jews?”
text.KJV: 15:9 But Pilate answered them, saying, Will ye that I release vnto you the King of the Iewes?
text.DRV: 15:9 And Pilate answered them, and said: Will you that I release to you the King of the Jews?

text.Base: For he realized that it was out of jealousy that the chief priests had handed him over.
text.KJV: 15:10 (For hee knew that the chiefe Priests had deliuered him for enuie.)
text.DRV: 15:10 For he knew that the chief Priests for envy had delivered him.

text.Base: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas for them instead.
text.KJV: 15:11 But the chiefe Priests mooued the people, that hee should rather release Barabbas vnto them.
text.DRV: 15:11 But the chief Priests moved the people, that he should release Barabbas rather to them.

text.Base: Pilate spoke to them again, “Then what do you wish me to do with the man you call the King of the Jews?”
text.KJV: 15:12 And Pilate answered, and said againe vnto them, What will yee then that I shall do vnto him whom ye call the King of the Iewes?
text.DRV: 15:12 And Pilate again answering, said to them: What will you then that I do to the King of the Jews?

text.Base: They shouted back, “Crucify him!”
text.KJV: 15:13 And they cried out againe, Crucifie him.
text.DRV: 15:13 But they again cried: Crucify him.

text.Base: Pilate asked them, “Why, what evil has he done?” But they shouted all the more, “Crucify him!”
text.KJV: 15:14 Then Pilate saide vnto them, Why, what euill hath hee done? And they cried out the more exceedingly, Crucifie him.
text.DRV: 15:14 And Pilate saith to them: Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried the more: Crucify him.

text.Base: So Pilate, wishing to satisfy the crowd, released Barabbas for them; and after flogging Jesus, he handed him over to be crucified.
text.KJV: 15:15 ¶ And so Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas vnto them, and deliuered Iesus, when he had scourged him, to be crucified.
text.DRV: 15:15 And Pilate willing to satisfy the people, released to them Barabbas, and delivered Jesus, having whipped him, for to be crucified.

text.Base: Then the soldiers led him into the courtyard of the palace (that is, the governor’s headquarters); and they called together the whole cohort.
text.KJV: 15:16 And the souldiers led him away into the hall, called Pretorium, and they call together the whole band.
text.DRV: 15:16 And the soldiers led him into the court of the Palace, and they call together the whole band:

text.Base: And they clothed him in a purple cloak; and after twisting some thorns into a crown, they put it on him.
text.KJV: 15:17 And they clothed him with purple, and platted a crowne of thornes, and put it about his head,
text.DRV: 15:17 and they clothe him in purple, and platting a crown of thorns, they put it upon him.

text.Base: And they began saluting him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
text.KJV: 15:18 And beganne to salute him, Haile King of the Iewes.
text.DRV: 15:18 And they began to salute him: Hail King of the Jews.

text.Base: They struck his head with a reed, spat upon him, and knelt down in homage to him.
text.KJV: 15:19 And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit vpon him, and bowing their knees, worshipped him.
text.DRV: 15:19 And they smote his head with a reed: and they did spit on him. And bowing the knees, they adored him.

text.Base: After mocking him, they stripped him of the purple cloak and put his own clothes on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
text.KJV: 15:20 And when they had mocked him, they tooke off the purple from him, and put his owne clothes on him, and led him out to crucifie him.
text.DRV: 15:20 And after they had mocked him, they stripped him of the purple, and put on him his own garments, and they lead him forth to crucify him.

text.Base: They compelled a passer-by, who was coming in from the country, to carry his cross; it was Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus.
text.KJV: 15:21 And they compell one Simon a Cyrenian, who passed by, comming out of the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to beare his Crosse.
text.DRV: 15:21 And they forced a certain man that passed by, Simon a Cyrenean coming from the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to take up his cross.

text.Base: Then they brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha (which means the place of a skull).
text.KJV: 15:22 And they bring him vnto the place Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, the place of a skull.
text.DRV: 15:22 And they bring him into the place Golgotha, which being interpreted is, The place of Calvary.

text.Base: And they offered him wine mixed with myrrh; but he did not take it.
text.KJV: 15:23 And they gaue him to drinke, wine mingled with myrrhe: but he receiued it not.
text.DRV: 15:23 And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh; and he took it not.

text.Base: And they crucified him, and divided his clothes among them, casting lots to decide what each should take.
text.KJV: 15:24 And when they had crucified him, they parted his garments, casting lots vpon them, what euery man should take.
text.DRV: 15:24 And crucifying him, they divided his garments, casting lots upon them, who should take which.

text.Base: It was nine o’clock in the morning when they crucified him.
text.KJV: 15:25 And it was the third houre, and they crucified him.
text.DRV: 15:25 And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.

text.Base: The inscription of the charge against him read, “The King of the Jews.”
text.KJV: 15:26 And the superscription of his accusation was written ouer, THE KING OF THE IEWES.
text.DRV: 15:26 And the title of his cause was superscribed, King of The Jews.

text.Base: And with him they crucified two bandits, one on his right and one on his left.
text.KJV: 15:27 And with him they crucifie two theeues, the one on his right hand, and the other on his left.
text.DRV: 15:27 And with him they crucify two thieves; one on the right hand, and another on his left.

text.Base: Those who passed by derided him, shaking their heads and saying, “Aha! You who would destroy the temple and build it in three days,
text.KJV: 15:29 And they that passed by, railed on him, wagging their heads, and saying, Ah thou that destroyest the Temple, and buildest it in three dayes,
text.DRV: 15:29 And they that passed by, blasphemed him, wagging their heads, and saying: Vah, he that destroyeth the Temple, and in three days buildeth it:

text.Base: save yourself, and come down from the cross!”
text.KJV: 15:30 Saue thy selfe, and come downe from the Crosse.
text.DRV: 15:30 save thyself, coming down from the cross.

text.Base: In the same way the chief priests, along with the scribes, were also mocking him among themselves and saying, “He saved others; he cannot save himself.
text.KJV: 15:31 Likewise also the chiefe Priests mocking, said among themselues with the Scribes, He saued others, himselfe he cannot saue.
text.DRV: 15:31 In like manner also the chief Priests mocking said with the Scribes one to another: He saved others, himself he cannot save.

text.Base: Let the Messiah, the King of Israel, come down from the cross now, so that we may see and believe.” Those who were crucified with him also taunted him.
text.KJV: 15:32 Let Christ the King of Israel descend now from the Crosse, that we may see and beleeue: And they that were crucified with him, reuiled him.
text.DRV: 15:32 Let Christ the King of Israel come down now from the cross that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him, railed at him.

text.Base: When it was noon, darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon.
text.KJV: 15:33 And when the sixth houre was come, there was darkenesse ouer the whole land, vntill the ninth houre.
text.DRV: 15:33 And when it was the sixth hour, there was made darkness upon the whole earth until the ninth hour.

text.Base: At three o’clock Jesus cried out with a loud voice, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
text.KJV: 15:34 And at the ninth houre, Iesus cryed with a loude voice, saying, Eloi, Eloi, lamasabachthani? which is, being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
text.DRV: 15:34 And at the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a mighty voice, saying: Eloi, Eloi, lamma-sabacthani? Which is being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

text.Base: When some of the bystanders heard it, they said, “Listen, he is calling for Elijah.”
text.KJV: 15:35 And some of them that stood by, when they heard it, said, Behold, he calleth Elias.
text.DRV: 15:35 And certain of the standers about hearing, said: Behold, he calleth Elias.

text.Base: And someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink, saying, “Wait, let us see whether Elijah will come to take him down.”
text.KJV: 15:36 And one ranne, and filled a spunge full of vineger, and put it on a reed, and gaue him to drinke, saying, Let alone, let vs see whether Elias will come to take him downe.
text.DRV: 15:36 And one running and filling a spunge with vinegar, and putting it about a reed, gave him drink, saying: Let be, let us see if Elias come to take him down.

text.Base: Then Jesus gave a loud cry and breathed his last.
text.KJV: 15:37 And Iesus cryed with a loude voice, and gaue vp the ghost.
text.DRV: 15:37 And Jesus putting forth a mighty voice, gave up the ghost.

text.Base: And the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom.
text.KJV: 15:38 And the vaile of the Temple was rent in twaine, from the top to the bottome.
text.DRV: 15:38 And the veil of the temple was rent in two, from the top to the bottom.

text.Base: Now when the centurion, who stood facing him, saw that in this way he breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was God’s Son!”
text.KJV: 15:39 ¶ And when the Centurion which stood ouer against him, saw that hee so cryed out, and gaue vp the ghost, hee said, Truely this man was the Sonne of God.
text.DRV: 15:39 And the Centurion that stood over against him, seeing that so crying he had given up the ghost, said: Indeed this man was the Son of God.

text.Base: There were also women looking on from a distance; among them were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.
text.KJV: 15:40 There were also women looking on afarre off, among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of Iames the lesse, and of Ioses, and Salome:
text.DRV: 15:40 And there were also women looking on a far off: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less, and of Joseph, and Salome;

text.Base: These used to follow him and provided for him when he was in Galilee; and there were many other women who had come up with him to Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 15:41 Who also when hee was in Galile, followed him, and ministred vnto him, and many other women which came vp with him vnto Hierusalem.
text.DRV: 15:41 and when he was in Galilee, they followed him, and ministered to him, and many other women that came up together with him to Jerusalem.

text.Base: When evening had come, and since it was the day of Preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,
text.KJV: 15:42 ¶ And now when the euen was come, (because it was the Preparation, that is, the day before the Sabbath)
text.DRV: 15:42 And when evening was now come (because it was the Parasceve, which is the Sabboth-eve)

text.Base: Joseph of Arimathea, a respected member of the council, who was also himself waiting expectantly for the kingdom of God, went boldly to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
text.KJV: 15:43 Ioseph of Arimathea, an honourable counseller, which also waited for the kingdome of God, came, and went in boldly vnto Pilate, and craued the body of Iesus.
text.DRV: 15:43 came Joseph of Arimathæa a noble Senator, who himself also was expecting the Kingdom of God; and he went in boldly to Pilate, and asked the body of Jesus.

text.Base: Then Pilate wondered if he were already dead; and summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he had been dead for some time.
text.KJV: 15:44 And Pilate marueiled if he were already dead, and calling vnto him the Centurion, hee asked him whether hee had beene any while dead.
text.DRV: 15:44 But Pilate marveled if he were now dead. And sending for the Centurion, asked him if he were now dead.

text.Base: When he learned from the centurion that he was dead, he granted the body to Joseph.
text.KJV: 15:45 And when he knew it of the Centurion, he gaue the body to Ioseph.
text.DRV: 15:45 And when he understood by the Centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.

text.Base: Then Joseph bought a linen cloth, and taking down the body, wrapped it in the linen cloth, and laid it in a tomb that had been hewn out of the rock. He then rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.
text.KJV: 15:46 And hee bought fine linnen, and tooke him downe, and wrapped him in the linnen, and laide him in a sepulchre, which was hewen out of a rocke, and rolled a stone vnto the doore of the sepulchre.
text.DRV: 15:46 And Joseph buying sindon, and taking him down, wrapped him in the sindon, and laid him in a monument, that was hewed out of a rock. And he rolled a stone to the door of the monument.

text.Base: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where the body was laid.
text.KJV: 15:47 And Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of Ioses behelde where he was laide.
text.DRV: 15:47 And Mary Magdalene and Mary of Joseph beheld where he was laid.

text.NRSV: When the sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they might go and anoint him.
text.Rotherham: And, the Sabbath having passed, Mary the Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, that, coming, they might anoint him.
text.KJV: 16:1 And when the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of Iames, and Salome, had bought sweete spices, that they might come and anoint him.
text.DRV: 16:1 And when the Sabboth was past, Mary Magdalene and Mary of James, and Salome bought spices, that coming they might anoint Jesus.

text.NRSV: And very early on the first day of the week, when the sun had risen, they went to the tomb.
text.Rotherham: And, very early, on the first of the week, they are coming towards the tomb—when, the sun, arose.
text.KJV: 16:2 And very early in the morning, the first day of the week they came vnto the sepulchre, at the rising of the sunne:
text.DRV: 16:2 And very early the first of the Sabboths, they come to the monument, the sun being now risen.

text.NRSV: They were saying to one another, “Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance to the tomb?”
text.Rotherham: And they were saying one to another—Who, shall roll away for us the stone, out of the door of the tomb?
text.KJV: 16:3 And they said among themselues, who shall roll vs away the stone from the doore of the sepulchre?
text.DRV: 16:3 And they said one to another: Who shall roll us back the stone from the door of the monument?

text.NRSV: When they looked up, they saw that the stone, which was very large, had already been rolled back.
